item.title: 让我们谈谈设计模式... | Season 9 of DevDiscuss is here! Let's talk about design patterns...
item.description:
开发团队的Ben Halpern

发表于在首映式中，我的共同主持人@ridhwana和我专注于使用设计模式来改进您与@lydiahallie和@addyosmani一起构建 Web 应用程序的方式——这本书是新书Learning Patterns的合著者。

我们希望您喜欢收听 DevDiscuss 的第 9 季首播：这个节目我们解决了影响我们作为开发人员生活的紧迫问题。

在您最喜欢的播客平台上收听的链接：

  • 听笔记
  • 调入
  • 本杰明·施罗斯 - Apr 25 4月25日



原文:

Ben Halpern for The DEV Team

Posted on May 11

DevDiscuss Season 9 is here! 🎉

In the premiere, my cohost @ridhwana and I focused on using design patterns to improve how you architect web apps with @lydiahallie & @addyosmani — co-authors of the new book Learning Patterns.

We hope you enjoy listening to the season 9 premiere of DevDiscuss: the show where we tackle the burning questions that impact all our lives as developers.

item.title: Flutter 发布 3.0.0 有什么新功能？ | Flutter release 3.0.0 What's new ?
item.description:
斯坦尼斯拉夫·伊林

发表于这次大会的消息之一是发布了 Flutter 3.0.0 版本，这次谷歌团队做了哪些准备？ 让我们弄清楚吧！

稳定支持 MacOS 和 Linux

现在，flutter 已准备好在所有桌面平台上进行生产，Linux 和 macOS 已达到稳定并包含以下功能：

  • 级联菜单和对 macOS 系统菜单栏的支持
  • 全面支持所有桌面平台上的国际文本输入
  • 所有桌面平台的可访问性
  • 弃用 Windows 7/8 进行开发

新的 Google Pixel 手机使用了它，现在 Flutter 应用程序也支持它。

在这里你可以查看 Flutter 团队关于如何在你的 Flutter 应用程序中实现新的 Material 3 api 的文章

可折叠手机支持

什么手机？ 是那些能够展开的手机

如果您一直在关注 Flutter 社区的发布，您可能已经注意到这个存储库flutter/pinball

是的，这个惊人的游戏是在 Flutter 上使用火焰制作的
令我惊讶的是，Flutter 团队在这次更新中非常关注游戏的制作

老实说，这很棒。 这个动作展示了 Flutter 是一个可以制作休闲游戏的强大框架的另一个例子。

而在本次更新中，Flutter Team 还发布了休闲游戏工具包

它旨在让您更轻松地创建简单游戏并使用 Google 服务支持它们。

网站说我们可以

通过对广告、应用内购买、Firebase、Play 服务和游戏中心等服务的预构建集成加快游戏开发

我想知道这会发生什么，以及 Flutter 上的游戏故事将如何发展。 我什至想在 Flutter 上写一个游戏，现在就给自己发火😁

那么，如果没有 linter 更新，flutter 更新怎么会发生呢？ 是的，对于 Flutter 上的新应用程序， flutter_lints版本将自动为 2.0.0。

现在 Flutter in web 可以在支持它的浏览器中自动使用 ImageDecoder API。

更多在这里

结论

我很高兴添加了许多新功能，这些功能有助于我最喜欢的框架的扩展和普及

如果我没有提到更新中的任何内容，请写在评论中
感谢您的阅读并继续关注！😘



原文:

Stanislav Ilin

Posted on May 12

Google I/O is happening now. One of the news from this conference is release a Flutter version 3.0.0 What has the google team prepared this time? Let's figure it out!

Stable support for MacOS and Linux

Now flutter is ready for production on all desktop platforms Linux and macOS have reached stable and include this features:

  • Cascading menus and support for the macOS system menu bar
  • Full support for international text input on all desktop platforms
  • Accessibility on all desktop platforms
  • Universal binaries by default on macOS
  • Deprecating Windows 7/8 for development

Material Design 3(Material You)

Material You is new design language update. New Google Pixel phones used it and now Flutter applications is support it too.

Material You included features like dynamic color, an updated color system and typography, updates to many components, and new visual effects introduced in Android 12 like a new touch ripple design and a stretch overscroll effect.

Here you can check article from the Flutter Team about how to implement new Material 3 api in your Flutter application

Foldable phone support

Another feature in new Flutter release is a stable support for foldable phones. What phones? It is those phones that are able to unfold

MediaQuery now contains a list of DisplayFeatures, describing the bounds and states of device elements like hinges, folds, and cutouts.

Flutter Casual Games Toolkit

If you have been following the releases of the Flutter community, you may have noticed this repository flutter/pinball

Yes, this amazing game was made on Flutter using flame
I was surprised that the Flutter team paid a lot of attention to the production of games in this update

Honestly, it's wonderful. This action shows another example of the fact that Flutter is a powerful framework on which you can make casual games.

And in this update, Flutter Team also released the Casual Games Toolkit

It is designed to make it easier to create simple games and support them using Google services.

The website says that we can

Speed up your game's development with pre-built integrations for services like Ads, In-App Purchases, Firebase, Play Services, and Game Center

I wonder what will come of this and how the story of the games on Flutter will develop. I even wanted to write a game on Flutter and flame myself now 😁

flutter_lints updated to 2.0.0

Well, how could a flutter update happen without a linter updates? Yes, for new applications on Flutter, the flutter_lints version will be 2.0.0 automatically.

Image decoding and app lifecycles for Web

Now Flutter in web can automatically uses the ImageDecoder API in browsers that support it.

The new lifecycle API for Flutter web apps gives you the flexibility to control the bootstrap process of your Flutter app from the hosting HTML page. More here

Conclusion

The Flutter Team amazes with each of its new updates
I am glad that many new features are being added that contribute to the expansion and popularization of my favorite framework

Write in the comments if I didn't mention anything from the update
Thank you for reading and stay tuned!😘
item.author: frezyx

item.title: 通用忍者平台新闻：付款 | 新的电子商务 API | Common Ninja Platform News: Payments | New E-Commerce APIs
item.description:
我们很高兴与您分享与我们的开发者平台相关的最新消息和更新。 我们计划每月发布一次这些更新，敬请期待！

提醒您，Common Ninja 开发者平台让您可以非常轻松地为电子商务平台构建应用程序并从中获利。 使用我们的单一电子商务 API，您可以一次构建您的应用程序，并将其与多个平台集成，例如Shopify 、 BigCommerce 、 Wix 、 WooCommerce等。 无需重新构建应用程序并使其在每个平台上单独运行。

付款

  • 产品
  • 订单
  • 顾客

    • 付款

    我们很自豪地展示全新的支付功能——包括一个简单的界面，允许您为您的应用创建支付计划，将它们连接到流行的支付提供商，如 Stripe、PayPal 和 Shopify 应用支付 API，并动态创建结帐 URL您应用的用户🚀

    在文档中详细了解它的工作原理。

    我们添加了 3 个新的电子商务 API：

    • 订单- 使用新的订单 API，您可以创建、更新和查看商店的订单。
    • 客户- 一个使用商店客户列表的简单 API。

    您可以在此处了解有关所有受支持 API 的更多信息。

    更多精彩更新即将发布，敬请期待！



    原文:

    We are very excited to share with you recent news and updates related to our developer platform. We are planning on posting these updates once a month, so stay tuned!

    Just to remind you, the Common Ninja Developer Platform allows you to build & monetize apps for e-commerce platforms very easily. With our single e-commerce API you can build your app once, and integrate it with multiple platforms like Shopify, BigCommerce, Wix, WooCommerce, and more. There's no need to rebuild the app and make it work on every platform individually.

    🎉 What’s New:

    • Payments
    • API updates
      • Products
      • Orders
      • Customers

    Payments

    We are proud to present the brand new payments capabilities — that includes a simple interface that allows you to create payment plans for your app, connect them to popular payment providers like Stripe, PayPal, and Shopify app payment APIs, and dynamically create checkout URLs for your app’s users 🚀

    Learn more about how it works in the docs.

    API Updates

    We’ve added 3 new e-commerce APIs:

    • Products - a new set of API endpoints for managing the store’s products resource.
    • Orders - with the new orders API you can create, update, and see store’s orders.
    • Customers - a simple API to consume a list of store’s customers.

    You may learn more about all of the supported APIs here.

    More exciting updates will be released soon so stay tuned!
item.author: dsternlicht

item.title: AWS CDK 凭证的快捷方式：SSO、SAML 和命名配置文件的简单设置 | Shortcut for AWS CDK credentials: insanely simple setup for SSO, SAML, and named profiles
item.description:
我们都爱CDK！ 我们不是吗？ 自推出以来，我们终于有了一个 Typescript 工具，可以在 AWS 上精确地编写 IaC，并针对无服务器应用程序进行结构化。

但是有一个关键点并不像它应该的那样敏捷：本地凭证管理。

在不编写复杂脚本和广泛使用环境变量的情况下，没有简单的方法可以在不同的环境中重用相同的模板。

另一点是，您通常大部分时间都必须使用长期凭证，这可能是一个安全威胁。

最后，根据文档， CDK 部分支持 Single Sign-on credentials 。 看到这个问题。

不仅对于 SSO，目前仍有一些未解决的问题可以使用 Leapp 解决。

例如，此问题请求 MFA 缓存。 目前，每次发出请求时，CDK 都会提示用户输入 MFA 代码。 此外，由于 Leapp 为您管理凭证文件，因此很容易避免像这样的不一致问题。

但不要害怕！ 本文将向您展示如何通过明智地使用我们的开源工具来改进您的 CDK 模板。

您会看到，可以在模板之外自动生成凭证——让它更干脆、更直接——使用 SSO 并同时激活多个凭证集。 通过使用命名配置文件，这将部署到错误环境的可能性降低到零。

我们准备了一个简单的测试用例，您可以按照它来了解有哪些可能性以及您可以在自己的项目中做什么。 让我们开始！

一切从一个简单的例子开始

为了更好地了解我们的工具可以为开发人员带来哪些优势，我们想向您展示不带和带 Leapp的 CDK 代码和终端命令的片段。

我们的示例包含一个 CDK 模板，用于在两个不同的账户中部署相同的 S3 存储桶。 即使非常微不足道，它的目的是展示如何通过将 Leapp 引入开发人员例程来简化代码。

引导程序

使用 CDK 时，您首先必须引导要在其中部署基础设施的 AWS 环境（如果您还没有的话）。 如果没有 Leapp，您通常会使用这个 cdk bootstrap 命令，正如官方 AWS 文档所建议的那样：

 cdk bootstrap aws : //ACCOUNT-NUMBER-1/REGION-1 aws://ACCOUNT-NUMBER-2/REGION-2

在此示例中，我们在一个命令中引导了您需要的两个帐户。 工作负载容易出错，不安全，并且如果您需要引导大量工作负载，可能会变得非常难以管理。

使用 Leapp，您需要已经设置一个或多个会话（不知道如何设置？在这里查看！）。 转到桌面应用程序，选择您的会话并双击它。

您还可以更改该会话的区域和命名配置文件。 左键单击它并选择更改→区域/命名配置文件

现在您可以键入cdk bootstrap ，CDK 将使用您选择的区域中的默认命名配置文件自动引导会话。 引导多个帐户时，或者如果您不使用默认命名配置文件，请添加标志cdk bootstrap --profile NAMED_PROFILE

如果您不想离开终端，可以使用新发布的Leapp CLI ！

使用命令leapp session start选择要启动的会话，更改其区域和命名配置文件，如果需要，使用leapp session change-region和leapp session change-profile然后你就设置好了！

合成和部署

第一件事第一！ 为了使我们的示例正确部署，当您实例化 CDK 堆栈时，请确保将道具中的env设置为以下值：

 { account : *** process . *** env . CDK_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT , region : *** process *** . env . CDK_DEFAULT_REGION }

在使用cdk init,您可以在bin 文件夹中找到此文件，并且在cdk.json文件中也引用了该文件。

一旦一切准备就绪并且账户被正确引导，在 Leapp 中开始你的会话，就像你在引导步骤中所做的那样。 请记住相应地更改区域和命名配置文件。 如果您想将出错的可能性降至最低，您可以使用自定义标志在脚本中以编程方式执行此操作。 检查奖金部分！

通过 CDK 使用单点登录

正如我们之前所说，CDK 仅部分支持 AWS Single Sign-on 生成的凭证，但使用 Leapp 可以克服这个限制。 并且也无需更改脚本中的任何内容！

您必须在 Leapp 中创建一个 *integration *****，就像创建 AWS 会话一样。 请看下面的视频：

通过这样做，您将恢复您的所有组织帐户和角色； 现在您只需要启动一个 SSO 会话，Leapp 就会创建与 CDK 完全兼容的短期凭证！

繁荣！ 现在您可以毫不费力地将 SSO 与 CDK 一起使用！

凭证过程

到目前为止显示的所有示例都基于短期凭证，这很棒，但即使是临时的，您仍然为潜在的攻击者敞开大门：AWS 文件中的凭证仍然是纯文本的，因此可以利用。

为了克服这个问题，AWS 还提供了在发出 SDK 或 CLI 命令之前即时生成凭证的能力。 此功能称为凭据过程！

 [ profile default] credential_process = leapp session generate SESSIONID

通过这样做，每次 CDK 需要访问一个或多个 SDK 命令时，Leapp都会自动发出有效凭据，而无需在您的文件中写入任何内容！

当然，它也适用于命名配置文件！

奖励：如何使用 Leapp CLI 自动化 CDK 部署

在这个简单的片段中，我们想向您展示如何创建命名配置文件，将其与新的AWS IAM 用户会话关联，启动该会话并部署您的基础设施，方法是预先设置配置文件名称。

 PROFILE_NAME = my-profile-name && leapp profile create --profileName $PROFILE_NAME && PROFILE_ID = $( leapp profile list -x | grep $PROFILE_NAME | awk '{print $1}' ) && leapp session add --providerType aws --sessionType awsIamUser --profileId $PROFILE_ID --sessionName MY-SESSION-NAME --region eu-west-1 --accessKey ACCESSKEY --secretKey SECRETKEY && SESSION_ID = $( leapp session list -x | grep 'MY-SESSION-NAME' | awk '{print $1}' ) && leapp session start --sessionId $SESSION_ID && cdk deploy --profile $PROFILE_NAME

总结

在本文中，我们了解了如何利用 Leapp 作为凭证​​管理系统来提高 CDK 模板的安全性。

通过使用 Leapp，您无需在凭证（或配置）文件或环境变量中编写任何长期或短期凭证。 通过使用凭证处理功能，您根本不需要凭证！

我们已经展示了 CDK 如何支持命名配置文件，这也是 Leapp 管理的一项功能，因此您可以同时保持所有凭据处于活动状态，从而减少 IDE 和 Leapp 之间的上下文切换。

我们展示了一些脚本，可让您将 CDK 工作程序与 Leapp CLI 集成，以进一步简化您的日常操作。

感谢 Leapp 从 AWS SSO 会话生成临时凭证，我们看到您也间接启用了单点登录。

我们希望这些细微的改进能让您的日常工作更轻松。 那么，你打算用 CDK 做什么呢？ 您对我们如何改进 Leapp 有什么建议吗？ 来我们社区打个招呼吧！

直到下一次，再见并保持安全😷

诺沃拉里团队。



原文:

We all love CDK! Don’t we all? Since its introduction, we’ve finally got a Typescript tool to write IaC on AWS precisely and structured for serverless applications.

But there is a crucial point that is not as agile as it could be: local credentials management.

There is no simple way to re-use the same template in different environments without writing complex scripts and extensively using environment variables.

Another point is that you are usually bound to long-term credentials most of the time, and that is a possible security threat.

Lastly, according to the documentation, CDK partially supports Single Sign-on credentials. See this issue.

Not only for SSO but currently, there are still some open issues that can potentially be addressed using Leapp.

For example, this issue requests MFA caching. Currently, CDK prompts the user for MFA code every time a request is issued. Leapp can avoid this as it correctly caches credentials until the session token is expired.

Furthermore, inconsistency issues like this one are easily avoided as Leapp manages the credentials file for you.

But fear not! This article will show you how you can improve your CDK templates by making wise use of our open-source tool.

You’ll see that it is possible to automate credentials generation outside the template – keeping it drier and more straightforward – use SSO and having more than one credential set active simultaneously. This reduces the possibility to deploy to the wrong environment to zero by using named profiles.

We have prepared a simple test case that you can follow along to understand what are the possibilities and what you can do in your own project. Let’s begin!

It all starts with a simple example

To better understand what advantages our tool can give to a developer, we want to show you snippets of CDK code and terminal commands without and with Leapp.

Our example consists of a CDK template to deploy the same S3 bucket in two different accounts. Even if very trivial, its purpose is to demonstrate how you can simplify your code by introducing Leapp into your developer routine.

Bootstrap

When using CDK you first have to bootstrap the AWS environments that you want to deploy your infrastructure in, if you haven’t already. Without Leapp, you would normally use this cdk bootstrap command, as suggested by official AWS documentation:

cdk bootstrap aws://ACCOUNT-NUMBER-1/REGION-1 aws://ACCOUNT-NUMBER-2/REGION-2

In this example, we bootstrapped both the accounts you’ll need in a single command. The workload is error-prone, not safe, and can become very difficult to manage if you need to bootstrap lots of them.

With Leapp, you need one or more sessions already set up (don’t know how to? Check it here!). Go to the desktop app, select your session and double click it.

You can also change the region and named profile for that session. Left-click on it and select Change → Region/Named Profile

Now you can type cdk bootstrap and CDK will automatically bootstrap the session with the default named profile in the region that you selected. When bootstrapping multiple accounts, or if you’re not using the default named profile, add the flag cdk bootstrap --profile NAMED_PROFILE

If you don’t want to leave your terminal, you can use the newly released Leapp CLI!

Use the command leapp session start to select which session to start, change its region and named profile if you need to with leapp session change-region and leapp session change-profile and then you’re set!

Synth and Deploy

First thing first! For our example to deploy properly, when you instantiate your CDK stack, make sure to set env in your props to the following value:

{ account: ***process.***env.CDK_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT, region: ***process***.env.CDK_DEFAULT_REGION
}

In a default CDK project created using cdk init, you can find this file inside the bin folder, and it’s also referenced in the cdk.json file.

Once everything’s ready and the accounts are properly bootstrapped, start your sessions in Leapp as you did for the bootstrap step. Remember to change region and named profile accordingly. If you want to reduce the possibility of error to the minimum, you can programmatically do that in a script using custom flags. Check the Bonus section!

cdk deploy will deploy in the default named profile session you set in Leapp.

cdk deploy --profile NAMED_PROFILE will deploy in a different named profile instead.

Use Single Sign-on with CDK

As we said before, CDK only partially supports credentials generated by AWS Single Sign-on, BUT with Leapp it is possible to overcome this limitation. And without changing anything in your scripts too!

You have to create an *integration *****in Leapp, like you would for an AWS session. See the video below:

By doing so, you’ll recover all your Organization accounts and roles; now you just have to start one of your SSO sessions and Leapp will create short-lived credentials completely compatible with CDK!

Boom! Now you’re using SSO with CDK without hassle!

Credential Process

All the examples shown until now are based on short-lived credentials, which is awesome, but even if temporary, you are still leaving an open door to potential attackers: credentials in AWS files are still in plain text and therefore exploitable.

To overcome this issue AWS also gives the ability to generate credentials on the fly, right before issuing an SDK or CLI command. This feature is called credential process!

[profile default]
credential_process = leapp session generate SESSIONID

Leapp overwrites the AWS config file command by adding the correct session ID for you and using its CLI to generate credentials in place of AWS.

By doing this, every time CDK needs to access one or more SDK commands, Leapp will automatically issue valid credentials, without writing anything in your files!

And of course, it works with named profiles too!

Bonus: How to use Leapp CLI to automate CDK deploy

Leapp comes with a CLI, which allows to automate all the actions you can do with the Desktop App via flags.

In this simple snippet we want to show you how to create a named profile, associate it with a new AWS IAM User session, start that session and deploy your infrastructure, by setting a profile name beforehand.

PROFILE_NAME=my-profile-name
&& leapp profile create --profileName $PROFILE_NAME && PROFILE_ID=$(leapp profile list -x | grep $PROFILE_NAME | awk '{print $1}') && leapp session add --providerType aws --sessionType awsIamUser --profileId $PROFILE_ID --sessionName MY-SESSION-NAME --region eu-west-1 --accessKey ACCESSKEY --secretKey SECRETKEY && SESSION_ID=$(leapp session list -x | grep 'MY-SESSION-NAME' | awk '{print $1}') && leapp session start --sessionId $SESSION_ID && cdk deploy --profile $PROFILE_NAME

To conclude

In this article, we’ve seen how you can improve the security of your CDK templates by leveraging Leapp as your credential management system.

By using Leapp, you don’t need to write any long or short-lived credential neither in your credential (or config) file nor environment variables. And by using the credential process feature you don’t need credentials at all!

We have shown how CDK supports named profiles which is a feature also managed by Leapp, so you can keep all your credentials active simultaneously, reducing the context switch between your IDE and Leapp.

We have showcased some scripts that let you integrate your CDK work routine with Leapp CLI to simplify your daily operations even further.

Thanks to Leapp generating temporary credentials from AWS SSO sessions, we have seen that you are also indirectly enabling Single Sign-on.

We hope that these slight improvements will make your day-by-day work easier. So, what are you planning to do with CDK? Do you have any suggestions on how we can improve Leapp? Come say “hi” in our community!

Until next time, goodbye and stay safe 😷

Noovolari team.
item.author: urz9999

item.title: 使用 ECS 部署容器化应用 | Deploy Containerized Applications with ECS
item.description:
git clone https://github.com/anuvindhs/CLOUD-is-AWSome.git

码头工人

构建一个 docker 文件

  • 让我们为我们的网站构建 NGINX docker 文件。

  • 在文件夹中创建 docker 文件后，复制粘贴以下命令

FROM nginx:latest COPY ./ /usr/share/nginx/html/

  • 我正在使用 VScode，请查看以下内容以了解文件夹结构

    确保您可以通过 AWS CLI 进行编程访问

  • 创建存储库

    • 从 AWS 控制台转到 ECR 并创建存储库
    • 创建一个 ECR 存储库，我将我的命名为webapp-ecr-repo

    登录 ECR

    如果您打开存储库，在右侧您可以看到查看推送命令的选项。

    现在使用 AWS CLI 登录 ECR

     aws ecr get-login-password --region xx-xxxxxx-xx| docker login --username AWS --password-stdin xxxxxxxxxx.dkr.ecr.xx-xxxxxx-xx.amazonaws.com

    构建 docker 镜像

    docker build -t webapp-ecr-repo .

    让我们用 repo 名称标记我们的图像

    docker tag webapp-ecr-repo:latest xxxxxxxxx.dkr.ecr.xx-xxxxxx-xx.amazonaws.com/webapp-ecr-repo:latest

    让我们将图像推送到存储库

    docker push xxxxxxxxxx.dkr.ecr.xx-xxxxxxx-xx.amazonaws.com/webapp-ecr-repo:latest

    如果您在 Windows 上使用 PowerShell，请使用 Windows 命令将图像推送到存储库。

    创建集群

    • 转到 ECS 仪表板，然后单击Create Cluster 。

    • 选择EC2 Linux + 网络

    • 输入集群名称和EC2 实例类型，我将其保留为 t2.micro。

    • 选择可用的默认 VPC 和子网。

    • 同时启用自动分配公共 IP

    • 您可以使用默认安全组或创建新的安全组，也可以附加已创建的安全组。
    • 现在点击创建

    创建任务定义

    • 单击创建任务定义，然后选择EC2

    • 我将我的命名为Taskdef-webapp-ecr

    • 将Task memory更新为128并将 Task CPU更新为1 vCPU ，然后单击Add Container

    • 添加一个名字，我的是container-webapp-ecr

    • 从存储库中复制您的 ECR 存储库 URI 并在图像列上更新它

    • 映射您的端口，这里是 80:80 ，

    • 现在添加并创建任务定义。

    运行任务

    • 转到Clusters ，选择Task然后单击Run New task

    • 选择EC2

    • 选择我们创建的任务定义。

    • 选择我们的集群

    • 将所有内容保留为默认值，然后单击运行任务

    创建任务后，您将能够看到运行状态
    如果您导航到外部链接，您将能够看到实时网站。

    恭喜，现在您已经成功配置了一个已推送到 ECR 并部署在带有 ECS 的容器上的 docker 镜像。

    ✅Connect with me on Twitter ✅在Twitter 上与我联系
    🤝🏽Connect with me on Linkedin 🤝🏽在Linkedin上与我联系
    🧑🏼‍🤝‍🧑🏻 Read more post on dev.to or iCTPro.co.nz 🧑🏼‍🤝‍🧑🏻 在dev.to或iCTPro.co.nz上阅读更多帖子
    💻 Connect with me on GitHub 💻 在GitHub 上与我联系

    Bruno Rezende Novais - Bruno Rezende Novais - May 4 5月4日



原文: 
git clone https://github.com/anuvindhs/CLOUD-is-AWSome.git

Docker

Building a docker file

  • Lets build our NGINX docker file for our website.

  • once you have created the docker file inside the folder, copy-paste the below commands

FROM nginx:latest
COPY ./ /usr/share/nginx/html/

  • I am using VScode, check below to understand the folder structure

    AWS Permissions

  • Make sure you have programmatic access via AWS CLI

  • Full permissions to ECS & AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryFullAccess

ECR

Create a repository

  • Goto ECR from AWS console and create a repository
  • Create a ECR repo ,i have named mine webapp-ecr-repo

Login to ECR

if you open the repo, on the right-hand side you can see an option for view push commands.

Now using AWS CLI login to ECR

aws ecr get-login-password --region xx-xxxxxx-xx| docker login --username AWS --password-stdin xxxxxxxxxx.dkr.ecr.xx-xxxxxx-xx.amazonaws.com

Build the docker image

docker build -t webapp-ecr-repo .

Lets tag our image with repo name

docker tag webapp-ecr-repo:latest xxxxxxxxx.dkr.ecr.xx-xxxxxx-xx.amazonaws.com/webapp-ecr-repo:latest

Lets push the image to the repository

docker push xxxxxxxxxx.dkr.ecr.xx-xxxxxxx-xx.amazonaws.com/webapp-ecr-repo:latest

if you are using PowerShell on windows please use windows commands to push the image to the repo.

ECS

Create a cluster

  • Goto ECS dashboard and click Create Cluster.

  • select EC2 Linux + Networking

  • Enter Cluster name & EC2 instance type , am keeping it to t2.micro.

  • Select the default VPC and Subnets available.

  • Also enable Auto assign public IP

  • you can use default Security group or create a new one or can attach a Security group which is already created.
  • Now click Create

Creating Task Definition

  • Click on create Task Definition & then select EC2

  • I am naming mine as Taskdef-webapp-ecr

  • Update Task memory to 128 and Task CPU to 1 vCPU, then click Add Container

  • Add a name, mine is container-webapp-ecr

  • Copy your ECR repo URI from the repository and update that on the image coloumn

  • Map your ports, here its 80:80 ,

  • Now add, and create the Task definition.

Run the Task

  • goto Clusters, select Task then click Run New task

  • Select EC2

  • Select out Task Definition we created.

  • Select our Cluster

  • leave everything default and click Run Task

Once the Task is created you will be able to see a Running Status
if you navigate to external Link you will be able to see the live website.

Congratulations, now you have successfully configured a docker image which you have pushed to ECR and Deployed on a container with ECS.

🧑🏼‍🤝‍🧑🏻 Read more post on dev.to or iCTPro.co.nz
💻 Connect with me on GitHub

Bruno Rezende Novais - May 4

item.title: [BADGE.AR] 根据您的成就创建 NFT | [BADGE.AR] Create NFTs from your Achievements
item.description:
根据您的成就创建 NFT。

当我们说“成就”时，我们的意思是您可以连接您的帐户并根据该社区或提供商给予您的奖励创建 NFT。 例如，您可以添加您的 Steam 帐户并从您的 Steam 成就创建 NFT。 换句话说，您可以从您的游戏会话中铸造 NFT。

对于这个 MVP，唯一可用的提供商是 Steam，但我们计划在未来添加其他类型的提供商。 这可能会打开根据您在其他平台/活动中的成就创建 NFT 的可能性。

为了实现这一点，我们使用appwirte作为后端来管理
我们的前端和连接到 cardano 区块链的节点之间的交互。

启发我们这样做的想法是建立一个去中心化的地方来存储我们来自不同平台的成就、徽章、奖励，并使这些成就不仅仅是我们获得它们的平台中的徽章。

提交类别：

链接到代码

根据您的成就创建 NFT。

概述

当我们说“成就”时，我们的意思是您可以连接您的帐户并根据该社区或提供商给予您的奖励创建 NFT。 例如，您可以添加您的 Steam 帐户并从您的 Steam 成就创建 NFT。 换句话说，您可以从您的游戏会话中铸造 NFT。

对于这个 MVP，唯一可用的提供商是 Steam，但我们计划在未来添加其他类型的提供商。 这可能会打开根据您在其他平台/活动中的成就创建 NFT 的可能性。

为了实现这一点，我们使用 appwirte 作为后端来管理我们的前端和连接到 cardano 区块链的节点之间的交互。

启发我们这样做的原因是……

链接到现场演示

网络应用

团队

  • 卡特里尔·穆勒

    开发者

      • 皮纳塔
      • 甲骨文

      演示视频

      截图



      原文:

      Create NFTs from your Achievements.

      BadgeAr is a place where you can transform your awards, trophies, and badges into a digital token (NFT).

      When we say "achievements" we mean that you can connect your accounts and create NFTs from awards given to you by that community or provider. For example, you can add you Steam account and create a NFT from your Steam achievements. In other words, you can mint NFTs from your gaming sessions.

      For this MVP, the only abailable provider is Steam, but we plan to add other kind of providers in the future. This could open the posibility to create NFTs from your achievements in other platforms / activities.

      In order to achieve this, we use appwirte as a backend to manage
      the interaction between our frontend and our nodes connected to the cardano blockchain.

      What inspired us to do this was the idea of having a decentralized place to store our achievements, badges, awards from different platforms and to make these achievements be more than just a badge within the platforms where we get them.

      Submission Category:

      Web3 Wunderkinds

      For this MVP, the only abailable provider is Steam, but we plan to add other kind of providers in the future. This could open the posibility to create NFTs from your achievements in other platforms / activities.

      In order to achieve this, we use appwirte as a backend to manage the interaction between our frontend and our nodes connected to the cardano blockchain.

item.title: Django vs React：你应该选择哪个框架？ | Django vs React: Which Framework You Should Go For?
item.description:
古普雷特·库马尔

发表于在当前应用程序开发市场的时代，有许多用于 Web 开发的框架，如 Django、Node.js、Ruby on Rails、Laravel、Meteor 等。Django 是最流行和广泛使用的框架之一网络应用程序的开发。

许多知名网站也建立在 Django 框架之上。 市场上有很多可用的 Django 框架。 它们很容易学习，但在某些方面有所不同。 你可以先学习 react，然后你就无法抗拒学习 Django。 在本文中，我们将介绍 react 和 Django 的基本使用，然后通过编写示例对两者进行比较。

什么是姜戈？
它由一位网页设计师开发，旨在成为在线应用程序中最快、最安全的框架之一。 它最大限度地使用 Python 语言，使其成为最快的框架之一。

什么是反应？

大多数 Web 应用程序都在使用 React 框架，因为它能够使应用程序动态、快速和灵活。 它被初创公司和老牌公司所使用。 几年前，Facebook 将“React”这个词引入了 Web 开发者社区。 下面我们提到了 Django 与 React 的一些基本优缺点。 你应该意识到这一点。

在设计中包含电池

  • 快速发展
  • 安全
  • 基于 Python 的网络框架
  • 易于扩展
  • 社区支持

    • 该产品不适合小型项目。

  • 它有一个单一的框架。
  • 它不支持众多处理器。
  • 紧密相连

    • 创建动态 Web 应用程序变得更加简单。

  • 易于理解和使用
  • 性能提升
  • 可重用组件
  • 以对 SEO 友好而著称
  • 便捷工具的支持
  • 测试代码的范围
  • 拥有 JavaScript 库的好处

    • 高增长率

  • 查看零件
  • 糟糕的文档
  • 哪个是更好的框架：Django vs React？
    好吧，这两个框架都更好，而且需求量也很大。 决定哪个更好有点困难。 我们将尝试更多地分解差异，以便您决定哪个更好。

    确实，这两个框架都很棒。

    然而，你不能到处使用 React。 特别是，React 不适用于服务器端渲染。 如果需要构建全栈应用程序，Django 是更合适的选择。 最后的话……
    这个问题的答案是，这取决于您将使用它们构建什么。 如果您正在寻找 Python 的强大功能，那么我们建议您选择 Django。 它是健壮的、可扩展的和安全的。 它拥有庞大的 3rd 方应用生态系统。



    • 原文:

    Gurpreet Kumar

    Posted on May 12

    In the present era of the application development market, there are a number of frameworks for web development, like- Django, Node.js, Ruby on Rails, Laravel, Meteor, etc. Django is one of the most popular and widely used frameworks for the development of web applications.

    Some of the applications like Pinterest, Instagram, and Disqus are developed by the Django framework. Many high-profile websites are also built on the Django framework. There are lots of Django frameworks available in the market. The Django framework has lots of features, which makes it a good choice for developers around the world.

    Both react and Django are very famous in today's web development. They are very easy to learn, but they are different in some aspects. You can learn react at first, then you cannot resist learning Django. In this article, we will introduce the basic use of react and Django, and then compare the two by writing an example.

    What Is Django?
    Django is a free and open-source Python programming Web framework, which is widely used for the development of websites. It was developed by a web designer, with the aim of being one of the fastest and most secure frameworks for online applications. Django is written in Python and uses the Model View Controller (MVC) architecture. It uses the Python language to its best capacity, making it one of the fastest frameworks.

    What is React?

    The React framework is being used by the majority of web applications because of its ability to make the applications dynamic, fast, and flexible. It is being used by startups and established companies alike. A couple of years ago, Facebook introduced the word "React" to the web developer community. React is an open-source JavaScript library for building user interfaces. React was created by Jordan Walke, a software engineer at Facebook.

    Django vs React: Pros and Cons. Below we have mentioned some of the essential Pros and cons of Django vs React. You should be aware of that.

    Pros of Django

    1. Include batteries in the design
    2. Rapid Development
    3. Security
    4. Python-based web framework
    5. Easily scalable
    6. Community Support

    Cons of Django

    1. This product is not appropriate for small projects.
    2. It has a monolithic framework.
    3. It doesn't support numerous processors.
    4. Django is not easy to learn.
    5. There are no features in the ORM system.
    6. Closely connected

    Pros of React

    1. Creating dynamic Web apps has become simpler.
    2. Easy to comprehend and use
    3. Performance Enhancement
    4. Reusable Components
    5. Known to be SEO Friendly
    6. The Support of Handy Tools
    7. Scope for Testing the Codes
    8. The Benefit of Having a JavaScript Library

    Cons of React

    1. The high rate of growth
    2. View Part
    3. Poor Documentation
    4. JSX as a barrier

    Which Is Better Framework: Django vs React?
    Well, both frameworks are better and also in huge demand. Deciding which one is better is a bit difficult. We will try to break down the difference more so that you can decide which one is better.

    The Django community is huge and constantly growing, the React community is much smaller, but growing rapidly. Django is a full-stack framework, React is a library. Django is an all-in-one solution, React is a piece of the puzzle. Django is mature, React is a new kid. It is true that both frameworks are great.

    However, you can’t use React everywhere. In particular, React doesn’t work well with server-side rendering. If you need to build a full-stack application, Django is a more appropriate choice. Django has better documentation, React has more community support.

    Final Words…
    The answer to this question is that it depends on what you are going to build with them. If you are looking for the power of Python, then we recommend that you choose Django. It is robust, scalable, and secure. The Django framework is known for its flexibility and ease of use. Django is best suited for complex data-driven applications. Django has a huge community of programmers who are constantly contributing to the project. The Django framework is highly customizable. It has a huge ecosystem of 3rd party apps.
item.author: gurpreet2030

item.title: API 优先有很多途径——选择你自己的冒险 | There Are Many Paths to API-First — Choose Your Own Adventure
item.description:
当组织讨论什么是 API 优先方法时，这可能会产生焦虑、困惑甚至敌意。 作为 Postman 的首席布道者，我认识到这些讨论的重要性。 使用正确的方法，API 优先将为您的团队减轻痛苦和痛苦，并为您的组织节省时间和金钱。 这最终会决定在当今的数字环境中保持竞争力还是落后。

它们在我们的桌面、网络和移动应用程序背后，还将标志、电器和汽车等日常物品连接到互联网。 当我们使用这些对象时，我们倾向于关注我们用来控制它们的界面。 但就在应用程序的表面之下，API 正在承担繁重的工作，实现连接并执行其他有意义的操作。 并认识到优先考虑 API 时所产生的隐私、安全性和可重用性。

没有管道就没有自来水，没有电源线就没有电力。 在没有 API 的业务情况下，您将无法获得数字资源和功能。 能够管理您的业务运营需要在任何给定时刻对您的企业状态进行观察和控制。 随着 API 越来越多地定义操作， API 治理就是您实现目标的方式。

对于许多踏上 API 优先之旅的团队来说，它是北极星。 它是关于使用OpenAPI 规范来定义和设计 API 的表面积； 为其他利益相关者生成模拟服务器以练习和理解 API； 记录 API； 并收集利益相关者关于如何改进它的反馈。 然后对设计进行迭代，直到您拥有满足消费者需求的 API 合约，并在开发期间通过测试进行验证。 您可以快速迭代并最终通过人类和机器可读的合同从另一端出来，该合同可用于在生产中验证 API，确保随着时间的推移没有任何变化。

还是有很多困惑

在 API 的世界中，关于什么是 API 优先存在混淆。 我们中的许多人看到了 API 优先，特别是 API 设计优先的潜力，在与其他团队谈论它时可能会对此感到有些厌烦。 我们并不总是认识到企业内部的一些实际情况。 有时将 API 设计优先描述为我们的北极星可能会使我们想要接触的人感到困惑，从而使整个企业的API 优先转换变得困难。 我希望通过更精确的讲故事、讲座和研讨会来帮助缓解这种困惑。 我相信我们可以扩大 API 优先的方法，使其对所有团队更具包容性，从而减少我们在努力赢得整个企业组织的人心时遇到的摩擦。

通往 API 优先的不同路径

但是，除了 API 设计优先之外，还有 API 优先的方法，包括：

  • 代码优先：在对使用 API 的应用程序进行编码之前，您优先考虑对 API 进行编码。
  • 原型优先：在生成 OpenAPI 定义之前，您使用集合开发、模拟和记录您的 API。 使用代理或 Postman 拦截器对 API 进行逆向工程，生成集合，然后最终从集合中生成 OpenAPI 也很常见
  • 代理优先：您通过代理或 Postman Interceptor 运行现有 API，并根据现有流量生成集合
  • 还有一个很好的老式手工制作现有 API 的Postman 集合，然后从该集合生成 OpenAPI。

要获得 API 优先的资格，这些方法中的每一种都必须生成一个 API 合约，以确保每个人都在同一页面上，通过测试作为操作的一部分进行验证，并以机器可读的方式自动化API 生命周期。

找到您最喜欢的实现 API 优先优势的方法

最后，我并不关心您如何获得 API 优先。 我很高兴看到您的团队配备了设计优先、代码优先、原型优先、代理优先或手动方法来交付、操作和迭代 API。 您只需要确保最终有一个 API 合约可用于支持文档，使用合约测试进行验证，并确保 API 可被生产者和消费者发现。

如果您能够满足消费者的需求，并继续在 Web、移动和设备应用程序以及系统集成中提供价值，那么没人会关心您是如何到达那里的。 设计优先绝对是团队实现 API 优先之旅的理想方式，但在选择的路径上应该减少分歧，更多地关注我们组织的旅程和转型。

就个人而言，我最喜欢的方法是原型优先。 尽管我喜欢编辑 OpenAPI 定义，但我发现创建集合、模拟、记录和迭代新 API 是获得真实事物的最快方法。 实际上，我发现自己以这种方式操作 API，添加数据作为示例，并在模拟服务器上构建我的应用程序和集成数周甚至数月。

然后，在我稳定了我的底层数据模型并充实了 API 的使用方式之后，我生成了一个 OpenAPI，生成了合约测试，然后开始开发一个 API。

我发现这是两全其美的，但这就是我。 我知道这种方法并不适合所有人，我也不打算贬低不同的方法。 他们只需要生成一个让消费者满意的 API。 最终，我们应该花更少的时间批评那些代码优先的人或告诉 API 设计优先的人他们正在放慢速度，并找到一种在我们的企业组织中采用多种方法的方法。

这篇文章有很多通往 API 优先的路径——选择你自己的冒险，首先出现在Postman 博客上。



原文:

API-first is an important topic, but it’s also one that’s sometimes misunderstood and miscommunicated. This can produce anxiety, confusion, and even hostility as organizations discuss what qualifies as an API-first approach. As chief evangelist here at Postman, I recognize how important these discussions are. There’s a significant meaning and purpose wrapped up in the API-first concept. With the right approach, API-first will save your team pain and suffering, and save your organization time and money. This can ultimately spell the difference between remaining competitive or falling behind in today’s digital landscape.

APIs are powering our world

APIs are powering everything around us. They are behind our desktop, web, and mobile apps, and also connect everyday objects like signs, appliances, and cars to the internet. When we use these objects, we tend to focus on the interface that we use to control them. But just beneath the surface of the apps, APIs are doing the heavy lifting, enabling connectivity and performing other meaningful actions. API-first is simply about “seeing” the APIs as equally important–if not more–than the surface application. And recognizing the privacy, security, and reusability that result when APIs are prioritized.

API-first is about prioritization

You don’t get tap water without pipes, nor electricity without power lines. And you don’t get digital resources and capabilities in business situations without APIs. API-first is about acknowledging that you must prioritize APIs to achieve the connectivity, productivity, and quality you desire across your teams. Being able to govern your business operations requires observability and control over the state of your enterprise at any given moment. And with operations increasingly defined by APIs, API governance is how you get there.

The beauty of API design-first

API design-first is a subset of an API-first strategy. It’s the North Star for many teams that embark on an API-first journey. API design-first is about designing APIs in a collaborative way before you write code or release anything into production. It’s about using the OpenAPI Specification to define and design the surface area of your API; generating a mock server for other stakeholders to practice with and understand the API; documenting the API; and gathering feedback from stakeholders on how to improve it. Then iterating upon the design until you have an API contract that meets the needs of consumers, as verified by tests during development. API design-first is about making sure APIs meet the needs of consumers. You are able to iterate rapidly and ultimately come out the other side with a human- and machine-readable contract that can be used to validate the API in production, ensuring that nothing has changed over time.

There is still a lot of confusion

In the world of APIs, there is confusion about what constitutes API-first. Many of us who see the potential of API-first, and specifically API design-first, can get a little nerdy about it while talking about it with other teams. We don’t always recognize some of the realities on the ground within the enterprise. Sometimes describing API design-first as our North Star can confuse the people we want to reach, making API-first transformation across the enterprise difficult. I’m looking to help alleviate this confusion with more precise storytelling, talks, and workshops. I believe we can widen the API-first approach to be more inclusive to all teams, reducing the friction we encounter as we work to win hearts and minds across enterprise organizations.

Different paths towards API-first

API design-first is the most logical and healthy approach to achieving API-first because it allows teams to move faster while ensuring producer and consumers stakeholders are on the same page. However, there are API-first approaches besides API design-first, including:

  • Code-first : You prioritize coding the API before you code the applications that use it.
  • Prototype-first: You develop, mock, and document your API using a collection before generating an OpenAPI definition. It is also common to use a proxy or Postman Interceptor to reverse engineer APIs, generating a collection, and then ultimately an OpenAPI from the collection
  • Proxy-first: You run your existing API through a proxy or Postman Interceptor, and generate a collection based upon existing traffic
  • There is also good old-fashioned handcrafting of a Postman Collection for an existing API and then generating an OpenAPI from that collection.

To qualify as API-first, each of these approaches must produce an API contract that ensures everyone is on the same page, is validated as part of operations via tests, and automates the API lifecycle in a machine-readable way.

Find your favorite approach to achieving API-first benefits

In the end, I am not concerned with how you get to API-first. I’d be glad to see your teams equipped with design-first, code-first, prototype-first, proxy-first, or manual approaches to delivering, operating, and iterating upon APIs. You just need to make sure there ultimately is an API contract that can be used to power documentation, be validated using contract tests, and ensure the API is discoverable by producers and consumers.

The OpenAPI will provide you with a source of truth for the entire API lifecycle, enable collaboration, observability, and lay the foundation for governance. If you can meet the needs of your consumers, and continue delivering value within the web, mobile, and device applications, as well as system integration, nobody will care how you got there. Design-first is definitely the ideal way for teams to approach their API-first journey, but there should be less disagreement on the chosen path and more focus on the journey and transformation of our organizations.

Personally, my favorite approach is prototype-first. As much as I love editing an OpenAPI definition, I find creating a collection, mocking, documenting, and iterating upon a new API to be the fastest way to get to the real thing. I actually find myself operating the API in this fashion, adding data as examples, and building my applications and integrations on top of the mock server for weeks or even months.

Then, after I have stabilized my underlying data model and fleshed out how the API will be consumed, I generate an OpenAPI, produce contract tests, and then get to work developing an API.

I find this to be the best of both worlds, but this is me. I understand this approach won’t work for everyone, and I am not about to disparage different approaches. They just need to produce an API that makes consumers happy. Ultimately, we should spend less time criticizing folks who are code-first or telling the API design-first folks they are slowing things down, and find a way to embrace multiple approaches within our enterprise organizations.

The post There Are Many Paths to API-First—Choose Your Own Adventure appeared first on Postman Blog.
item.author: jansche

item.title: 仅崩溃系统的快速故事 | The quick story of Crash-only System
item.description:
前段时间我正在做一个项目，检查所有的盒子是否绝对糟糕。 最大的障碍之一是每次用户操作都会发出数百个事件。 显然，它引起了许多不可预测的问题。 应该妥善处理的问题。 或者淘汰。 这两者都意味着费力的工作，通常成本高昂并且对项目构成威胁。 如果有第三条路怎么办？

在提到的项目中，我们已经诊断出一个功能占错误总数的 20%。 只有一个！ 此外，几乎所有使用它的尝试都以更大或更小的问题告终。 为改进它做了很多工作。 许多才华横溢的开发人员不得不认输。 而且，更糟糕的是，每一次尝试，每一个开发人员，都会在源代码中留下各种工件，从而通过增加其复杂性来火上浇油。

尽管如此，我们还是不能放弃。 它产生了太多的问题。

该功能的特点是刷新整个应用程序，几乎重新渲染它等等。 在此之后，我们测量了平均周期时间，并在第一次应用程序运行期间添加了初始引导时间。 原来是500ms。 很多，我的意思是很多，所以我们开始寻找可能的改进。 我们的目标是一个雄心勃勃的目标，它假设低于 30 毫秒，这代表 Chrome 中允许的最大宏任务时间。 几周后我们到达了它。 然后是第二阶段的时间 - 每次功能发生错误时，我们都想关闭和打开系统，重新启动应用程序的整个状态，包括所有错误。 我们可以这样做，因为应用程序不需要持久状态。 如果不到 30 毫秒，用户甚至不会注意到它，对吧？ 很快发现，30 毫秒足以注意到“闪烁”，这就是我们使用 OpenGL 中已知的技巧的原因 - 我们在旧帧的背景中加载下一帧，然后切换它们。 换句话说，应用程序的新实例隐藏在旧实例后面，直到它准备好显示。 整个操作耗时30ms多一点。

几周后，我们似乎完全无意识地、无意地实施了 Crash-only System。 引用维基百科：“仅崩溃软件是指通过简单地重新启动来处理故障而不尝试任何复杂恢复的计算机程序。 与其花费数百小时尝试开发正确的错误处理，也永远无法确定是否涵盖了所有情况，我们可以从不同的角度解决问题——假设如果出现错误，服务将能够足够快地重新启动而不会干扰整个系统的正常运行。

但我们更进一步！ 根据统计数据，我们快速计算出功能中出现错误的概率。 可能性如此之高，以至于我们决定进一步简化解决问题的新方法——我们没有维护负责诊断错误发生时刻并重新启动系统的源代码，而是假设问题肯定会发生，所以我们重新启动了立即申请。

我知道你在想什么。 这里的一切都是一个大谎言，因为实际上我们没有解决任何问题，我们只是隐藏了我们无法解决的问题，对吧？ 话虽如此，事实是我们：（1）显着提高了应用程序性能； 而解决方案，虽然起初看起来可能令人惊讶，但事实证明它对每个人来说都是微不足道的和众所周知的 - 我们唯一需要的就是关闭和打开它。



原文:

Some time ago I was working on a project that checked all the boxes for being absolutely awful. One of the biggest obstacles was emitting hundreds of events with every single user action. Obviously, it caused many unpredictable issues. Issues which should have been handled properly. Or eliminated. Both of which mean laborious effort, often costly and threatening for the project. What if there was a third path?

In the mentioned project we had diagnosed that one functionality accounted for 20% of the overall number of bugs. Just one! Moreover, almost any attempt to use it ended up with a bigger or smaller issue. A lot was done to improve it. Many talented developers had to admit defeat. And, what’s even worse, every single try, every single developer, left various artifacts behind in the source code which added fuel to the fire by increasing its complexity.

Still, we couldn’t give up. It was producing too many issues.

The functionality was characterized by refreshing the whole application, almost rendering it anew, among other things. Following that lead, we measured the average cycle time and adding to it initial bootstrapping time during the first application run. It turned out that it was 500ms. It’s a lot, and I mean a lot a lot, so we started looking for possible improvements. Our goal was an ambitious one, it assumed going down below 30ms which stands for maximal permissible macro-task time in Chrome. We reached it a few weeks later. Then it was time for phase two - each time an error occurred in the functionality, we wanted to turn off and on the system, restarting the whole state of the application including all the errors. We could do it, because the application didn’t require the persistent state. If it takes less than 30ms, a user won’t even notice it, right? Quickly turned out that 30ms is long enough to notice a “blink”, that’s why we used a trick known from OpenGL among others - we loaded next frame in the background of the old one and then switched them. In other words, the new instance of the application hid behind the old one until it was ready to be displayed. The whole operation took slightly more than 30ms.

A few weeks later it appeared that we implemented Crash-only System totally unconsciously and unintentionally. Quoting Wikipedia: “Crash-only software refers to computer programs that handle failures by simply restarting, without attempting any sophisticated recovery.” Isn’t it mind-blowing? Instead of spending hundreds of hours trying to develop proper error handling, never being sure if all the cases were covered, we can approach the problem from a different angle - assume that if there’s an error the service would be able to reboot quickly enough without interfering the proper functioning of the system as a whole.

But we went one step further! Based on statistical data, we quickly calculated the probability of a bug occurrence within the functionality. The probability was so high that we decided to further simplify the new approach to the problem - instead of maintaining the source code responsible for diagnosing the moment of bug occurrence and restarting the system, we assumed that the problem will occur for sure so we reboot the application immediately.

I know what you’re thinking. Everything here is one big lie, because in fact we didn’t fix anything, we only hid the problem which we couldn’t solve, right? Having said that, the fact is we: (1) have significantly boosted application performance; (2) from the technical point of view the system works properly right now, because it meets the users’ expectations; (3) over a course of a few weeks, we have reduced the overall number of bugs by 20%. And the solution, although it might seem surprising at first, it turned out to be trivial and well-known for everyone - the only thing we needed was just to turn it off and on.
item.author: kedzierskidawid

item.title: 从建造到销售 - EP.08 | From building to selling - EP.08
item.description:
欢迎来到 Crossroad 构建之旅的第八天！ 本系列讲述了我从构建到销售的独立黑客产品之旅。 如果你错过了第七天，你可以在这里查看。 如果您想观看重播，我还将今天的Twitch 流上传到YouTube 。

完成了设计。 当我查看我昨天做的设计时，我注意到什么不起作用。 深色的背景色让整个页面感觉封闭和压抑。 我需要打开一些东西，这意味着我必须摆脱背景颜色并开始使用空白。

您可以点击此链接查看最终设计。

正如你所看到的，我在这个设计中走向了一个完全不同的方向。 更多的空白，浅绿色的主题，并使视频成为标题中更加关键的部分。 潦草的线条也让页面感觉就像你在沿着一条路径，购买路径！ 但是，设计还不完美。 例如，导航中的十字路口“标志”需要大 2 倍，我需要为内容创建更好的图像。 虽然间距比昨天的版本好得多，但我可以让它更加一致，并且文本的行高。

也许我明天会更新设计，但我想我会在前端工作的同时进行这些更改，以节省时间。

这是令人难以置信的，黑暗模式一切！

使用我自己的产品。 在处理新标题时，我想，“如果我要购买任何模板，我对营销页面不感兴趣，直到我看到它的实际效果。” 所以，由于这个产品是因为我不得不抓痒（以及 Twitter 上的一些人）而构建的，所以我将创建links.twankrui.com作为第一个使用 Crossroad 模板的站点。

为了证明产品有效，我会在发布产品之前在简历页面中设置我的链接。 这样，我可以包含“显示示例”按钮，以便人们知道该产品实际上已经在生产环境中使用。

一旦其他人开始在生物页面中发布他们的链接，我计划添加一个“展示”部分。

提供免费版本。 除了为自己创造收入外，我还在学习营销。 我需要加深对该领域的了解，以更好地制作销售和实际销售的东西。

我正在阅读父亲推荐给我的一本名为“常识直销和数字营销”的书。 这是一本非常厚实的书，到目前为止，它是一本很棒的书。

今天我读到了公司如何赠送免费的东西来收集潜在客户的电子邮件地址。 有时，人们可能会在免费获得某些东西后向您购买。 这对我来说并不新鲜，但我必须阅读这篇文章才能想出一个关于 Crossroad 的想法。

在定价部分，我将添加一个免费选项。

我需要开发一个模板，该模板既包含足够的块以用作生物页面中的链接，又使您想购买专业版以获得更多块。

此选项将允许我收集获得免费版本的人的电子邮件地址，然后我可以开始与这些人交谈，看看我是否可以将他们转换为精简版或专业版。 如果没有，我仍然可以通过反馈的形式获得一些价值。

也许他们正在生物工具的链接中寻找一些东西，即使是 Crossroad 也不提供。 如果是这样，我想知道它是什么。

让我们建立着陆页！ 明天我将开始编写登录页面。 我们将设置 NextJS 环境并研究如何实现模板的文档。 理想情况下，我希望将它们放在同一个项目中。

所以，回到编码，它是！

再次感谢您阅读此构建日志。 祝你有美好的一天，我明天见（或今天在我的直播中）！ 如果您想同时保持更新，请在 Twitter 上关注我。

带着爱，

旺克鲁伊



原文:

Welcome to day eight of the Crossroad build journey! This series follows my journey of indie hacking a product from building to selling. If you missed day seven, you could check it out here. I also uploaded today's Twitch stream to YouTube if you want to watch the replay.

Finished the design. 🚀

When I looked at the designs, I made yesterday, I noticed what was not working. The dark background colors made the whole page feel closed and depressed. I needed to open things up, so that meant that I had to get rid of the background color and start using whitespace.

You can follow this link to see the final design.

As you can see, I went in a completely different direction with this design. More whitespace, a light green theme, and making the video an even more critical part of the header. The scribbly lines also make the page feel like you are following a path, the path to purchase! 😏

However, the design isn’t perfect yet. For example, the Crossroad “logo” in the navigation needs to be 2x bigger, and I need to create better images for the content. While the spacing is way better than yesterday’s version, I can make it a bit more consistent and the line height of the text.

Maybe I’ll update the design tomorrow, but I think I’ll make these changes while working on the front-end for the sake of time.

P.S. Did you know that Figma released dark mode this week? It’s incredible, dark mode everything!

Using my own product. 🛳

While working on the new header, I thought, "If I was buying any template, I am not interested in the marketing page until I've seen it in action." So, since this product is built because I have to scratch my itch (and some people on Twitter), I will create links.twankrui.com as the first site using the Crossroad template.

To prove the product works, I will set up my link in bio page before I am going to launch the product. This way, I can include the "show example" button so people know the product is actually already used in a production environment.

I plan to add a "showcase" section once other people start shipping their link in bio pages.

Offering a free version. 💲

Besides creating an income for myself, I am also learning about marketing. I need to deepen my knowledge of the field to get better at making stuff that sells and actually selling it.

I am reading a Book called "Commonsense Direct and Digital Marketing" recommended to me by my father. It's a pretty dense book, and thus far, it's been a great read.

Today I read about how companies give away free stuff to gather email addresses for leads. Sometimes, people are likely to buy from you after getting something for free. This isn't new for me, but I had to read this to come up with an idea for Crossroad.

In the pricing section, I am going to add a free option.

I need to develop a template that both include enough blocks to use as your link in bio page, but also makes you want to buy the pro version for more blocks.

This option will allow me to gather the email addresses of the people who got the free version, and then I can start to talk to these people to see if I can convert them to the lite or pro version. If not, I can still get some value in the form of feedback.

Maybe they are looking for something in a link in bio tool, that even Crossroad doesn't offer. If so, I want to know what it is.

Let’s build the landing page! 🔨

Tomorrow I am going to start coding the landing page. We’ll set up the NextJS environment and also research how we can implement the documentation for the template. Ideally, I want to have those in the same project.

So, back to coding, it is!

Thanks again for reading this build log. Have a great day, and I’ll see you tomorrow (or in my stream today)! 🚀

If you want to stay updated in the meantime, give me a follow on Twitter.

With love,

Twankrui
item.author: twankrui

item.title: JavaScript 中的提升和临时死区（TDZ）？ | Hoisting and Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ) in JavaScript?
item.description:
在第一次尝试时，起重和时间死区可能很难理解。 我将尝试以最简单的方式之一让您理解，请与我在一起直到最后。

首先要了解提升，我们必须了解 JS 程序是如何运行的。

一个 JS 程序分两个阶段运行：

  1. 内存分配阶段
  2. 代码执行阶段

内存分配阶段

在 JS 程序中，所有变量都只分配内存而不是它们的值，所有函数都分配有它们的主体。

代码执行阶段

内存分配代码执行完成后。 代码执行是逐行完成的

吊装

在英语词典中提升的意思：提升或拉起。

在 JS 中的提升也是以同样的方式完成的。 在定义之前调用变量或函数时，这称为提升。

当一个 var 变量被提升时，它给出undefined但是当一个 let 或 const 变量被提升时，它给出ReferenceError

代码示例：
如果是 var

console.log(bestFood); // returns undefined because bestFood's TDZ does not exist here var bestFood = "Vegetable Fried Rice"; // bestFood's TDZ does not exist here

在 let 和 const 的情况下

console.log(bestFood); // returns ReferenceError because bestFood's TDZ
let bestFood = "Vegetable Fried Rice";

发生这种情况是因为当 var 变量的内存分配完成时，它会分配一个占位符“未定义”但在 let 和 const 的情况下不会发生这样的事情

当变量未初始化并且您尝试在变量完成初始化之前访问变量时，会出现临时死区或 TDZ ，然后它会给出ReferenceError

函数也可以被提升。

代码示例：

catName("Tiger"); function catName(name) { console.log("My cat's name is " + name);

输出：我的猫叫Tiger

正如您已经知道的，当函数被提升时，整个函数体在内存分配阶段被复制。 因此，如果函数提升它不会给出任何错误。



原文:

Hoisting and Temporal dead zone can be difficult to understand in the first try. I will try to make you understand in one of the simplest ways possible please be with me till the end.

First to understand hoisting we will have to understand how does a JS program runs.

A JS program runs in 2 phases:

  1. Memory allocation phase
  2. Code execution phase

Memory Allocation Phase

In a JS program all the variables are allocated only memory not their values and all the functions are allocated with their body.

Code Execution Phase

After memory allocation code execution is done. Code execution is done line by line

Hoisting

Meaning of hoisting in English dictionary: raise or haul up.

Hoisting in JS is also done in same way. When a variable or function is called before it is defined, this is known as hoisting.

Hoisting in case of let and const variables is different from hoisting with var variables

When a var variable is hoisted it gives undefined but when a let or const variable is hoisted it gives ReferenceError

code Example:
In case of var

console.log(bestFood); // returns undefined because bestFood’s TDZ does not exist here var bestFood = "Vegetable Fried Rice"; // bestFood’s TDZ does not exist here

console.log(bestFood);

In case of let and const

console.log(bestFood); // returns ReferenceError because bestFood’s TDZ
let bestFood = "Vegetable Fried Rice";

This happens because when memory allocation of var variable is done it allocates a placeholder "undefined" but in case of let and const no such thing happens

Temporal Dead Zone or TDZ occurs when a variable is not initialized and you attempt to access a variable before its complete initialization, then it gives a ReferenceError

TDZ ends when you initialize that variable

Functions can also be hoisted.

code example:

catName("Tiger"); function catName(name) { console.log("My cat's name is " + name);

}

output: My cat's name is Tiger

As you already know that when functions are hoisted then whole body of function is copied in memory allocation phase. So, In case function hoisting it does not give any error.
item.author: devashishsethi

item.title: SSG 相对于 SSR 的优势 | Advantages of SSG over SSR
item.description:
我们将在本文中了解为什么应该使用 SSG 而不是 SSR。

好吧，不要把这个当作一概而论

除了将 HTML 传递给客户端之外，服务器无需执行任何操作。 因此，对于连接速度较慢的用户来说，加载静态站点比加载服务器端呈现 (SSR) 站点要快得多，因为内容已经被处理。

静态网站也很快，即使来源很慢。 这是因为静态站点不需要对源进行处理——它只需要获取和传递文件。

静态站点改进了第一个字节 (TTFB) 的时间。 该指标受许多因素的影响，包括延迟、缓存和后续请求的处理。 因为在使用 SSG 时不需要服务器端处理，它们可以为您提供即时 TTFB，因此可以从 Google Lighthouse 获得更好的网络核心生命体征得分。

如果你曾经使用过 CSS 预处理器，你就会熟悉这个概念：如果你在代码中犯了错误并尝试编译它，你会得到一个错误。 这是因为这些预处理器在客户端运行，实际上并没有编译任何东西。

静态站点生成器 (SSG) 相对于服务器端渲染 (SSR) 的优势很多，但其中有一个对我来说很突出。 这是关于弹性的。 虽然服务器呈现的站点依赖于 API，但静态站点生成器并不关心。 如果您的 API 出现故障，您的页面仍将显示在构建时获取的数据。

例如，如果您有一个像 NASA 的 Astronomy Picture of the Day API 这样的 API，但它在圣诞节、元旦、超级碗或任何一天流量很大的时候出现故障，您的网站仍然可以工作，因为它在自己的文件和目录中拥有所需的所有数据。 唯一不起作用的是补水——在初始渲染后，您的页面将无法获取新图片。 但至少你的用户仍然会看到一些东西！

首先，使用 SSG 可以实现更简单的缓存逻辑。 在我的网站上，我将生成的 HTML 缓存在 CDN 上，并且仅在我的降价文件之一更改时才重新生成它。 这使我即使在移动设备上也能获得非常快的页面加载时间，尽管将网站托管在一个前面没有 CDN 的小型服务器上。

借助 SSG，您可以使用 CDN 缓存来提高性能，而无需进行大量额外配置。 如果您有一个包含 50 个页面的静态站点，您可以将它们全部放在 CDN 上并从那里而不是您自己的服务器提供它们，这会极大地帮助您的页面加载时间——但是如果您更新其中一个页面，您需要使它们失效CDN 上的缓存版本。 如果每个页面都有自己的缓存控制标头，这很容易

它的运行成本也大大降低，因为您不必为多台服务器的冗余维护付费。

您可以将它们托管在您喜欢的任何云提供商上，它们将继续按照宣传的方式工作。

少一台服务器的安全态势是另一个很大的好处。 如果 SSG 受到威胁，它只能访问自己的数据，而不能访问您网络或环境中的任何其他数据。

无限规模是另一个主要优势。 只要您的 Web 服务器上有足够的容量来处理初始流量高峰，您就可以添加更多 Web 服务器，而不必担心将它们放在哪里或它们将消耗多少带宽。

结论

为什么应该使用 SSG 而不是 SSR 有很多优点。 我们只看到少数，还有更多。
但是当我们开始开发一个 Web 应用程序时，很明显没有通用的解决方案。 我们需要根据我们的项目做出决定，并且应该始终仔细权衡各个方面。 有时候SSR会比SSG更有优势！



原文:

We will see in this article why you should use SSG over SSR.

Well, don't take this for a generalization

1.Performance

The server doesn't have to do anything except deliver the HTML to the client. Because of this, for users with a slow connection, it's a much faster experience to load a static site than it is to load a server-side rendered (SSR) site because the content has already been processed.

Static sites are also fast, even if the origin is slow. This is because there's no processing required on the origin for static sites—it just has to fetch and deliver the file.

Static sites improve time to first byte (TTFB). TTFB is an important metric that shows how fast your site begins loading in users' browsers—the lower this number, the better. This metric is influenced by many factors including latency, caching, and processing on subsequent requests. Because there's no server-side processing required when using SSGs, they give you instant TTFB and therefore better web core vitals scores from Google Lighthouse.

2.Build Time error catching

One of the biggest advantages of SSG is the ability to do build time error-catching. If you've ever used a CSS preprocessor, you'll be familiar with this concept: if you make a mistake in your code and try to compile it, you'll get an error. This is because these preprocessors are running on the client side and are not actually compiling anything.

3.Resilience

The advantages of static site generators (SSGs) over server-side rendering (SSR) are numerous, but there is one that stands out to me. It's about resilience. While a server-rendered site is dependent on the API, a static site generator doesn't care. If your API goes down, your page will still display the data that was fetched at build time.

For example, if you have an API like NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day API, and it goes down for some reason on Christmas or New Years Day or the Super Bowl or whatever day it is that you're having lots of traffic, your site will still work because it has all the data it needs in its own files and directories. The only thing that won't work is rehydration—your page won't be able to fetch new pictures after the initial render. But at least your users will still see something!

4. Simpler caching logic

First of all, with SSG simpler caching logic is possible. On my site, I cache the generated HTML on a CDN and only regenerate it when one of my markdown files changes. This allows me to have a very fast page load time even on mobile devices despite hosting the site on a small server with no CDN in front of it.

With SSG you can boost performance using CDN caching without a lot of extra configuration. If you have a static site with 50 pages, you can put them all on a CDN and serve them from there instead of your own server, which helps your page load time tremendously—but if you update one of those pages, you need to invalidate that cached version on your CDN. If every page has its own cache-control header, this is easy

5. Less infra complexity

SSG is a simpler architecture, with fewer moving parts. It’s also significantly less expensive to run, since you don’t have to pay for the redundant upkeep of multiple servers.

SSGs are also cloud agnostic. You can host them on any cloud provider you like and they will continue to work as advertised.

The security posture of having one less server to worry about is another big benefit. If an SSG is compromised, it only has access to its own data, not anything else on your network or in your environment.

Infinite scale is another major advantage. As long as you have enough capacity on your web server to handle the initial traffic spike, you can add more web servers without worrying about where to put them or how much bandwidth they will consume.

Conclusion

There is the lot of advantages why you should use SSG over SSR. And we see only few, there is a lot more.
But when we start developing a web-application, it's become clear that there is no universal solution. We need to make the decision depending on our project, and should always weigh all aspects carefully. Sometimes SSR will be more advantages than SSG !

item.title: 2022 年开发人员应该知道的 20 大 AngularJS 开发工具 | Top 20 AngularJS Development Tools That Developers Should Know In 2022
item.description:
西蒙丹尼尔斯

发表于如果您想构建功能强大且动态的应用程序同时考虑减少开发时间，请考虑使用 AngularJS 框架。

由于 HTML5 的简单架构具有广泛的直观功能和简单的语法，它已成为最受欢迎的框架之一。

开发人员可以轻松构建健壮且可管理的 Web 应用程序，因为该框架具有简化前端开发的潜力。

这里有一些工具。

为开发者构建应用程序的 20 个最佳和最受欢迎的 Angular 开发者工具

  1. 业力
  2. 量角器
  3. 网络风暴
  4. 茉莉花
  5. 角火
  6. 角度 IDE
  7. 摩卡.js
  8. 代码管弦乐队
  9. 角启动
  10. 崇高的文本
  11. 角种子
  12. 发电机角度
  13. 角甲板网格
  14. 视频
  15. 意思是
  16. 阿普塔纳工作室
  17. 角gettext

它是最好的 Angular 开发工具之一，可帮助开发人员在实时环境中轻松测试他们的代码。 在提供最佳测试环境方面，Karma 从来没有给开发人员留下深刻印象，并且最适合在平板电脑和手机等真实设备和浏览器上测试应用程序。

业力的特点

公司使用 Karma

它是 Google 创建的 AngularJS 开发工具之一，用于允许端到端测试并在浏览器上运行的 Web 应用程序。

它在真实软件中进行测试，并允许您的测试使用 Protractor 自动同步到页面。 它支持 Node.js、Mocha、Jasmine 和 Selenium。

量角器的特点

公司使用量角器

它是最好的 AngularJS 开发工具之一，被认为是一个聪明的代码编辑器，使其成为开发人员轻松工作的合适选择。

公司使用 Webstorm • 图形 • 技术堆栈 • 阿里巴巴游记

由于其简单、直观和简单的语法，开发人员可以轻松使用它并有效地执行测试。

该测试框架因其简单而干净的语法帮助开发人员有效地编写测试，因此成为开发人员难以置信的选择之一。

公司使用 Jasmine • 沃尔玛 • GitLab • 优衣库

其全面的 API 和三向数据绑定使其简化了开发过程。 此外，它的开发能力有助于轻松管理二进制数据，包括视频和图片。 它可以提供有效、简单和快速的开发，并附带用于标签检测和自动完成代码的 HTML 模板智能。

它有一个深刻的界面，可以直观地测试同步和异步代码。 它是 Angular 开发人员工具之一，可按顺序执行测试，以提供可定制且公平的报告，同时将未识别的问题映射到相关的测试用例。

公司使用 Mocha • Coursera • Asana

您可以通过为每个应用程序释放 DjAngular 来创建 AngularJS 内容以进行 AngularJs 开发。 它是一个可重用的应用程序，可实现更好的应用程序协调和灵活性。 它是一个具有命名空间 AngularJS 内容功能的可定制框架。

换句话说，当开发人员对程序进行一些更改时，它会自动更新程序。 这是通过使用使集成更容易的独特协议来实现的。

由于 Angular Kickstart 中包含可扩展的构建系统，它在开发阶段为开发人员简化了开发过程。 毫无疑问，由于其易于使用的界面，它已成为开发人员最好的 Angular 开发工具。

它拥有一个简单而干净的用户界面，具有多种功能，并提供卓越的性能。 它的效率、简单的编码能力和卓越的输出质量使 Sublime Text 成为 Web 开发的杰出 Angular 开发工具之一。

公司使用 Sublime Text • Lyft • CRED • Trivago • Hubspot • Fiverr

如果你比较其他 Angular 在线编辑器，这个工具的独特之处在于它的快速加载并且是一个非常轻量级的功能。

公司使用 jsfiddle • Mozilla 开发者网络 • Google Docs

它利用能够快速显示和识别每个范围的指令和控制器。

它还有助于提供开发环境以顺利运行应用程序。

它是一个 Angular 开发工具，可促进项目的设置并提供启动应用程序项目所需的所有样板。

它非常适合不需要视觉表示的代码或指令。

您可以使用 Videogular 集成视频支持，并将视频以及其他多媒体元素无缝结合到具有可定制前端的 Angular JS 应用程序中。

它是使用 Node.js、AngularJS、Mongo DB 和 Express 创建动态和强大应用程序的 Angular 开发人员工具之一。 公司使用 Mean • Fiverr • Sisense • Vungle • UNIQLO

它具有强大的 Git 功能，可提供更好的源代码管理。 使用该工具的好处是它支持 HTML、CSS、PHP、Ruby 和 JavaScript。

它包括一个具有简单调试功能的集成调试器和具有命令定制功能的特性，以及一个编程环境。

开发人员使用 Angular-gettext 将用自然语言编写的带注释的代码块动态转换为具有可理解指令的浏览器或框架。

总结 - 开发动态应用程序的最佳 AngularJS 工具

以上是能够简化 Web 应用程序开发并帮助构建卓越应用程序的顶级 Angular.JS 开发工具。

如果您想通过无缝特性和功能更快地开发您的应用程序，您的企业必须聘请精通 Web 开发的 AngularJS 开发人员。

在 Zenesys，我们拥有大量才华横溢且经验丰富的 AngularJS 开发人员，他们可以帮助您构建动态应用程序以简化您的项目目标。

头孢诺吉解决方案 - May 12 5月12日



原文:

simonedaniels

Posted on May 12

If you are someone who wants to build a powerful and dynamic application while considering less development time, consider an AngularJS framework.

AngularJS framework provides a diverse range of tools and functionality to make development work simple and easier.

It has become one of the most-loved frameworks due to the simple architecture of HTML5 with a wide range of intuitive features and a simple syntax.

Developers can easily build robust and manageable web applications, as this framework has the potential to simplify frontend development.

AngularJS offers a magnitude of development tools for web development. Here are some of the tools.

20 Best and most popular Angular developer tools to build apps for developers

  1. Karma
  2. Protractor
  3. Webstorm
  4. Jasmine
  5. Angular Fire
  6. Angular IDE
  7. Mocha.js
  8. DjAngular
  9. Code Orchestra
  10. Angular Kickstart
  11. Sublime Text
  12. jsfiddle
  13. NG-inspector
  14. Angular seed
  15. Generator Angular
  16. Angular Deckgrid
  17. Videogular
  18. Mean
  19. Aptana Studio
  20. Angular-gettext

1. Karma

It is one of the best Angular developer tools that help developers to test their code in a real-time environment without any hassles. Karma's intuitive on-ground testing' feature quickly uncovers potential flaws, defects, and glitches and helps developers ease the testing task easier.

When it comes to offering the best testing environment, Karma has never failed to impress the developers and is best suitable for testing apps on real devices and browsers like tablets and phones.

Features of Karma

• Real devices testing • Open Source • Easy Debugging

• Continuous Integration

Companies use Karma

• Accenture • GitLab

• Typeform

2. Protractor

It is one of the AngularJS dev tools created by Google and used for web applications that allow end-to-end testing and runs on browsers.

It conducts tests in real software and allows your tests to be automatically synchronized to the page utilizing Protractor. It supports Node.js, Mocha, Jasmine, and Selenium.

Features of Protractor

• Easy setup and installation • Support Data-Driven test • Auto-Synchronization

• Optimal speed

Companies use Protractor

• Gartner • IWB

• Prudential Financial

  1. Webstorm Webstorm is the best IDE for AngularJS development that includes an Angular online editor and is compatible with Node.js, HTML, and CSS, along with all current technologies. It is one of the finest AngularJS dev tools and is considered a clever code editor, making it a suitable choice for developers to work with ease.

Features of Webstorm • Smart editor • Advanced coding assistance • Unified UI • Tool integration

• Built-in debugger

Companies use Webstorm • Graphy • Tech Stack • Alibaba Travels

• Lyft

  1. Jasmine Jasmine, one of the AngularJS dev tools, is a behaviour-driven framework used to test web development. Due to its simple, intuitive, and easy syntax, it becomes easy for developers to use it and perform the testing efficiently.

Angular developer tools offered by Jasmine don't depend on the JavaScript network, DOM, or browsers. This testing framework helps developers effectively write tests due to its simple and clean syntax and hence become one of the incredible choices for developers.

Features of Jasmine • Unit-testing web apps • Open-source • Clean & easy syntax

• Straightforward API

Companies use Jasmine • Walmart • GitLab • UNIQLO

• Ocado Technology

  1. Angular Fire You can effectively create a backend for your Angular projects with Angular fire. Its comprehensive API and three-way data binding make it simplify the development process. In addition, its development capabilities help in easy management of binary data, including videos and pictures. AngularJS dev tools provided by Angular Fire help improve the developer experience, especially for novice developers.

Features of Angular Fire • Dependency injection • Real-time binding synchronization • Comprehensive API

• Three-way data binding

  1. Angular IDE Angular IDE is the best IDE for AngularJS development for both beginners and professionals and comes with smart syntax-aware source code coloring. It can provide effective, easy, and fast development and come along with the HTML template intelligence for tag detection and auto-completed codes.

Features of Angular IDE • Real-time code validation • Advanced settings with full-formatting • Supports Angular 7

• Auto-completion of code

  1. Mocha.js Mocha.js is a free JavaScript test framework that works in the browser and on Node.js. It has a profound interface that can intuitively test code for synchronous and asynchronous. It is one of the Angular developer tools to perform tests in a sequential order to provide customizable and fair reporting while mapping unidentified issues to their relevant test cases.

Features of Mocha • Browser support • Open-source

• Test coverage reporting

Companies use Mocha • Coursera • Asana

• Frontend Stack

  1. DjAngular These AngularJS dev tools help in improving AngularJS app integration. You can create AngularJS content by unleashing DjAngular per app for AngularJs development. It's a reusable application that enables better application reconciliation and flexibility. It is a customizable framework with a namespacing AngularJS content feature.

Features of DjAngular • Flexible framework • Better Integration

• Namespacing AngularJS content

  1. Code Orchestra Code orchestra is an Angularjs dev tool that lets programmers code in real-time, and it's also allowed them to make changes and save codes. In other words, when a developer makes some changes to the program, it automatically updates the program. This is accomplished by using a unique protocol that makes integration easier.

Features of Code Orchestra • Simple and clean code • Different platform testing simultaneously • Simple app architecture

• Support all platforms like Mac/Linux

  1. Angular Kickstart This makes it feasible to develop AngularJS applications quickly by harnessing angular developer tools. It streamlines the development process easier for developers during the development phase due to the scalable build system included in the Angular Kickstart. Undoubtedly, it has become the best angular development tool for developers due to its easy-to-use interface.

Features of Angular Kickstart • Consistent project structure • Optimized production release

• Ease the development process

  1. Sublime Text It is a sophisticated and best IDE for AngularJS development text editor for code and markup. It boasts a simple and clean user interface with a plethora of features and offers exceptional performance. Its efficiency, simple coding capabilities, and exceptional output quality make Sublime Text one of the prominent angular developer tools for web development.

Features of Sublime Text • Available for OS X, Windows, and Linux • Offer custom UT toolkit • Cross-platform editing

• Easy project switching

Companies use Sublime Text • Lyft • CRED • Trivago • Hubspot • Fiverr

• Stack

  1. jsfiddle Jsfiddle is an Angular online editor that comes with Angular developer tools that have the capability to make the coding easier for programmers. The notable feature of jsfiddle is that it allows you to experiment with CSS, Angular, JavaScript, and HTML code on a single screen. If you compare other Angular online editors, this tool differentiates itself with its quick loading and is an extremely light feature.

Features of jsfiddle • Remote debugging • Adjustable panel size • JavaScript or CoffeeScript

• Real-time collaboration

Companies use jsfiddle • Mozilla Developer Network • Google Docs

• BrowserStack

  1. NG-inspector NG-inspector is a Safari, Firefox, and chrome extension that allows the developers to develop, debug, and keenly understand the working of AngularJS applications by unleashing the inspector window.

It utilizes directives and controllers capable of displaying and identifying each scope quickly.

Features of NG-inspector • DOM highlighting • Real-time updating

• Controllers and directives

  1. Angular seed For a standard Angular JS application, the Angular seed serves as a skeletal structure or prototype that can bootstrap an Angular app project using the best IDE for AngularJS development. It also helps in offering the dev environment to run the applications smoothly.

Features of Angular seed • Support multiple AngularJS applications • Full docker support

• Pre-configured support

  1. Generator Angular Generator Angular is one of the best Angular developer tools available, allowing you to quickly commence an app development project by developing an editable wireframe with simple defaults.

It is an Angular Development tool that facilitates the setup of projects and provides all of the boilerplate required to start the application project.

Features of Generator Angular • Customize structure and paths • Simple to keep tracking settings

• Quick set up of a project

  1. Angular Deckgrid Angular Deckgrid is one of the Angular dev tools that are lightweight grids and is used for code/instructions where the directive being referenced is independent of its visual representation. It's excellent for codes or instructions that don't require visual representations.

Features of Angular Deckgrid • Lightweight framework

• CSS files for visuals

  1. Videogular It is an HTML5 video player that can manage all of your AngularJS video needs. You can use Videogular to integrate video support and combine videos as well as other multimedia elements seamlessly into the Angular JS app with a customizable frontend.

Features of Videogular • Supports IDEs • Customized Plugins

• Live media streaming

  1. Mean Mean is considered a 'boilerplate application' and is great for using multiple devices and platforms. It is one of the Angular developer tools to create dynamic and powerful apps utilizing Node.js, AngularJS, Mongo DB, and Express. Mean can leverage the custom-configured modules, including Passport and Mongoose, to speed up the development time.

Features of Mean • Open-source • Build dynamic apps and websites

• Pre-bundled modules

Companies use Mean • Fiverr • Sisense • Vungle • UNIQLO

• Accenture

  1. Aptana Studio Aptana Studio is the best IDE for AngularJS development tool, making it possible to build websites and web apps quickly. It has powerful Git features to offer better source code management. The benefit of using the tool is that it supports HTML, CSS, PHP, Ruby, and JavaScript.

It includes an integral debugger with easy debugging capabilities and features with command customization capabilities, and a programming environment.

Features of Aptana Studio • Debugger • IDE customization

• Deployment Wizard

  1. Angular-gettext Are you a novice developer and looking for a tool to ease the code translations in AngularJS? Angular-gettext is your go-to option offering excellent AngularJS developers tools. Developers use Angular-gettext to dynamically transform annotated code blocks written in natural language into a browser or a framework with understandable instructions.

Features of Angular-gettext • Seamless transactions • Effortless integration

• Rich Ecosystem

Wrapping up- Best AngularJS tools to develop dynamic apps

The above are the top Angular.JS development tools capable of simplifying web app development and helping in building remarkable apps.

If you want to develop your app faster with seamless features and functionalities, your business must hire an AngularJS developer proficient in web development.

At Zenesys, we have a large pool of talented and experienced AngularJS developers who helps you in building a dynamic app to streamline your project objectives.

Cefnogi Solutions - May 12

穆罕默德·阿里·切拉瓦拉 (MAC) - 古拉夫·辛格·拉瓦特—— May 3 5月3日



原文:

ngou loic

Posted on May 12

Speed Log(Speedest Logistics) is a mobile application that allows user to send parcels within a City in a few click only. Principal features are:

  • User registration and authentification

  • Sending parcel

  • Make an offer to carry a parcel

  • Track a parcel by Scanning the QrCode or by Entering the code manually.

The components of Appwrite We used to develop this application are:

  • AppWrite Authentification: User can create an account and register with email in our application.


  • Appwrite Database: Once the user is registered, We need more informations about the user, so we used this to save users informations, parcels, cities and quarters.

  • Appwrite Functions: We created a custom function to send a push notification to users when a new parcel is created, when a new offer for carrying parcel is made and when an offer has been accepted by the sender.


  • Appwrite Realtime: Once the user starts delivering a parcel, a background task launch to update the deliverer location continuously so the sender can track the realtime location using the map.

Submission Category:

Mobile Moguls

Link to Code

https://github.com/loicgeek/speed_log

Additional Resources / Info

We used firebase to send and receive push notifications in our application, ressources used are:

Custom domain:
We used our contabo VPS to install appwrite on server.

Additional screenshots
