苏拉夫

发表于什么是计费管理系统
计费系统是任何企业销售流程中最关键的部分。 建立一个强大的流线型计费系统需要付出很多努力和思考。 在新的现代世界中，计费系统或通常称为计费软件在帮助企业提高业务流程效率方面表现突出。

计费系统的含义 计费系统是软件和硬件的组合，旨在处理服务和产品的发票以及时间账单跟踪。 计费管理系统包括帮助为客户创建账单和发票的程序和流程。 计费系统允许离线和在线向客户发送账单和发票。 只需单击几下，它就可以帮助企业主以全面的方式跟踪多个发票和帐户。 出于以下原因，无论规模大小的企业都需要计费管理系统软件： 优化业务流程 跟踪销售和付款 生成无差错的账单和发票

有效管理现金

计费系统的显着特点 可定制

不同的业务对账单和发票的要求是不同的。 计费系统必须具有自定义发票的灵活性，以便它可以包含所有必需的详细信息和品牌标识。

数据备份
计费系统创建分类帐和金额的主数据。 它还创建离线或在线自动备份，以确保您的数据安全可靠。 计费管理系统还为纳税申报表和其他用途提供轻松的数据访问。

消费税合规
大多数业务类别都征收 GST，企业必须定期遵守 GST 申报和合规性。 计费系统具有实际简化电子发票和 GST 申报流程的功能。

报告
报告是业务经理的另一项重要任务。 无论如何，生成正确和及时的报告是非常重要的。 计费系统有助于生成多个报告。 这有助于企业主及时做出明智的决定。 计费系统的报告功能可以节省准备报告的时间和成本，并提供更好的分析。

付款
如今的计费系统还包括链接和二维码，用于通过多种支付选项促进在线支付。 计费系统还提供自动过帐和简单的交易对帐功能。 计费管理系统与在线银行集成在一起，可以轻松地通过软件进行银行交易。 任何工作都不需要去银行。

企业为什么要使用计费系统？

快速发票
由于现成的格式和客户数据，使用计费软件比手动账单更快地创建账单和发票。 用户还可以根据要求轻松定制账单/发票。 这有助于节省时间和计费成本。 此外，计费系统还提供多种语言的计费功能。 以当地语言生成账单给客户一种个性化的感觉。

安全
部署计费系统后，可以根据要求固定用户访问权限和角色。 这有助于防止数据的误用或泄漏。 这也确保了计费系统的透明度。

减少文书工作
计费系统直接减少了文书工作。 这对您的业务和地球都有好处。 由于自动化，大多数过程都是自动完成的。 计费系统使在系统中保存记录变得容易。 没有更多的文件堆。 这对会计和报告准备有很大帮助。

轻松付款
借助在线发票，付款流程变得更加高效和透明。 计费系统还向客户发送自动通知、电子邮件和提醒以解决未付款项。 这也有助于在不付出额外努力的情况下获得报酬。 计费系统还维护所有付款和交易的记录。

如何选择和维护计费系统？
选择计费系统取决于每个业务的要求。 小型企业可能有不同的需求，大型企业可能有不同的需求。 根据需要，必须选择计费系统。 还应该记住，计费系统应该足够灵活，可以随着业务的增长而增长。

企业主必须确保计费系统由以下关键组件组成： 产品和服务主 条形码扫描 轻松快速的计费 GST 合规性 数据备份 在线/离线 安全和安全 应收账款 应付账款管理 付款处理

库存管理

它与移动应用程序集成，并带有成熟的库存管理系统。 这种计费管理系统最有利于希望快速发展业务的中小型企业。 计费系统可以通过提高生产力和效率来极大地影响所有类型的企业。 投资计费系统可以在短期和长期内提供利润，因为计费和发票软件是企业最重要的部分。

尼克·泰勒（他/他）—— May 12 5月12日

Ivad Yves HABIMANA - Ivad Yves HABIMANA - May 11 5月11日



原文:

Sourav Seo

Posted on May 12

What is Billing Management System
The billing system is the most crucial part of the sales process in any business. A lot of efforts & thoughts go into setting up a powerful streamlined billing system. In the new modern world, the billing system or commonly referred to as billing software has been prominent in helping businesses to improve their efficiency in business processes.

Meaning of billing system A billing system is a combination of software & hardware designed to handle invoicing for services & products as well as time bill tracking. Billing management system includes procedures and processes that help create bills and invoices for customers. Billing systems allow sending bills and invoices to the customers offline and online. It helps the business owner's in a comprehensive manner to keep a track of multiple invoice and accounts just by few clicks. Businesses irrespective of size need to have billing management system software for the following reasons: Optimization of business processes To keep track of sales and payments To generate error-free bills and invoices

To manage cash effectively

Salient features of billing system Customizable

Requirement of billing & invoices is different for different businesses. Billing system must have the flexibility to customize invoices so that it could include all the required details & brand identity.

Data Backup
Billing system creates the master data of ledger & amounts. It also creates automatic backup offline or online to keep your data safe & secure. Billing management systems also provide easy data access for tax returns & other usages.

GST Compliance
GST is levied on most of the business category & the businesses have to follow GST filing and compliances regularly. Billing system is overloaded with the feature that actually simplifies the e-invoicing & GST return filing process.

Reporting
Reporting is another crucial task for the business managers. No matter what, generating correct & timely reports is very crucial. Billing systems help generate multiple reports. This helps the business owners to take timely informed decisions. Reporting feature of the billing system can save time & cost of preparing reports & give better analysis.

Payments
Billing systems nowadays also include links & QR codes for facilitating online payments via multiple payment options. Billing system also has the provision of auto-posting & easy reconciliation of transactions. Billing management systems are integrated with online banking that makes it easy to do banking transactions from the software. There is no need to visit the banks for any work.

Why should businesses use billing system?

Quick invoices
Due to ready formats & customer data, bills & invoices are created faster using billing software than manual bills. The user can also customize bills/invoices based on requirements with minimal effort. This helps in saving time & cost of billing. In addition, the billing system also has the provision of billing in multiple languages. Generating bills in local languages give a personalized touch to the customers.

Security
Once the billing system is deployed, user access & roles can be fixed as per the requirement. This helps prevent misuse or leakage of data. This also ensures transparency in the billing system.

Reduce paperwork
Billing system directly reduces the paperwork. It is good for your business as well as good for the planet. Due to automation, most of the processes are done automatically. Billing system makes it easy to keep records in the system. No more heaps of files. This helps immensely in accounting & reports preparation.

Easy payments
The payment process is made more efficient & transparent with the help of online invoices. Billing system also sends automated notifications, emails & reminders to customers for outstandings. This also helps in getting paid without extra effort. Billing system also maintains records of all payments & transactions.

How to choose & maintain a billing system?
Choosing a billing system depends on the requirements of each business. A small business may have different requirement, a large business might have varied needs. Depending on the needs, a billing system must be chosen. It should also be kept in mind that the billing system should be flexible enough to grow with the business.

Business owners must ensure the billing system consists of the following key components: Product and service master Barcode scanning Easy & fast billing GST compliance Data backup Online/ offline Security and safety Accounts receivable Accounts payable management Payment processing

Stock management

Marg ERP is a billing system that allows business owners to select standard plans or customize based on their requirements. It is integrated with mobile apps & comes with a full-fledged inventory management system. This kind of billing management system is most beneficial for small to mid-sized businesses who want to grow their business with speed. Billing systems can immensely impact all types of businesses by improving productivity & efficiency. Investing in a billing system can provide profits in the short and long run because billing and invoicing software is the most important part of a business.

Nick Taylor (he/him) - May 12

Ivad Yves HABIMANA - May 11

路易斯·古斯塔沃·卡西亚托里

发表于今天快速学习，当您需要检查特定模块中的公共功能时，请使用帮助程序exports/1 ，这里有一个示例：

 iex ( 1 ) > exports Enum all? / 1 all? / 2 any? / 1 any? / 2 at / 2 at / 3 chunk / 2 chunk / 3 chunk / 4 chunk_by / 2 chunk_every / 2 chunk_every / 3 chunk_every / 4 chunk_while / 4 concat / 1 concat / 2 count / 1 count / 2 count_until / 2 count_until / 3 dedup / 1 dedup_by / 2 drop / 2 drop_every / 2 drop_while / 2 each / 2 empty? / 1 fetch / 2 fetch! / 2 filter / 2 filter_map / 3 find / 2 find / 3 find_index / 2 find_value / 2 find_value / 3 flat_map / 2 flat_map_reduce / 3 frequencies / 1 frequencies_by / 2 group_by / 2 group_by / 3 intersperse / 2 into / 2 into / 3 join / 1 join / 2 map / 2 map_every / 3 map_intersperse / 3 map_join / 2 map_join / 3 map_reduce / 3 max / 1 max / 2 max / 3 max_by / 2 max_by / 3 max_by / 4 member? / 2 min / 1 min / 2 min / 3 min_by / 2 min_by / 3 min_by / 4 min_max / 1 min_max / 2 min_max_by / 2 min_max_by / 3 min_max_by / 4 partition / 2 product / 1 random / 1 reduce / 2 reduce / 3 reduce_while / 3 reject / 2 reverse / 1 reverse / 2 reverse_slice / 3 scan / 2 scan / 3 shuffle / 1 slice / 2 slice / 3 slide / 3 sort / 1 sort / 2 sort_by / 2 sort_by / 3 split / 2 split_while / 2 split_with / 2 sum / 1 take / 2 take_every / 2 take_random / 2 take_while / 2 to_list / 1 uniq / 1 uniq / 2 uniq_by / 2 unzip / 1 with_index / 1 with_index / 2 zip / 1 zip / 2 zip_reduce / 3 zip_reduce / 4 zip_with / 2 zip_with / 3

也适用于 Erlang 模块：

 iex ( 2 ) > exports :io nl / 1 columns / 0 columns / 1 rows / 1 get_chars / 2 get_chars / 3 get_password / 0 get_password / 1 setopts / 1 write / 1 write / 2 read / 1 read / 2 read / 4 fwrite / 1 fwrite / 3 fread / 2 fread / 3 scan_erl_exprs / 1 scan_erl_exprs / 2 scan_erl_exprs / 3 scan_erl_exprs / 4 scan_erl_form / 1 scan_erl_form / 2 scan_erl_form / 4 parse_erl_exprs / 1 parse_erl_exprs / 2 parse_erl_exprs / 4 parse_erl_form / 1 parse_erl_form / 2 parse_erl_form / 3 parse_erl_form / 4 request / 1 requests / 1 requests / 2 scan_erl_form / 3 fwrite / 2 getopts / 1 nl / 0 rows / 0 put_chars / 2 setopts / 2 get_line / 2 parse_erl_exprs / 3 read / 3 request / 2 getopts / 0 get_line / 1 put_chars / 1 format / 1 format / 2 format / 3 printable_range / 0

伊丽莎白 Werdnik - Feb 24 2月24日



原文:

Luis Gustavo Caciatori

Posted on May 12

Quick learn for today, when you need to check the public functions from a specific module use the helper exports/1, here an example:

iex(1)> exports Enum
all?/1 all?/2 any?/1 any?/2
at/2 at/3 chunk/2 chunk/3
chunk/4 chunk_by/2 chunk_every/2 chunk_every/3
chunk_every/4 chunk_while/4 concat/1 concat/2
count/1 count/2 count_until/2 count_until/3
dedup/1 dedup_by/2 drop/2 drop_every/2
drop_while/2 each/2 empty?/1 fetch/2
fetch!/2 filter/2 filter_map/3 find/2
find/3 find_index/2 find_value/2 find_value/3
flat_map/2 flat_map_reduce/3 frequencies/1 frequencies_by/2
group_by/2 group_by/3 intersperse/2 into/2
into/3 join/1 join/2 map/2
map_every/3 map_intersperse/3 map_join/2 map_join/3
map_reduce/3 max/1 max/2 max/3
max_by/2 max_by/3 max_by/4 member?/2
min/1 min/2 min/3 min_by/2
min_by/3 min_by/4 min_max/1 min_max/2
min_max_by/2 min_max_by/3 min_max_by/4 partition/2
product/1 random/1 reduce/2 reduce/3
reduce_while/3 reject/2 reverse/1 reverse/2
reverse_slice/3 scan/2 scan/3 shuffle/1
slice/2 slice/3 slide/3 sort/1
sort/2 sort_by/2 sort_by/3 split/2
split_while/2 split_with/2 sum/1 take/2
take_every/2 take_random/2 take_while/2 to_list/1
uniq/1 uniq/2 uniq_by/2 unzip/1
with_index/1 with_index/2 zip/1 zip/2
zip_reduce/3 zip_reduce/4 zip_with/2 zip_with/3

Also works with Erlang modules:

iex(2)> exports :io
nl/1 columns/0 columns/1 rows/1
get_chars/2 get_chars/3 get_password/0 get_password/1
setopts/1 write/1 write/2 read/1
read/2 read/4 fwrite/1 fwrite/3
fread/2 fread/3 scan_erl_exprs/1 scan_erl_exprs/2
scan_erl_exprs/3 scan_erl_exprs/4 scan_erl_form/1 scan_erl_form/2
scan_erl_form/4 parse_erl_exprs/1 parse_erl_exprs/2 parse_erl_exprs/4
parse_erl_form/1 parse_erl_form/2 parse_erl_form/3 parse_erl_form/4
request/1 requests/1 requests/2 scan_erl_form/3
fwrite/2 getopts/1 nl/0 rows/0
put_chars/2 setopts/2 get_line/2 parse_erl_exprs/3
read/3 request/2 getopts/0 get_line/1
put_chars/1 format/1 format/2 format/3
printable_range/0

item.title: 教程上传文件/照片 Ke Firebase Pada React Native | Tutorial Upload File/Photo Ke Firebase Pada React Native
item.description:
普图埃卡穆利亚纳

发表于安装firebase pada projek kamu

阿陶

查看按钮

<TouchableOpacity style={styles.btnUpload}> <Text style={styles.btnLabel}>Upload Photo</Text> </TouchableOpacity>

安装图像选择器
阿陶

yarn add react-native-image-picker

导入react-native-image- picker pada 文件 screen kamu, misalkan root/src/screens/UploadPhoto.js

 import {launchImageLibrary} from 'react-native-image-picker';

安装 react-native-uuid

阿陶

yarn add react-native-uuid

导入pada文件screen kamu

 import uuid from 'react-native-uuid';

导入 firebase jangan lupa 导入 firebase juga 

 import React from "react"; import { StyleSheet, Text, TouchableOpacity } from "react-native"; import uuid from "react-native-uuid"; import { launchImageLibrary } from "react-native-image-picker"; import { getStorage, ref, uploadBytes, } from "firebase/storage"; import firebase from "../config/Firebase";

赛勒赛🚀😎



原文:

putu eka mulyana

Posted on May 12

install firebase pada projek kamu

atau

Tambahkan Firebase configuration pada projeck kamu
saya menyimpan Firebase configuration pada root/src/config/Firebase.js isi file Firebase configuration seperti ini :

import { initializeApp } from "firebase/app";

const firebaseConfig = {
  apiKey: "xxxxx",
  authDomain: "xxxxx",
  databaseURL: "xxxxxxx",
  projectId: "xxxxxxx",
  storageBucket: "xxxxxxx",
  messagingSenderId: "xxxxxxx",
  appId: "xxxxxxx",
  measurementId: "xxxxxxx"
};

// Initialize Firebase
const app = initializeApp(firebaseConfig);

export default app;

view button

<TouchableOpacity style={styles.btnUpload}>
    <Text style={styles.btnLabel}>Upload Photo</Text>
</TouchableOpacity>

install image picker
saya menggunakan library react-native-image-picker

npm i react-native-image-picker

atau

yarn add react-native-image-picker

import react-native-image-picker pada file screen kamu, misalkan root/src/screens/UploadPhoto.js

import {launchImageLibrary} from 'react-native-image-picker';

install react-native-uuid

atau

yarn add react-native-uuid

import pada file screen kamu

import uuid from 'react-native-uuid';

import firebase jangan lupa import firebase juga

import React from "react";
import {
  StyleSheet,
  Text,
  TouchableOpacity
} from "react-native";

import uuid from "react-native-uuid";
import { launchImageLibrary } from "react-native-image-picker";
import {
  getStorage,
  ref,
  uploadBytes,
} from "firebase/storage";

import firebase from "../config/Firebase";

Buat function upload photo

const uploadPhoto= async () =>{
    await launchImageLibrary({}, async (response) => {
        if (response.didCancel || response.error) {
            Alert.alert("oops, batal memilih foto.");
        } else {
            let uri = response.assets[0].uri;
            let fileName = response.assets[0].fileName;

            let storage = await getStorage(firebase);

            const rersult = await fetch(uri);
            const blob = await rersult.blob();

            uploadBytes(ref(storage, fileName), blob, {
            contentType: "image/jpeg",
            firebaseStorageDownloadTokens: uuid.v4(),
            }).then(() => {
            console.log("Upload With blob");
            });
        }
    });
}

tambahkan onPress pada button

<TouchableOpacity style={styles.btnUpload} onPress={()=> uploadPhoto()}>
    <Text style={styles.btnLabel}>Upload Photo</Text>
</TouchableOpacity>

Selesai 🚀😎
item.author: putuekamulyana

item.title: 欢迎加入俱乐部：Solana、Terra、Arbitrum 加入 GetBlock 的生态系统 | Welcome to the club: Solana, Terra, Arbitrum joined GetBlock’s ecosystem
item.description:
顶级区块链节点提供商GetBlock的团队很高兴地庆祝其产品堆栈最近几个月最令人印象深刻的新增功能。 在 2022 年第一季度，许多主流智能合约平台被添加到其工具包中，包括 Solana、Terra 和 Arbitrum 等。

对于所有三个新人，我们的团队推出了共享和专用节点； 可以通过 JSON RPC、REST API 和 WebSockets 方法访问区块链。 通过添加这些解决方案，GetBlock 在构建 Web3 的多网络、去中心化未来的道路上实现了一个重要的里程碑。 将不同的区块链集成到一个堆栈中后，它​​能够为其新老客户提供具有无与伦比的灵活性和互操作性的 Web3 解决方案。
根据团队的估计，它的峰值带宽超过了 70,000 TPS。

其总 TVL 跃升 1,000%，而 LUNA 价格飙升 100 倍。 在很大程度上，这种受欢迎程度应归功于其旗舰 DeFi 锚协议 (ANC) 在 TerraUSD (UST) 质押上提供的惊人 APY。

它通过 Optimistic Rollups 机制发送“压缩”信息，从而减轻以太坊（ETH）主网的交易压力。 它的激活使以太坊（ETH）的吞吐量提高了 300 倍，而 Arbitrum 的交易费用仍然是业内最低的。

此外，GetBlock 为所有类型的去中心化系统创建节点集群和定制的资源管理器。



原文:

Team of GetBlock, a top-tier blockchain nodes provider, is thrilled to celebrate the most impressive addition to its offerings stack in recent months. Over Q1, 2022, a number of mainstream smart contracts platforms was added to its toolkit including the likes of Solana, Terra, and Arbitrum.

Solana (SOL) and Terra (LUNA) are two largest smart contract environments outside of the Ethereum (ETH) scene. Arbitrum is a low-cost Ethereum-based L2 scalability solution based on Optimistic Rollups.

For all three newcomers, our team rolled out shared and dedicated nodes; the blockchains can be accessed through JSON RPC, REST API and WebSockets methods. All APIs for Solana, Terra, and Arbitrum are now available with 98-99 percent uptime.

By adding these solutions, GetBlock accomplishes a crucial milestone on its way to building a multi-network, decentralized future of Web3. Having integrated different blockchains into one stack, it is able to offer its new and existing clients Web3 solutions with unmatched levels of flexibility and interoperability.
GetBlock offers one-stop solution for all top-tier DeFi platforms

Invented by Anatoly Yakovenko in 2017, Solana (SOL), with its unique Proof-of-History (PoH) consensus promotes itself as a most eccentric and powerful Ethereum (ETH) killer. Its peak bandwidth eclipses 70,000 TPS, as per the team’s estimations.

Terra (LUNA) gained huge popularity in 2021; its aggregated TVL jumped by 1,000 per cent while LUNA price rocketed 100x. Largely, this popularity should be attributed to the amazing APY offered by its flagship DeFi Anchor Protocol (ANC) on TerraUSD (UST) staking.

Arbitrum is a second-layer solution for Ethereum (ETH) scalability. It reduces the transactional pressure on Ethereum (ETH) mainnet by sending ‘compressed’ information through Optimistic Rollups mechanism. Its activation gave Ethereum (ETH) throughput a 300x boost whilst transactional fees in Arbitrum remain among the lowest in the industry.

GetBlock offers reliable and resource-efficient connection to shared and dedicated nodes of 40+ blockchains. Also, GetBlock creates node clusters and customized explorers for decentralized systems of all types.
item.author: janelanglay

item.title: Shopify 商店开发权威指南 | The Definitive Guide to Shopify Store Development
item.description:
信不信由你，如今的顾客都喜欢在网上购物。 根据美国商务部的数据，2021 年美国电子商务增长了14.2% 。 未来四年，这一数字预计将增长 50%，在全球范围内达到近 7.4 万亿美元。 如果您还想在蓬勃发展的电子商务市场中分一杯羹，则必须从实体店转向电子商务世界。

涉足电子商务领域并不像听起来那么容易。 在您考虑建立在线商店的那一刻，您将被众多电子商务商店开发平台所淹没。 由于许多电子商务平台提供简单的可扩展性、转换优化、客户参与和保留、全渠道集成和长期增长，因此从众多选项中选择正确的一个可能是一个难以破解的难题，尤其是对于那些刚接触电子商务的世界。 如果您还在努力寻找最适合您的业务模式和目标的正确解决方案，为什么不考虑Shopify 电子商务开发。 继续阅读直到最后，找出值得尝试使用 Shopify 进行 eStore 开发的重要原因。

什么是 Shopify？

十多年来，Shopify 已经从 5 人发展到全球 5,000 多人。 根据 Shopify 官方网站上的数据，这个专有的在线商店开发平台已经产生了超过 2000 亿美元的销售额。

据BuiltWith 称，有超过 500 万个站点由 Shopify 平台提供支持，其中 2.63% 位于前 100 万个站点中。 一些利用 Shopify 平台潜力的领先品牌包括 Kylie Cosmetics、 Netflix 、Tesla、Gymshark、Fitbit、Red Bull、KKW Beauty & KKW Fragrance、Steve Madden、百威、纽约时报、经济学人，以及举几个例子。

考虑 Shopify 进行 eStore 商店开发的 3 个主要原因

与其他电子商务商店开发平台不同，Shopify 允许商家以低于电影票的价格开始在线销售。 是的，你没听错。 使用Shopify Lite ，您每月只需 9 美元即可获得在线销售所需的一切。 选择 Shopify 进行电子商务商店开发更有趣的是，它允许商家进行 14 天的免费试用，而无需信用卡详细信息。 如果您发现该平台很好地满足了您的业务需求，您可以升级到根据您的需求量身定制的付费计划。 除了 Shopify Lite，Shopify 还提供其他四种受欢迎的计划，即： • Basic - 每月 29 美元起，非常适合新的电子商务企业。 建立在线商店可能需要几周到几个月的时间，因为开发人员需要根据独特的业务需求从头开始编写代码。 另一方面，如果您选择 Shopify 作为您的在线商店开发平台，您将获得一个拖放式 UI，可促进更快的 eStore 开发。 注册后几分钟内，商家就可以开始在平台上列出他们的产品，并在同一天推出他们的电子商务商店。 此外，Shopify 允许商家运营在线商店，而无需担心托管和高昂的开发成本。

今天，大多数用户通过移动设备访问互联网。 根据Statista的一份报告，83% 的美国人口通过移动设备访问互联网。 通过 Shopify 商店开发，您将获得一个针对移动设备进行优化的在线商店，该商店可以根据用户的屏幕尺寸快速调整自身。 换句话说，Shopify 允许商家建立一个移动响应式在线商店，使客户能够轻松地浏览您的商店，找到他们正在寻找的产品，并直接通过他们的手机方便地完成结帐。

由于 Shopify 是一个专有的电子商务商店开发平台，它为商家提供有限的定制。 与开源电子商务平台不同，您无法使用 Shopify 以您想要的方式自定义您的在线商店。

将现有商店从其他平台迁移到 Shopify 或从 Shopify 迁移到其他电子商务平台可能非常具有挑战性。 在迁移过程中，您最终可能还会丢失一些关键信息。

包起来

随着电子商务的兴起，全球对 Shopify 网站开发人员的需求正在激增。 如果您还想利用 Shopify 平台提供的众多好处，建议您从可靠的技术合作伙伴那里聘请经过认证的专业人员。 通过与合适的Shopify 商店开发公司合作，您将获得一个针对您的独特业务需求量身定制的强大且可扩展的 eStore。 这篇富有洞察力且详细的文章涵盖了使用 Shopify 进行电子商务的优缺点，以便您在为在线商店选择平台时做出明智的业务决策。 希望，您现在可以轻松开始您的电子商务之旅。



原文:

Believe it or not, customers love to shop online these days. In 2021, US eCommerce grew by 14.2% according to the U.S. Department of Commerce figures. Another report by Statista indicates that the retail eCommerce sales in 2021 amounted to 4.938 trillion USD. In the next four years, this figure is predicted to increase by 50%, reaching nearly 7.4 trillion U.S. dollars worldwide. If you also want to grab your share of the pie in the burgeoning eCommerce market, it becomes imperative to make a shift from a brick-and-mortar store to the world of eCommerce.

Making a foray into the eCommerce realm is not as easy as it sounds. The moment you think of building an online store, you’ll be inundated with a multitude of eCommerce store development platforms. Since many eCommerce platforms offer easy scalability, conversion optimization, customer engagement and retention, omnichannel integration, and long-term growth, choosing the right one from a plethora of options can be a hard nut to crack, particularly for those who are new to the world of online commerce. If you are also struggling to find the right solution that caters best to your business model and objectives, why not consider Shopify eCommerce development. Continue reading till the end to find out the strong reasons why it is worth giving Shopify a try for your eStore development.

What is Shopify?

Launched back in 2006, Shopify is a popular proprietary eCommerce store development platform that powers millions of businesses in around 175 countries across the globe. Over a decade, Shopify has come a long way from 5 people to more than 5,000 globally. As per the data present on the Shopify official website, this proprietary online store development platform has generated more than $200 billion USD in sales.

According to BuiltWith, there are over 5 million sites that are powered by the Shopify platform, and out of them, 2.63% are in the top 1 million sites. Some of the leading brands that are leveraging the potential of the Shopify platform include Kylie Cosmetics, Netflix, Tesla, Gymshark, Fitbit, Red Bull, KKW Beauty & KKW Fragrance, Steve Madden, Budweiser, The New York Times, The Economist, and to name a few.

3 Prime Reasons to Consider Shopify for eStore Store Development

1) Reasonably Priced

Unlike other eCommerce store development platforms, Shopify allows merchants to start selling online for less than the price of a movie ticket. Yes, you heard absolutely right. With Shopify Lite, you get everything you need to sell online for just 9 US dollars per month. What’s more interesting about choosing Shopify for eCommerce store development is that it allows merchants to run a 14-day free trial, with no credit card details required. If you find that the platform is catering to your business requirements well, you can upgrade to the paid plans tailored to your requirements. Besides Shopify Lite, Shopify also offers four other popular plans namely: • Basic - It starts with 29 USD per month and is ideal for new eCommerce businesses. • Shopify - It starts with 79 USD per month and is ideal for expanding businesses having consistent sales. • Advanced - It starts with 299 USD per month and is ideal for well-established businesses.

• Shopify Plus - It starts with 2000 USD per month and is ideal for businesses having a higher volume of online sales.

2) Easy to Setup and Use

Setting up an online store may take from a couple of weeks to months since developers need to code from scratch tailored to unique business requirements. On the other hand, if you choose Shopify as your online store development platform, you’ll get a drag-and-drop UI that facilitates faster eStore development. Within minutes after signing up, merchants can start listing their products on the platform and launch their eCommerce store the same day. Furthermore, Shopify allows merchants to run an online store without the fuss over hosting, and high development costs.

3) Mobile Commerce Ready

Today, a majority of users access the internet through mobile devices. According to a report by Statista, 83% of the U.S. population access internet through mobile devices. In 2021, the number of mobile internet users in the United States alone stood at 276.8 million people and this figure is predicted to cross 300 million by 2026. Furthermore, retail m-commerce sales in the United States are expected to reach 710 billion U.S. dollars by 2025. Considering this massive growth in sales through mobile devices, it becomes imperative for merchants to have an online store that is mobile commerce ready.

With Shopify store development, you’ll get a mobile mobile-optimized online store that quickly adapts itself based on the users’ screen size. In other words, Shopify allows merchants to build a mobile responsive online store that enables customers to easily navigate through your store, find the products they are looking for, and conveniently complete checkout right from their mobile phones.

*Cons of Shopify for eCommerce Store Development *

1) Limited Customization

Since Shopify is a proprietary eCommerce store development platform, it offers limited customization to merchants. Unlike open-source eCommerce platforms, you can’t customize your online store the way you want with Shopify.

2) Migration Difficulty

Migrating your existing store from other platforms to Shopify or moving away from Shopify to other eCommerce platforms can be quite challenging. You may also end up losing some of your critical information during the migration process.

Wrapping Up

With the rise of eCommerce, the demand for Shopify website developers is surging across the globe. If you also want to harness the host of benefits offered by the Shopify platform, it is recommended to hire certified professionals from a reliable tech partner. By associating with the right Shopify store development company, you’ll get a robust and scalable eStore tailored to your unique business requirements. This insightful yet detailed piece covers both the pros and cons of using Shopify for eCommerce so that you can make an informed business decision when choosing a platform for your online store. Hope, you can now begin your eCommerce journey with ease.
item.author: elenamia

item.title: 完整的现代 React 开发人员 2022 | The Complete Modern React Developer 2022
item.description:
安德鲁·拜斯登

发表于这将是一门在 2022 年成为完整的现代 React 开发人员的课程。本课程中唯一未涉及的三个主题是Redux 、 GraphQL和React Native ，它们可能会在未来的课程中涉及。 我们将构建一个超级基本的 Twitter 克隆，它具有用于发布、阅读和删除推文的 CRUD 功能。

本课程将为您提供成为全栈软件开发人员的技能和知识。 在本课程中，您将学习：

先决条件

确保您已设置好开发环境并安装介绍中列出的所有工具/库。 我在 Mac 上工作，所以我提到的一些工具仅适用于 macOS，但是如果您使用 Windows 或 Linux，您应该能够找到替代品并能够跟进。

您将需要安装以下工具来使用 MongoDB NoSQL 数据库。 蒙戈什

您将需要安装以下工具来使用 PostgreSQL 数据库。 它是 mongosh 的 PostgreSQL 等价物。

在使用 GUI 处理 PostgreSQL 数据库时，我更喜欢 Valentina Studio。 这是一个很棒的工具，因为它甚至可以连接到 MongoDB 和 MySQL 数据库。 虽然有像PgAdmin这样的替代品，所以只要使用你觉得舒服的任何东西。

瓦伦蒂娜工作室

设置后端

在本节中，您将学习如何使用 Express.js 和 Nest.js 设置 Node 后端。 这两个框架都将使用不同的端点连接到 MongoDB 和 PostgreSQL 数据库。 作为奖励，当我们将 MongoDB 和 PostgreSQL 数据库放入 Docker 容器中时，您还将学习一些 DevOps。

所以基本上你可以在 Docker 容器中拥有一个数据库，任何外部应用程序都可以连接到它。 使用这种类型的设置，您甚至不需要在本地计算机上安装或设置数据库。 你可以让所有东西都在 Docker 容器中运行，并且这个设置将在任何人的机器上运行完全相同。

我认为这是本地安装的一个很好的替代方案，并且有了这些知识，它为您提供了与数据库交互的另一种选择。 此工作流程不需要大量设置，您可以使用 GUI 或命令行与 Docker 容器内的数据库进行交互，就像在本地或在线时一样。

本地数据库设置

打开您的命令行工具，我将使用Hyper并运行以下命令以连接到您的本地 MongoDB 安装。

首先运行此命令，它将显示您正在使用的数据库。 它应该返回默认数据库test 。

现在运行下面的命令，它将显示您当前创建了哪些数据库。

接下来运行命令以创建一个名为twitter的数据库。

最后使用下面的命令创建一个集合，当你使用命令show dbs; 再次在命令行中，您应该会看到您创建的名为twitter的数据库。

 db.createCollection ( 'contents' ) ;

最后，我们将添加一些起始数据副本并将下面的代码粘贴到您的命令行中。 如果您在插入数据后在命令行中运行此命令db.contents.find().pretty() ，那么您将能够看到表中的数据。

 db.contents.insertMany ([ { tweet: "Hello World!" , img: "" } , { tweet: "Content creation and programming are basically full time jobs. I have enough projects and work to keep me busy for years. Working in tech is definitely going to entertain you for a long time which is why so many people want to transition into this field." , img: "" } , { tweet: "JavaScript developers are forever in high demand" , img: "" } ])

如果您在计算机上打开 MongoDB Compass 应用程序并使用连接字符串mongodb://localhost:27017连接到本地安装，那么您应该能够看到所有数据库，包括我们刚刚在 GUI 中创建的数据库。 因此，现在您已设置为使用命令行或 GUI 与数据库进行交互。

在您执行任何操作之前，请检查您的 PostgreSQL 数据库是否在本地运行。 如果它没有运行，那么当您运行命令pgcli时，您可能会在命令行中遇到错误。 在 macOS 上，我将使用 Postgres.app，所以让它在你的机器上运行，然后它应该会出现在你操作系统顶部的菜单栏中。

现在转到命令行并运行以下命令以连接到本地 PostgreSQL 安装。

运行以下命令将显示所有 PostgreSQL 数据库。

将下面的 SQL 查询复制并粘贴到您的 pgcli 命令行窗口中，以创建一个名为twitter的数据库。 现在，如果您在同一个窗口中再次运行命令\l ，您应该会看到所有数据库，包括我们刚刚创建的数据库。

接下来我们需要在同一个窗口中连接到数据库，所以使用下面的命令来做到这一点。

接下来我们必须创建一个表并添加一些将进入数据库twitter的数据。 将下面的 SQL 代码复制并粘贴到您的命令行窗口中。

 CREATE TABLE contents ( id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid (), tweet VARCHAR ( 280 ) NOT NULL , img VARCHAR ( 500 ) NOT NULL ); INSERT INTO contents ( tweet , img ) VALUES ( 'Hello World!' , '' ), ( 'Content creation and programming are basically full time jobs. I have enough projects and work to keep me busy for years. Working in tech is definitely going to entertain you for a long time which is why so many people want to transition into this field.' , '' ), ( 'JavaScript developers are forever in high demand' , '' );

如果您在计算机上打开 Postgres.app 应用程序，您应该会看到所有数据库，包括我们刚刚创建的数据库。

如果您连接到 Valentina Studio 或您选择的数据库 GUI，您应该能够看到您创建的数据库。

瓦伦蒂娜工作室

在您的计算机上启动 Docker 应用程序，并针对每个数据库执行以下步骤。 首先在你的本地机器上创建一个名为complete-react-developer的文件夹
然后cd进入使用命令行。

仔细检查您是否位于complete-react-developer的根文件夹中，然后运行以下命令来设置项目。

 mkdir docker-twitter-mongodb cd docker-twitter-mongodb touch docker-compose.yml

在代码编辑器中打开该文件夹并将以下代码添加到docker-compose.yml文件中。

docker-compose.yml

如果缩进不正确，请注意 yaml 代码格式，它会给您带来错误。

 version : ' 3.9' services : mongo_db : container_name : db_container image : ' mongo:latest' restart : always ports : - ' 2717:27017' volumes : - ' mongo_db:/data/db' volumes : mongo_db : {}

现在运行以下代码以使用 MongoDB 数据库启动 docker 容器。

假设一切正常，您应该在 Docker 容器中运行 MongoDB 数据库。

连接到 Docker 容器内的 MongoDB 数据库

现在可以同时连接到本地 MongoDB 数据库和 MongoDB Docker 数据库，因为它们都配置为在不同的端口上运行。

本地 MongoDB 数据库位于端口 27017 上，因此请使用以下命令连接到本地 mongodb 数据库。

所以现在你有两个用于 Twitter 的 MongoDB 数据库，一个在本地，一个在 Docker 容器中。 让我们向数据库中添加一些数据，即使您删除了容器，这些数据也会持续存在。

在命令行中打开一个到 Docker 容器内的 MongoDB 数据库的 mongosh 连接。

运行以下命令。 您正在创建一个名为twitter的数据库，其中包含一个名为contents的集合。 然后您将一些数据插入到数据库中。

 use twitter ; db.createCollection ( 'contents' ) ; db.contents.insertMany ([ { tweet: "Hello World!" , img: "" } , { tweet: "Content creation and programming are basically full time jobs. I have enough projects and work to keep me busy for years. Working in tech is definitely going to entertain you for a long time which is why so many people want to transition into this field." , img: "" } , { tweet: "JavaScript developers are forever in high demand" , img: "" } ]) ;

现在当你运行命令db.contents.find().pretty(); 在命令行内部，它应该返回您刚刚插入的数据。 如果您转到 MongoDB 指南针并使用此连接字符串mongodb://localhost:2717 ，您应该会看到twitter数据库中的数据。

检查您是否位于complete-react-developer的根文件夹中，然后运行以下命令来设置项目。

 mkdir docker-twitter-postgresql cd docker-twitter-postgresql touch docker-compose.yml mkdir sql cd sql touch twitter.sql cd ..

在代码编辑器中打开该文件夹并将以下代码添加到docker-compose.yml和twitter.sql文件中。

docker-compose.yml

如果缩进不正确，请注意 yaml 代码格式，它会给您带来错误。

 version : ' 3.7' services : postgres : image : postgres:latest restart : always environment : - POSTGRES_USER=twitter - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=twitter - POSTGRES_DB=twitter ports : - ' 5433:5432' volumes : - ./postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data # copy the sql script to create tables - ./sql/twitter.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/twitter.sql

twitter.sql

 CREATE TABLE contents ( id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid (), tweet VARCHAR ( 280 ) NOT NULL , img VARCHAR ( 500 ) NOT NULL ); INSERT INTO contents ( tweet , img ) VALUES ( 'Hello World!' , '' ), ( 'Content creation and programming are basically full time jobs. I have enough projects and work to keep me busy for years. Working in tech is definitely going to entertain you for a long time which is why so many people want to transition into this field.' , '' ), ( 'JavaScript developers are forever in high demand' , '' );

现在运行以下代码以使用 PostgreSQL 数据库启动 Docker 容器。

当您看到显示数据库系统已准备好接受连接的日志时，您将知道它正在工作。 这也可以在 Docker 桌面应用程序中进行验证，如果您检查它，您应该会看到容器正在运行。

我们刚刚做的是设置一个 PostgreSQL 数据库，它将驻留在 Docker 容器内。 这个数据库甚至会从我们创建的 SQL 脚本中获得一些预构建的数据。

连接到 Docker 容器内的 PostgreSQL 数据库

我这样做是为了让我们可以在本地使用 5432 端口上的 Postgres 应用程序，并同时在 5433 端口上运行 Docker 数据库。

所以这里就是魔法发生的地方！ 使用下图中的连接凭据，您应该能够连接到 docker 容器内的 PostgreSQL 数据库！

顺便说一句，密码是twitter ，您可以在docker-compose.yml文件中找到凭据。

所以现在我们在端口 5432 上有一个名为twitter的本地 PostgreSQL 数据库。在端口 5433 上有一个名为twitter的 Docker PostgreSQL 数据库。Valentina Studio 可以同时连接到这两个数据库，您可以运行所有 SQL 查询。 更重要的是 Docker 容器内的 PostgreSQL 数据库可以持久化其数据。 如果您要删除正在运行的 Docker 容器，然后再次运行命令docker compose up ，一切都将保持不变！

如果需要，使用命令docker compose down停止 Docker 容器运行。

恭喜您刚刚学习了 MongoDB、PostgreSQL 和 Docker 的基础知识！

节点后端设置

这将分为两个部分。 一种用于使用 Express.js 和 TypeScript 创建后端。 另一个用于使用 Nest.js 和 TypeScript 创建后端，这样您就可以看到两者之间的区别并学习在 Node.js 中开发后端的替代方法。

因此将有 4 个 Node 后端可供使用：

  • 后端快递-mon​​godb
  • 后端快递-postgresql
  • 后端巢mongodb
  • 后端巢postgresql

将需要一个 REST API 测试工具，以便您可以测试各种路由和端点。 这些是我的前 3 个偏好，如果您愿意，可以随意使用其他东西。

邮差
迅雷客户端
失眠

你不需要创建所有这些后端，因为当我们创建 React 前端时，它只需要一个后端，但这仍然是值得学习的好知识。 显然，您可以只拥有一个连接到 MongoDB 和 PostgreSQL 的后端，在这些示例中更容易解释。

快递应用

后端快速 MongoDB

确保您位于complete-react-developer文件夹内。

运行以下命令来搭建您的项目。

 mkdir backend-express-mongodb cd backend-express-mongodb tsc --init npm init -y npm i express @types/express @types/node ts-node cors @types/cors mongoose @types/mongoose typescript rimraf copy-files dotenv nodemon touch .env mkdir src cd src touch app.ts mkdir controllers models routes touch controllers/Admin.ts touch models/Twitter.ts touch routes/Admin.ts cd ..

在代码编辑器中打开项目并转到根文件夹内的tsconfig.json文件并启用这些属性。

 "rootDir" : "./src" /* Specify the root folder within your source files. */,
"moduleResolution" : "node" /* Specify how TypeScript looks up a file from a given module specifier. */,
"outDir" : "./dist/src" /* Specify an output folder for all emitted files. */,

在下一步中打开package.json文件并添加这些运行脚本。

 "scripts" : { "start" : "node dist/src/app.js" , "dev" : "nodemon src/app.ts" , "clean" : "rimraf dist/" , "build" : "npm run clean && tsc && npm run copy-files" , "copy-files" : "copyfiles -u 1 src/**/*.html src/**/*.css src/**/*.json dist/src" } ,
运行脚本

开始
当文件有更新时，启动脚本使用 Node 运行应用程序，而不会自动重新加载。

开发者
干净的
建造
构建脚本删除dist文件夹，然后自动复制所有文件并将它们放回dist文件夹。

复制文件
复制文件脚本用于将文件从一个目录复制到另一个目录。

添加代码

最后将下面的代码添加到相应的文件中。

controllers/Admin.ts

 import { Response , Request } from ' express ' ; import mongoose from ' mongoose ' ; import Twitter from ' ../models/Twitter ' ; export const getTweets = ( req : Request , res : Response ): void => { Twitter . find (( err , data ) => { console . log ( data ); res . json ( data ); if ( err ) { console . log ( err ); } }); }; export const getTweet = async ( req : Request , res : Response ): Promise < any > => { const tweetId = req . params . tweetId ; console . log ( ' Tweet ID ' , tweetId ); // This line of code fixes the CastError: Cast to ObjectId failed for value "favicon.ico" (type string) at path "_id" for model "contents" if ( ! mongoose . Types . ObjectId . isValid ( tweetId )) return false ; await Twitter . findById ( tweetId ). exec (); Twitter . findById ( tweetId , ( err : any , tweet : any ) => { console . log ( tweet ); res . json ( tweet ); if ( err ) { console . log ( err ); } }); }; export const postTweet = ( req : Request , res : Response ) => { const { tweet , img } = req . body ; const twitter = new Twitter ({ tweet : tweet , img : img }); twitter . save (); console . log ( ' Tweet Created ' ); res . status ( 201 ). json ({ msg : ' Tweet Created ' }); }; export const updateTweet = ( req : Request , res : Response ) => { const tweetId = req . params . tweetId ; const { tweet , img } = req . body ; Twitter . findByIdAndUpdate ( tweetId , { tweet : tweet , img : img }). then (() => { console . log ( `Tweet ${ tweetId } Updated` ); res . json ({ msg : `Tweet ${ tweetId } Updated` }); }); }; export const deleteTweet = ( req : Request , res : Response ) => { const tweetId = req . body . tweetId ; Twitter . findByIdAndRemove ( tweetId , () => { res . json ({ msg : `Tweet ${ tweetId } Deleted` }); }); };

models/Twitter.ts

 import { Schema , model } from ' mongoose ' ; interface Twitter { tweet : string ; img : string ; } const schema = new Schema < Twitter > ({ tweet : { type : String , required : true }, img : { type : String , required : false }, }); const TwitterModel = model < Twitter > ( ' contents ' , schema ); export default TwitterModel ;

routes/Admin.ts

 import express from ' express ' ; import { getTweets , getTweet , postTweet , updateTweet , deleteTweet } from ' ../controllers/Admin ' ; const router = express . Router (); router . get ( ' / ' , getTweets ); router . get ( ' /:tweetId ' , getTweet ); router . post ( ' /delete ' , deleteTweet ); router . post ( ' /tweet ' , postTweet ); router . post ( ' /:tweetId ' , updateTweet ); export default router ;

app.ts

 import dotenv from ' dotenv ' ; dotenv . config (); console . log ( process . env . DB_HOST ); import express from ' express ' ; import cors from ' cors ' ; import mongoose from ' mongoose ' ; import adminRoute from ' ./routes/Admin ' ; const app = express (); app . use ( cors ()); app . use ( express . urlencoded ({ extended : false })); app . use ( express . json ()); app . use ( ' / ' , adminRoute ); const port = process . env . PORT || 8080 ; mongoose // Use DB_HOST_DOCKER to connect to the MongoDB Database in the Docker Container . connect ( ` ${ process . env . DB_HOST_LOCAL } ` ) . then (() => { app . listen ( port , () => console . log ( `Server and database running on port ${ port } , http://localhost: ${ port } ` )); }) . catch (( err : any ) => { console . log ( err ); });

.env

 DB_HOST_LOCAL = "mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017/twitter" DB_HOST_DOCKER = "mongodb://127.0.0.1:2717/twitter"

该应用程序设置为连接到本地 MongoDB 数据库，但您可以在app.ts .env中找到数据库连接字符串。

使用以下命令启动服务器。

测试 API

我将使用 Postman，但您可以使用任何您想要的 API 测试工具。 如果您想看到它完全工作，您必须做的第一件事是向数据库添加一些数据（如果您还没有这样做的话）。 为此使用创建推文路由并查看下面屏幕截图中的示例。

后端快递 PostgreSQL

我们将使用https://typeorm.io/创建一个连接到 PostgreSQL 数据库的 Express.js 应用程序。

您应该在文件夹complete-react-developer中。

运行以下命令来搭建您的项目。

 mkdir backend-express-postgresql cd backend-express-postgresql tsc --init npm init -y npm i express @types/express @types/node ts-node cors @types/cors typescript rimraf copy-files dotenv nodemon pg reflect-metadata typeorm mkdir src cd src touch app.ts app-data-source.ts mkdir entity cd entity touch Tweet.ts cd ../..

在代码编辑器中打开项目，找到tsconfig.json文件并将其中的所有代码替换为以下代码。

 { "compilerOptions" : { "lib" : [ "es5" , "es6" , "dom" ], "target" : "es5" , "module" : "commonjs" , "moduleResolution" : "node" , "emitDecoratorMetadata" : true , "experimentalDecorators" : true , "rootDir" : "./src" , "outDir" : "./dist/src" }
}

在下一步中打开package.json文件并添加这些运行脚本。

 "scripts" : { "start" : "node dist/src/app.js" , "dev" : "nodemon src/app.ts" , "clean" : "rimraf dist/" , "build" : "npm run clean && tsc && npm run copy-files" , "copy-files" : "copyfiles -u 1 src/**/*.html src/**/*.css src/**/*.json dist/src" } ,

将下面的代码添加到相应的文件中。

entity/Tweet.ts

 import { Entity , Column , PrimaryGeneratedColumn } from ' typeorm ' ; @ Entity () export class Tweet { @ PrimaryGeneratedColumn ( ' uuid ' ) id : string ; @ Column () tweet : string ; @ Column () img : string ; }

app-data-source.ts

 import { DataSource } from ' typeorm ' ; export const myDataSource = new DataSource ({ type : ' postgres ' , host : ' localhost ' , port : 5432 , username : ' postgres ' , password : '' , database : ' twitter ' , entities : [ ' dist/src/entity/*.js ' ], logging : true , synchronize : true , });

app.ts

 import * as express from ' express ' ; import { Request , Response } from ' express ' ; import { Tweet } from ' ./entity/Tweet ' ; import { myDataSource } from ' ./app-data-source ' ; // establish database connection myDataSource . initialize () . then (() => { console . log ( ' Data Source has been initialized! ' ); }) . catch (( err ) => { console . error ( ' Error during Data Source initialization: ' , err ); }); // create and setup express app const app = express (); app . use ( express . json ()); app . use ( express . urlencoded ({ extended : false })); // register CRUD routes // CREATE // READ // UPDATE // DELETE // READ: All tweets app . get ( ' /tweets ' , async function ( req : Request , res : Response ) { const tweets = await myDataSource . getRepository ( Tweet ). find (); res . json ( tweets ); }); // READ: Tweet by ID app . get ( ' /tweets/:id ' , async function ( req : Request , res : Response ) { const results = await myDataSource . getRepository ( Tweet ). findOneBy ({ id : req . params . id , }); return res . send ( results ); }); // CREATE: New tweet app . post ( ' /tweets ' , async function ( req : Request , res : Response ) { const tweet = await myDataSource . getRepository ( Tweet ). create ( req . body ); const results = await myDataSource . getRepository ( Tweet ). save ( tweet ); return res . send ( results ); }); // UPDATE: Tweet by ID app . put ( ' /tweets/:id ' , async function ( req : Request , res : Response ) { const tweet = await myDataSource . getRepository ( Tweet ). findOneBy ({ id : req . body . id , }); myDataSource . getRepository ( Tweet ). merge ( tweet , req . body ); const results = await myDataSource . getRepository ( Tweet ). save ( tweet ); return res . send ( results ); }); // DELETE: Tweet by ID app . delete ( ' /tweets/:id ' , async function ( req : Request , res : Response ) { const results = await myDataSource . getRepository ( Tweet ). delete ( req . body . id ); return res . send ( results ); }); const port = process . env . PORT || 8080 ; // start express server app . listen ( port , () => console . log ( `Server and database running on port ${ port } , http://localhost: ${ port } ` ));

该应用程序设置为连接到本地 PostgreSQL 数据库，但您可以在app-data-source.ts文件中进行更改。 如果需要，可以在 Docker 部分中找到 Docker 连接设置。 不要忘记，您需要先设置并运行 PostgreSQL 数据库，然后才能连接到它。

使用以下命令运行应用程序。

警告：您可能会收到错误EntityMetadataNotFoundError: No metadata for "Tweet" was found. 如果您尝试使用命令npm run dev使用 nodemon 来启动应用程序。 我认为这与静态和动态数据以及 nodemon 自动重新加载的事实有关。 因此，使用以下命令更安全，只需使用节点服务器进行干净构建，直到您手动重新启动它才会更新。

 npm run build npm run start

您现在应该熟悉如何使用 REST API 工具，但是此示例中的路由和 CRUD 请求略有不同。 看下面的例子，别忘了使用 CREATE tweet 路由添加一些数据到数据库中，这样你就可以看到一些数据了。

获取所有推文

请求：获取
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets

按 ID 获取推文

请求：获取
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets/d5d29839-788f-4d23-99ee-82b49ff1bbf1

创建推文

请求：POST
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets
正文原始：{“tweet”：'Hello World'，img：“”}

按 ID 更新推文

请求：PUT
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets/d5d29839-788f-4d23-99ee-82b49ff1bbf1
正文原始：{“tweet”：'Hello Moon'，img：“”}

按 ID 删除推文

请求：删除
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets/d5d29839-788f-4d23-99ee-82b49ff1bbf1 正文：x-www-form-urlencoded KEY：id

价值：你的身份证

巢应用

后端嵌套 MongoDB

是时候创建将连接到 MongoDB 的 Nest 后端了。 进入文件夹complete-react-developer并运行以下命令。 选择您喜欢的包管理器我将选择 npm。 如果您选择不同的选项，请记住稍后为其运行正确的命令。

 nest new backend-nest-mongodb

在代码编辑器中打开项目并准备生成一些控制器和服务文件。 我们还将在命令行中将 mongoose first cd安装到backend-nest-mongodb文件夹中并运行以下命令。

 cd backend-nest-mongodb npm install --save @nestjs/mongoose mongoose nest g controller twitter nest g service twitter

在我们创建其他项目文件之前，让我们进行一些文件清理。 删除以下文件：

app.service.ts
app.controller.ts
app.controller.spec.ts

现在是时候为这个项目创建其余的文件了。 进入backend-nest-mongodb的根文件夹并运行以下命令。

 touch src/twitter/twitter.module.ts mkdir src/twitter/ { dto,schemas } touch src/twitter/dto/create-twitter.dto.ts touch src/twitter/schemas/twitter.schema.ts

我们已经创建了该项目所需的所有文件，现在让我们添加代码。 使用以下代码添加或替换现有文件中的代码：

app.module.ts

 import { Module } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { MongooseModule } from ' @nestjs/mongoose ' ; import { TwitterController } from ' ./twitter/twitter.controller ' ; import { TwitterService } from ' ./twitter/twitter.service ' ; import { TwitterModule } from ' ./twitter/twitter.module ' ; @ Module ({ imports : [ TwitterModule , // Local MongoDb database // Change the port to 127.0.0.1:2717 to connect to Docker MongooseModule . forRoot ( ' mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017/twitter ' ), ], controllers : [ TwitterController ], providers : [ TwitterService ], }) export class AppModule {}

twitter.service.ts

 import { Model } from ' mongoose ' ; import { Injectable } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { InjectModel } from ' @nestjs/mongoose ' ; import { Twitter , TwitterDocument } from ' ./schemas/twitter.schema ' ; import { CreateTwitterDto } from ' ./dto/create-twitter.dto ' ; @ Injectable () export class TwitterService { constructor (@ InjectModel ( Twitter . name ) private twitterModel : Model < TwitterDocument > ) {} async create ( createTwitterDto : CreateTwitterDto ): Promise < Twitter > { const createdTwitter = new this . twitterModel ( createTwitterDto ); return createdTwitter . save (); } async findAll (): Promise < Twitter [] > { return this . twitterModel . find (). exec (); } async findOne ( id : string ): Promise < Twitter > { return this . twitterModel . findOne ({ _id : id }); } async update ( id : string , twitter : Twitter ): Promise < Twitter > { return this . twitterModel . findByIdAndUpdate ( id , twitter , { new : true }); } async delete ( id : string ): Promise < Twitter > { return this . twitterModel . findByIdAndRemove ({ _id : id }); } }

twitter.module.ts

 import { Module } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { MongooseModule } from ' @nestjs/mongoose ' ; import { Twitter , TwitterSchema } from ' ./schemas/twitter.schema ' ; @ Module ({ imports : [ MongooseModule . forFeature ([{ name : Twitter . name , schema : TwitterSchema }])], exports : [ MongooseModule ], }) export class TwitterModule {}

twitter.controller.ts

 import { Controller , Get , Post , Body , Param , Put , Delete } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { CreateTwitterDto , TwitterDto } from ' ./dto/create-twitter.dto ' ; import { TwitterService } from ' ./twitter.service ' ; @ Controller ( ' tweets ' ) export class TwitterController { constructor ( private twitterService : TwitterService ) {} @ Post () async create (@ Body () createTwitterDto : CreateTwitterDto ) { this . twitterService . create ( createTwitterDto ); } @ Get () async findAll (): Promise < TwitterDto [] > { return this . twitterService . findAll (); } @ Get ( ' :id ' ) async findOne (@ Param ( ' id ' ) id ): Promise < TwitterDto > { return this . twitterService . findOne ( id ); } @ Put ( ' :id ' ) update ( @ Body () updateTwitterDto : CreateTwitterDto , @ Param ( ' id ' ) id ): Promise < TwitterDto > { return this . twitterService . update ( id , updateTwitterDto ); } @ Delete ( ' :id ' ) delete (@ Param ( ' id ' ) id ): Promise < TwitterDto > { return this . twitterService . delete ( id ); } }

schemas/twitter.schema.ts

 import { Prop , Schema , SchemaFactory } from ' @nestjs/mongoose ' ; import { Document } from ' mongoose ' ; export type TwitterDocument = Twitter & Document ; @ Schema () export class Twitter { @ Prop () tweet : string ; @ Prop () img : string ; } export const TwitterSchema = SchemaFactory . createForClass ( Twitter );

dto/create-twitter.dto.ts

 export class CreateTwitterDto { id ?: string ; tweet : string ; img : string ; } export class TwitterDto { id ?: string ; tweet : string ; img : string ; }

现在应该设置一切，后端配置为连接到本地 MongoDB 数据库。 您可以通过编辑app.module.ts文件中的连接字符串将其更改为 Docker。

运行以下命令以监视模式启动应用程序。

值得一提的是，默认情况下 NestJS 应用程序在端口 3000 上运行，这与我们的 React 应用程序将使用的默认端口相同。 因此，为了保持一致性，您可能希望将其更改为 8080 或其他端口。 您可以在main.ts文件中执行此操作。 此外，您还需要启用 CORS，否则当您尝试将后端连接到前端时，您会遇到烦人的 CORS 错误。

main.ts

 import { NestFactory } from ' @nestjs/core ' ; import { AppModule } from ' ./app.module ' ; async function bootstrap () { const app = await NestFactory . create ( AppModule ); app . enableCors (); await app . listen ( 8080 ); } bootstrap ();

路由应该和以前一样，这里是一个复习，您可以在 Postman 或您使用的任何 REST API 工具中进行测试：

获取所有推文

路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets

按 ID 获取推文

请求：获取
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets/d5d29839-788f-4d23-99ee-82b49ff1bbf1

创建推文

请求：POST
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets
正文原始：{“tweet”：'Hello World'，img：“”}

按 ID 更新推文

请求：PUT
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets/d5d29839-788f-4d23-99ee-82b49ff1bbf1
正文原始：{“tweet”：'Hello Moon'，img：“”}

按 ID 删除推文

请求：删除
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets/d5d29839-788f-4d23-99ee-82b49ff1bbf1

后端巢 PostgreSQL

最后，我们现在将创建将连接到 PostgreSQL 的 Nest 后端。 在这个阶段之后，我们最终将进入 React 前端。 确保您位于文件夹complete-react-developer中并运行以下命令。 就像在上一章中选择你喜欢的包管理器一样，我将选择 npm。 如果您选择不同的选项，请记住稍后为其运行正确的命令。

 nest new backend-nest-postgresql

在代码编辑器中打开项目并准备生成一些控制器和服务文件。 我们还将安装 PostgreSQL 和TypeORM ，以便我们可以连接到 PostgreSQL 数据库。 首先在命令行中cd进入backend-nest-postgresql文件夹并运行以下命令。

 cd backend-nest-postgresql npm install --save pg @nestjs/typeorm typeorm nest g controller twitter nest g service twitter

在我们创建其他项目文件之前，让我们进行一些文件清理。 删除以下文件：

app.service.ts
app.controller.ts
app.controller.spec.ts

现在是时候为这个项目创建其余的文件了。 当您位于backend-nest-postgresql的根文件夹中时，请运行以下命令。

 touch src/twitter/ { twitter.module.ts,twitter.entity.ts } mkdir src/twitter/dto touch src/twitter/dto/twitter.dto.ts

我们已经创建了该项目所需的所有文件，现在让我们添加代码。 使用以下代码添加或替换现有文件中的代码：

app.module.ts

 import { Module } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { TypeOrmModule } from ' @nestjs/typeorm ' ; import { TwitterController } from ' ./twitter/twitter.controller ' ; import { TwitterService } from ' ./twitter/twitter.service ' ; import { TwitterModule } from ' ./twitter/twitter.module ' ; import { Connection } from ' typeorm ' ; @ Module ({ imports : [ TypeOrmModule . forRoot ({ type : ' postgres ' , host : ' localhost ' , port : 5432 , username : ' postgres ' , password : '' , database : ' twitter ' , entities : [ ' dist/**/*.entity{.ts,.js} ' ], synchronize : false , }), TwitterModule , ], controllers : [ TwitterController ], providers : [ TwitterService ], }) export class AppModule { constructor ( private connection : Connection ) {} }

twitter.service.ts

 import { Injectable , NotFoundException } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { InjectRepository } from ' @nestjs/typeorm ' ; import { DeleteResult , InsertResult , Repository } from ' typeorm ' ; import { Twitter } from ' ./twitter.entity ' ; @ Injectable () export class TwitterService { constructor ( @ InjectRepository ( Twitter ) private twitterRepository : Repository < Twitter > ) {} async addTwitter ( twitter : Twitter ): Promise < InsertResult > { return this . twitterRepository . insert ( twitter ); } async findAll (): Promise < Twitter [] > { return this . twitterRepository . find (); } async findOne ( id : string ): Promise < Twitter > { return this . twitterRepository . findOne ( id ); } async update ( id : string , twitter : Twitter ): Promise < Twitter > { const twitterUpdate = await this . findOne ( id ); if ( twitterUpdate === undefined ) { throw new NotFoundException (); } await this . twitterRepository . update ( id , twitter ); return this . twitterRepository . findOne ( id ); } async delete ( id : string ): Promise < DeleteResult > { const twitterUpdate = await this . findOne ( id ); if ( twitterUpdate === undefined ) { throw new NotFoundException (); } return this . twitterRepository . delete ( id ); } }

twitter.module.ts

 import { Module } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { TypeOrmModule } from ' @nestjs/typeorm ' ; import { TwitterController } from ' ./twitter.controller ' ; import { TwitterService } from ' ./twitter.service ' ; import { Twitter } from ' ./twitter.entity ' ; @ Module ({ imports : [ TypeOrmModule . forFeature ([ Twitter ])], controllers : [ TwitterController ], providers : [ TwitterService ], exports : [ TypeOrmModule ], }) export class TwitterModule {}

twitter.entity.ts

 import { Entity , Column , PrimaryGeneratedColumn } from ' typeorm ' ; @ Entity () export class Twitter { @ PrimaryGeneratedColumn ( ' uuid ' ) id : string ; @ Column () tweet : string ; @ Column () img : string ; }

twitter.controller.ts

 import { Controller , Get , Post , Patch , Delete , Param , Body } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { TwitterService } from ' ./twitter.service ' ; import { TwitterDto } from ' ./dto/twitter.dto ' ; import { Twitter } from ' ./twitter.entity ' ; @ Controller ( ' tweets ' ) export class TwitterController { constructor ( private twitterService : TwitterService ) {} @ Post () create (@ Body () twitter : Twitter ) { return this . twitterService . addTwitter ( twitter ); } @ Get () findAll (): Promise < TwitterDto [] > { return this . twitterService . findAll (); } @ Get ( ' :id ' ) getOneTwitter (@ Param ( ' id ' ) id : string ): Promise < Twitter > { return this . twitterService . findOne ( id ); } @ Patch ( ' :id ' ) updateTwitter ( @ Param ( ' id ' ) id : string , @ Body () twitter : Twitter ): Promise < Twitter > { return this . twitterService . update ( id , twitter ); } @ Delete ( ' :id ' ) deleteTwitter (@ Param ( ' id ' ) id : string ) { return this . twitterService . delete ( id ); } }

dto/twitter.dto.ts

 export class TwitterDto { tweet : string ; img : string ; }

现在应该设置一切，后端配置为连接到本地 PostgreSQL 数据库。 您可以通过编辑app.module.ts文件中的连接详细信息将其更改为 Docker。

尽管此应用程序使用了一个名为twitter的数据库表，但有一点值得注意。 查看可用于生成一些快速测试数据的示例 SQL。 如果您收到错误，可能是因为它希望找到一个名为twitter的表。

 CREATE TABLE twitter ( id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid (), tweet VARCHAR ( 280 ) NOT NULL , img VARCHAR ( 500 ) NOT NULL ); INSERT INTO twitter ( tweet , img ) VALUES ( 'Hello World!' , '' ), ( 'Content creation and programming are basically full time jobs. I have enough projects and work to keep me busy for years. Working in tech is definitely going to entertain you for a long time which is why so many people want to transition into this field.' , '' ), ( 'JavaScript developers are forever in high demand' , '' );

运行以下命令以监视模式启动应用程序。 和以前一样，路线非常相似，但也有一些不同。 另外不要忘记在main.ts文件中将端口更改为 8080。 像之前一样，您需要启用 CORS，否则当您尝试将后端连接到前端时，您会遇到烦人的 CORS 错误。

main.ts

 import { NestFactory } from ' @nestjs/core ' ; import { AppModule } from ' ./app.module ' ; async function bootstrap () { const app = await NestFactory . create ( AppModule ); app . enableCors (); await app . listen ( 8080 ); } bootstrap ();

创建推文

请求：POST
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets
正文原始：{“tweet”：'Hello World'，img：“”}

按 ID 更新推文

请求：补丁
路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets/d5d29839-788f-4d23-99ee-82b49ff1bbf1
正文原始：{“tweet”：'Hello Moon'，img：“”}

路线： http://localhost:8080/tweets/d5d29839-788f-4d23-99ee-82b49ff1bbf1 正文：x-www-form-urlencoded KEY：id

设置前端

终于到了前端部分！ 它不会像我们刚刚完成的后端部分那样接近，因为只有一个 React 前端！

构建 Twitter 克隆应用程序

该应用程序将是一个非常简单的 Twitter 克隆。 您可以创建、阅读和删除推文。 没有更新/编辑推文的选项，这正是它现在的样子😂但是用于更新的端点已经存在于后端，因此您可以根据需要实现它。 顺便说一句，这不是 Twitter 克隆课程，所以不要指望它是完美的像素和 100% 准确😁

代码库非常大，因此我创建了应用程序并将其放在 GitHub 上，而不是多次复制和粘贴代码并经历漫长的项目设置。 因此，您需要做的就是克隆/下载代码库并运行安装脚本。

接下来在您的代码编辑器中打开项目以查看代码库并在其各自的根文件夹中使用以下命令。 设置说明也在 README 文件中。

设置

  1. 在您的计算机上启动 Docker 桌面应用程序

  2. 在这种情况下，您可能需要在尝试安装前端 React 应用程序的依赖项时强制安装，否则可能会给您一个错误。

 # Run this command inside of the backend-nest-mongodb folder npm install # Run this command inside of the frontend folder npm install --force
  1. 如果您想在 REST API 工具中测试它们，请使用Backend Nest MongoDB部分中的路由。

    您应该看到您的数据库在 Docker 容器内运行，并且您的 Twitter Clone React 应用程序在浏览器中打开。

    在 React 所在的前端根文件夹中运行这些命令。 下面的命令启动 Storybook。

     # Starts Storybook npm run storybook

    您应该会看到一个 Storybook 组件库在浏览器中打开，其中包含一个用于撰写推文的组件。 您可以随意更改控件中的名称，以查看它在演示中的外观。 下面的命令运行单元和集成测试。

     # Runs the React testing library unit and integration tests npm run test

    您可能需要按a或Enter来触发新的测试运行。 所有测试都应该在您的控制台中通过。 下面的命令启动赛普拉斯。

     # Runs the Cypress End-To-End tests npx cypress open

    应打开一个新的 Cypress 窗口。 运行集成测试并准备好惊讶，因为它会自动为您发布 3 条推文！ 用你的 React 应用程序重新加载网页，你也会在那里看到新的推文！

    上下文 API

    此应用程序使用 Context API 来获取全局状态。 如果您想让应用程序连接到您的 MongoDB、PostgreSQL 或 Docker 数据库，那么您需要更改 API 路由和端口号http://localhost:8080/tweets 。 这同样适用于方法不要忘记其中一些使用 POST、PUT、PATCH、DELETE 等...这取决于您使用的后端。

    src/contexts/TwitterContext.tsx

     import { useEffect , useState , createContext , useContext } from ' react ' ; interface ContextProps { data : any ; loading : boolean ; handleToggleComposetweet : any ; toggleComposeTweet : boolean ; tweet : string ; setTweet : any ; postTweet : any ; deleteTweet : any ; } const TwitterContext = createContext ({} as ContextProps ); export const useTwitter = () => useContext ( TwitterContext ); const TwitterContextProvider = ( props : any ): any => { useEffect (() => { const getTweets = () => { const API = ' http://localhost:8080/tweets ' ; fetch ( API ) . then (( response ) => { console . log ( response ); return response . json (); }) . then (( data ) => { console . log ( data ); setLoading ( false ); setData ( data ); }) . catch (( err ) => { console . log ( err ); }); }; getTweets (); }, []); const [ data , setData ] = useState ([]); const [ loading , setLoading ] = useState ( true ); const [ toggleComposeTweet , setToggleComposeTweet ] = useState ( false ); const [ tweet , setTweet ] = useState ( '' ); const handleToggleComposetweet = () => { toggleComposeTweet === true ? setToggleComposeTweet ( false ) : setToggleComposeTweet ( true ); }; const postTweet = () => { if ( tweet === '' ) { let myHeaders = new Headers (); myHeaders . append ( ' Content-Type ' , ' application/json ' ); let raw = JSON . stringify ({ tweet : ' Congratulations this is what happens when you post an empty tweet 🤪 Create some validation 🙃 ' , img : '' , }); fetch ( ' http://localhost:8080/tweets ' , { method : ' POST ' , headers : myHeaders , body : raw , redirect : ' follow ' }) . then (( response ) => response . text ()) . then (( result ) => console . log ( result )) . catch (( error ) => console . log ( ' error ' , error )); } else { let myHeaders = new Headers (); myHeaders . append ( ' Content-Type ' , ' application/json ' ); let raw = JSON . stringify ({ tweet : tweet , img : '' , }); fetch ( ' http://localhost:8080/tweets ' , { method : ' POST ' , headers : myHeaders , body : raw , redirect : ' follow ' }) . then (( response ) => response . text ()) . then (( result ) => console . log ( result )) . catch (( error ) => console . log ( ' error ' , error )); } }; const deleteTweet = ( tweetId : string ) => { console . log ( ' Deleted ' , tweetId ); let urlencoded = new URLSearchParams (); fetch ( `http://localhost:8080/tweets/ ${ tweetId } ` , { method : ' DELETE ' , body : urlencoded , redirect : ' follow ' , }) . then (( response ) => response . text ()) . then (( result ) => console . log ( result )) . catch (( error ) => console . log ( ' error ' , error )); window . location . reload (); }; const value = { data , loading , toggleComposeTweet , handleToggleComposetweet , postTweet , tweet , setTweet , deleteTweet , }; return < TwitterContext . Provider value = { value } > { props . children } < /TwitterContext.Provider> ; }; export default TwitterContextProvider ;

    使用 React 测试库和 Jest 进行测试

    有两个测试文件，一个用于App.test.tsx ，一个用于TwitterMenu.test.tsx 。

    我将展示App.test.tsx的示例。 这些测试只是测试页面上是否显示了所需的文本。 每个组件都应该有一个测试文件与它们一起使用。

    App.test.tsx

     import { render , screen } from ' @testing-library/react ' ; import App from ' ./App ' ; describe ( ' <App /> ' , () => { it ( ' has a following text label ' , () => { render ( < App /> ); const el = screen . getByText ( /Following/i ); expect ( el ). toBeTruthy (); }); it ( ' has a followers text label ' , () => { render ( < App /> ); const el = screen . getByText ( /Followers/i ); expect ( el ). toBeTruthy (); }); it ( ' has a you might like heading ' , () => { render ( < App /> ); const el = screen . getByText ( /You might like/i ); expect ( el . innerHTML ). toBe ( ' You might like ' ); }); it ( ' has a whats happening heading ' , () => { render ( < App /> ); const el = screen . getByText ( /Whats happening/i ); expect ( el . innerHTML ). toBe ( ' Whats happening ' ); }); });

    使用 Cypress 进行端到端测试

    此赛普拉斯测试将自动发布 3 条推文。 这一切都是实时完成的，推文将显示在您的数据库和实时应用程序中。

    cypress/integratioin/tweet.spec.js

     describe ( ' user form flow ' , () => { beforeEach (() => { cy . viewport ( 1600 , 900 ); cy . visit ( ' http://localhost:3000/ ' ); }); it ( ' user posts a tweet ' , () => { // Post a tweet cy . get ( ' .compose-tweet-btn ' ). click (); cy . get ( ' textarea[name="tweet"] ' ). type ( ' What happened to all that fun you were having?! Come on, lets try to enjoy this! ' ); cy . wait ( 3000 ); cy . get ( ' .post-tweet-btn ' ). click (); }); it ( ' user posts a second tweet ' , () => { // Post a tweet cy . get ( ' .compose-tweet-btn ' ). click (); cy . get ( ' textarea[name="tweet"] ' ). type ( ' That was an Attack on Titan easter egg 🥚 😄 ' ); cy . wait ( 3000 ); cy . get ( ' .post-tweet-btn ' ). click (); }); it ( ' user posts a third tweet ' , () => { // Post a tweet cy . get ( ' .compose-tweet-btn ' ). click (); cy . get ( ' textarea[name="tweet"] ' ). type ( ' The Rumbling arrives on Marley 😱 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wT2H68kEmi8 ' ); cy . wait ( 3000 ); cy . get ( ' .post-tweet-btn ' ). click (); }); });

    部署

    完成构建应用程序后，最后一步是部署。 您需要让您的应用程序在线，以便每个人都可以看到它。 那里有几十个平台，但这里是我排名前 5 的平台，排名不分先后。

    1. 网络化
    2. 韦尔塞尔
    3. 数字海洋
    4. 最后的想法

      我们涵盖了所有 MERN 堆栈，包括 TypeScript、SQL、测试驱动开发、端到端测试甚至 Docker！ 恭喜您变得超级棒，因为您提高了技能和工作前景🔥🚀

      玩转数据库和 React 前端，你可以用它做很多事情。 例如，创建更多 Storybook 组件、集成测试、添加编辑推文的功能以及让图像和视频等媒体出现在推文中。

      雅库布·安德杰耶夫斯基—— Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

Andrew Baisden

Posted on May 10

This will be a course for becoming a Complete Modern React Developer in 2022. The only three topics which are not covered in this course are Redux, GraphQL and React Native which could be covered in a future course. TypeScript is going to be the main programming language covered however if you already know JavaScript then you should find it quite easy to understand because the syntax is not that much different.

We will be building a super basic Twitter clone that has CRUD functionality for posting, reading and deleting tweets.

This course will give you the skills and knowledge to become a Software Developer across the full stack. In this course you will learn:

Prerequisites

Ensure that you have your development environment setup and install all of the tools/libraries listed in the introduction. I work on a Mac so some of the tools I mention will be macOS only however you should be able to find alternatives and be able to follow along if you use Windows or Linux.

MongoDB Setup

You will need to install the following tools for working with MongoDB NoSQL databases. MongoDB Compass is a GUI for working with MongoDB databases. And mongosh is a MongoDB shell for working with MongoDB databases using the command line.

MongoDB Compass
mongosh

PostgreSQL Setup

You will need to install the following tools for working with PostgreSQL databases. Postgres.app is an app for managing PostgreSQL databases. And Pgcli is a command line interface for Postgres which comes with auto-completion and syntax highlighting. It is the PostgreSQL equivalent of mongosh.

My preference is Valentina Studio when it comes to using a GUI for working with PostgreSQL databases. It's a great tool because it can even connect to MongoDB and MySQL databases. There are alternatives though like PgAdminso just use whatever you feel comfortable with.

Postgres.app
Pgcli
Valentina Studio

Setting up the backend

In this section you will learn how to setup a Node backend using both Express.js and Nest.js. Both frameworks will connect to a MongoDB and PostgreSQL database using different endpoints. And as a bonus you will also learn some DevOps when we put a MongoDB and PostgreSQL database inside of a Docker container.

Docker basically gives developers the ability to package applications inside of containers. So essentially you can just have a database inside of a Docker container which any external application will be able to connect to. With this type of setup you don't even need to install or setup a database on your local machine. You can just have everything running inside of a Docker container and this setup will run exactly the same on anyones machine.

I think this a great alternative to having a local installation and with this knowledge it gives you another option for interacting with databases. This workflow does not require a huge setup and you can use either the GUI or command line to interact with your database inside of the Docker container the same way you would if it was local or online.

Local Database Setup

Pgcli Commands
mongosh Commands

MongoDB Local

Open your command line tool I will be using Hyper and run the command below to connect to your local MongoDB installation.

Firstly run this command which will show you which database you are using. It should return test which is the default database.

Now run the command below which will show you which databases you currently have created.

Next run the command to create a database called twitter.

Finally create a collection using the command below and when you use the command show dbs; again in the command line you should see the database called twitter which you created.

db.createCollection('contents');

Lastly we will add some starting data copy and paste the code below into your command line. If you run this command db.contents.find().pretty() in your command line after you have inserted the data then you will be able to see the data in the table.

db.contents.insertMany([ {tweet: "Hello World!", img: ""}, {tweet: "Content creation and programming are basically full time jobs. I have enough projects and work to keep me busy for years. Working in tech is definitely going to entertain you for a long time which is why so many people want to transition into this field.", img: ""}, {tweet: "JavaScript developers are forever in high demand", img: ""} ])

If you open the MongoDB Compass application on your computer and connect to your local installation using the connection string mongodb://localhost:27017 then you should be able to see all of your databases including the one we just created in the GUI. So now you are setup to use either the command line or the GUI for interacting with your databases.

PostgreSQL Local

Before you do anything check that your PostgreSQL database is running locally. If it's not running then you might get an error in the command line when you run the command pgcli. On macOS I will be using the Postgres.app so get it running on your machine and then it should show up in your menu bar at the top of your operating system.

Now go to the command line and run the command below to connect to your local PostgreSQL installation.

Running the command below will show you all of your PostgreSQL databases.

Copy and paste the SQL query below into your pgcli command line window to create a database called twitter. Now if you run the command \l again in that same window you should see all of the databases including the one we just created.

Next we need to connect to the database in the same window so use the command below to do that.

Next we have to create a table and add some data which will go inside of the database twitter. Copy and paste the SQL code below into your command line window.

CREATE TABLE contents ( id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid (), tweet VARCHAR(280) NOT NULL, img VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL ); INSERT INTO contents (tweet, img) VALUES ('Hello World!', ''), ('Content creation and programming are basically full time jobs. I have enough projects and work to keep me busy for years. Working in tech is definitely going to entertain you for a long time which is why so many people want to transition into this field.', ''), ('JavaScript developers are forever in high demand', '');

If you open the Postgres.app application on your computer you should see all of the databases including the one which we just created.

And if you connect to Valentina Studio or your database GUI of choice you should be able to see the database you created.

PostgreSQL database connection settings

Valentina Studio

Docker database setup

Start the Docker application on your computer and follow the steps below for each database. Firstly create a folder on your local machine called complete-react-developer
and then cd into using the command line.

MongoDB Docker

Double check that you are inside of the root folder for complete-react-developer and then run the commands below to setup the project.

mkdir docker-twitter-mongodb
cd docker-twitter-mongodb
touch docker-compose.yml

Open that folder in your code editor and add the following code to the docker-compose.yml file.

docker-compose.yml

Be careful with the yaml code formatting if the indentation is not correct it will give you errors.

version: '3.9'
services: mongo_db: container_name: db_container image: 'mongo:latest' restart: always ports: - '2717:27017' volumes: - 'mongo_db:/data/db'
volumes: mongo_db: {}

Now run the code below to start the docker container with the MongoDB database.

Assuming everything went correctly you should have a MongoDB Database running inside of a Docker Container.

Connecting to the MongoDB Database inside the Docker Container

It is now possible to connect to the local MongoDB Database and the MongoDB Docker Database simultaneously because they are both configured to run on different ports.

The local MongoDB Database is on port 27017 so use the command below for connecting to the local mongodb database.

The connection string for MongoDB Compass will be the following.

mongodb://localhost:27017

The MongoDB Docker Database is on port 2717 so use the command below for connecting to the Docker MongoDB database.

The connection string for MongoDB Compass will be the following.

So now you have two MongoDB databases for Twitter one local and one in a Docker Container. Let's add some data to the database which is also going to persist even if you delete the container.

In the command line open a mongosh connection to the MongoDB Database inside of the Docker container.

Run the commands below. You are creating a database called twitter with a collection called contents. And then you are inserting some data into the database.

use twitter; db.createCollection('contents'); db.contents.insertMany([ {tweet: "Hello World!", img: ""}, {tweet: "Content creation and programming are basically full time jobs. I have enough projects and work to keep me busy for years. Working in tech is definitely going to entertain you for a long time which is why so many people want to transition into this field.", img: ""}, {tweet: "JavaScript developers are forever in high demand", img: ""} ]);

Now when you run the command db.contents.find().pretty(); inside of the command line it should return the data you just inserted. If you go to MongoDB compass and use this connection string mongodb://localhost:2717 you should see the twitter database with the data inside.

PostgreSQL Docker

Check that you are inside of the root folder for complete-react-developer and then run the commands below to setup the project.

mkdir docker-twitter-postgresql
cd docker-twitter-postgresql
touch docker-compose.yml
mkdir sql
cd sql
touch twitter.sql
cd ..

Open that folder in you code editor and add the following code to the docker-compose.yml and twitter.sql files.

docker-compose.yml

Be careful with the yaml code formatting if the indentation is not correct it will give you errors.

version: '3.7'
services: postgres: image: postgres:latest restart: always environment: - POSTGRES_USER=twitter - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=twitter - POSTGRES_DB=twitter ports: - '5433:5432' volumes: - ./postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data # copy the sql script to create tables - ./sql/twitter.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/twitter.sql

twitter.sql

CREATE TABLE contents ( id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid (), tweet VARCHAR(280) NOT NULL, img VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL ); INSERT INTO contents (tweet, img) VALUES ('Hello World!', ''), ('Content creation and programming are basically full time jobs. I have enough projects and work to keep me busy for years. Working in tech is definitely going to entertain you for a long time which is why so many people want to transition into this field.', ''), ('JavaScript developers are forever in high demand', '');

Now run the code below to start the Docker Container with the PostgreSQL database.

When you see the log that says database system is ready to accept connections you will know that it's working. This can also be verified in the Docker Desktop Application if you check it you should see the container running.

What we have just done is setup a PostgreSQL database which will reside inside of a Docker container. This database is even going to have some prebuilt data from the SQL script that we created.

Connecting to the PostgreSQL Database inside the Docker Container

The docker-compose.yml file inside of the docker-twitter-postgresql folder has a port mapping of 5433:5432. 5433 is the local port and 5432 is the docker port which is also the default for Postgres. I did it this way so that we can use the Postgres app on port 5432 locally and run the Docker database on port 5433 simultaneously.

So here is where the magic happens! Use the connection credentials in the image below and you should be able to connect to the PostgreSQL database inside of the docker container!

The password is twitter by the way and you can find the credentials inside of the docker-compose.yml file.

So now we have a local PostgreSQL database called twitter on port 5432. And a Docker PostgreSQL database called twitter on port 5433. Valentina Studio can connect to both of them simultaneously and you can run all of your SQL queries. Whats more the PostgreSQL database inside of the Docker container can persist its data. If you were to delete the running Docker container and then run the command docker compose up again everything will remain the same!

Use the command docker compose down to stop the Docker container from running if you need to.

Congratulations you just learnt the basics of MongoDB, PostgreSQL and Docker!

Node backend setup

This will be split into two sections. One for creating a backend using Express.js and TypeScript. And another for creating a backend using Nest.js and TypeScript so you can see the difference between the two and learn alternative ways for developing a backend in Node.

So there will be 4 Node backends to play around with:

  • backend-express-mongodb
  • backend-express-postgresql
  • backend-nest-mongodb
  • backend-nest-postgresql

A REST API testing tool will be needed so that you can test the various routes and endpoints. These are my top 3 preferences feel free to use something else if you want to.

Postman
Thunder Client
Insomnia

You don't need to create all of these backends because when we create the React frontend it will only require one backend but this is still good knowledge worth learning. And obviously you could just have one backend that connects to both MongoDB and PostgreSQL it's just easier to explain in these examples.

Express App

Backend Express MongoDB

Be certain that you are inside of the folder complete-react-developer.

Run the commands below to scaffold your project.

mkdir backend-express-mongodb
cd backend-express-mongodb
tsc --init
npm init -y
npm i express @types/express @types/node ts-node cors @types/cors mongoose @types/mongoose typescript rimraf copy-files dotenv nodemon
touch .env
mkdir src
cd src
touch app.ts
mkdir controllers models routes
touch controllers/Admin.ts
touch models/Twitter.ts
touch routes/Admin.ts
cd ..

Open the project in your code editor and go to the tsconfig.json file inside of the root folder and enable these properties.

"rootDir": "./src" /* Specify the root folder within your source files. */,
"moduleResolution": "node" /* Specify how TypeScript looks up a file from a given module specifier. */,
"outDir": "./dist/src" /* Specify an output folder for all emitted files. */,

In the next step open the package.json file and add these run scripts.

"scripts": { "start": "node dist/src/app.js", "dev": "nodemon src/app.ts", "clean": "rimraf dist/", "build": "npm run clean && tsc && npm run copy-files", "copy-files": "copyfiles -u 1 src/**/*.html src/**/*.css src/**/*.json dist/src" },
Run Scripts

start
The start script runs the application using Node without auto reload when there are updates to the files.

dev
The dev script uses nodemon to automatically auto reload and update the files when there are changes.

clean
The clean script deletes the dist folder.

build
The build script deletes the dist folder and then automatically copies all of the files and puts them back into the dist folder.

copy-files
The copy files script is used for copying the files from one directory to a different one.

Adding the code

Lastly add the code below to their corresponding files.

controllers/Admin.ts

import { Response, Request } from 'express'; import mongoose from 'mongoose'; import Twitter from '../models/Twitter'; export const getTweets = (req: Request, res: Response): void => { Twitter.find((err, data) => { console.log(data); res.json(data); if (err) { console.log(err); } });
}; export const getTweet = async (req: Request, res: Response): Promise<any> => { const tweetId = req.params.tweetId; console.log('Tweet ID', tweetId); // This line of code fixes the CastError: Cast to ObjectId failed for value "favicon.ico" (type string) at path "_id" for model "contents" if (!mongoose.Types.ObjectId.isValid(tweetId)) return false; await Twitter.findById(tweetId).exec(); Twitter.findById(tweetId, (err: any, tweet: any) => { console.log(tweet); res.json(tweet); if (err) { console.log(err); } });
}; export const postTweet = (req: Request, res: Response) => { const { tweet, img } = req.body; const twitter = new Twitter({ tweet: tweet, img: img }); twitter.save(); console.log('Tweet Created'); res.status(201).json({ msg: 'Tweet Created' });
}; export const updateTweet = (req: Request, res: Response) => { const tweetId = req.params.tweetId; const { tweet, img } = req.body; Twitter.findByIdAndUpdate(tweetId, { tweet: tweet, img: img }).then(() => { console.log(`Tweet ${tweetId} Updated`); res.json({ msg: `Tweet ${tweetId} Updated` }); });
}; export const deleteTweet = (req: Request, res: Response) => { const tweetId = req.body.tweetId; Twitter.findByIdAndRemove(tweetId, () => { res.json({ msg: `Tweet ${tweetId} Deleted` }); });
};

models/Twitter.ts

import { Schema, model } from 'mongoose'; interface Twitter { tweet: string; img: string;
} const schema = new Schema<Twitter>({ tweet: { type: String, required: true }, img: { type: String, required: false },
}); const TwitterModel = model<Twitter>('contents', schema); export default TwitterModel;

routes/Admin.ts

import express from 'express'; import { getTweets, getTweet, postTweet, updateTweet, deleteTweet } from '../controllers/Admin'; const router = express.Router(); router.get('/', getTweets); router.get('/:tweetId', getTweet); router.post('/delete', deleteTweet); router.post('/tweet', postTweet); router.post('/:tweetId', updateTweet); export default router;

app.ts

import dotenv from 'dotenv'; dotenv.config(); console.log(process.env.DB_HOST); import express from 'express'; import cors from 'cors'; import mongoose from 'mongoose'; import adminRoute from './routes/Admin'; const app = express(); app.use(cors()); app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: false })); app.use(express.json()); app.use('/', adminRoute); const port = process.env.PORT || 8080; mongoose // Use DB_HOST_DOCKER to connect to the MongoDB Database in the Docker Container .connect(`${process.env.DB_HOST_LOCAL}`) .then(() => { app.listen(port, () => console.log(`Server and database running on port ${port}, http://localhost:${port}`)); }) .catch((err: any) => { console.log(err); });

.env

DB_HOST_LOCAL="mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017/twitter" DB_HOST_DOCKER="mongodb://127.0.0.1:2717/twitter"

The application is setup to connect to a local MongoDB database but you can change this in the app.ts file and you can find the database connection strings in the .env file.

Use the command below to start the server.

Testing the API

I will be using Postman but you can use whatever API Testing tool you want. If you want to see it fully working the first thing you will have to do is add some data to the database if you have not done so already. Use the Create tweet route for this and see the examples in the screenshots below.

GET all tweets

GET tweet by ID

CREATE tweet

UPDATE tweet by ID

DELETE tweet

Backend Express PostgreSQL

We will be using https://typeorm.io/to create an Express.js application that connects to a PostgreSQL database.

You should be inside of the folder complete-react-developer.

Run the commands below to scaffold your project.

mkdir backend-express-postgresql
