item.description: 原文:
item.author: ben

item.title: 初学者的抽象介绍 | Introduction to abstraction for beginners
item.description:
在面向对象编程中，抽象是一个关键概念，它允许您创建表示对象的一般概念而不是该对象的特定实例的类和方法。 这样做的目的是向用户隐藏实现细节。 这篇博客将解释什么是抽象，它是如何在 C# 中实现的，以及抽象的优势。

面向对象编程中的抽象对用户隐藏了不重要和复杂的细节，只显示了重要信息。 这意味着用户无需了解程序的复杂性即可使用它； 抽象使得在不了解底层实现细节的情况下使用复杂对象变得容易。 当你开车时，你不需要知道发动机是如何从 A 点到达 B 点的。你可以上车转动钥匙，剩下的事情由发动机来处理。 这是现实世界中抽象的一个例子。 抽象常常与封装相混淆。 但是，封装和抽象是两个不同的概念。 封装是面向对象编程中的另一个关键概念，它指的是将数据与操作该数据的方法捆绑在一起，并涉及限制对数据和方法的访问，这有时被称为数据隐藏。 抽象是一种向用户隐藏对象的实现细节的方法，而封装是一种向用户隐藏数据本身的方法。

抽象类如何提供抽象？


抽象类是不能被实例化的类，这意味着你不能创建抽象类类型的对象。 但是，抽象类可以被另一个类继承。 继承抽象类时，继承类必须为基类的所有抽象成员提供实现。 结果是基类通过有效地添加抽象层（抽象类）来向继承类隐藏这些成员的实现细节。

如何在 C# 中实现抽象

如前所述，抽象是在类和方法中实现的。 在 C# 中，您可以使用关键字“abstract”创建一个抽象类，它可以包含抽象和非抽象成员。 抽象类是无法实例化的类，这意味着您无法创建该类型的对象。 抽象方法没有主体，它们必须在派生类中实现。 非抽象方法是具有主体的方法，无需在派生类中实现即可调用它们。 在下面的示例中，我们创建了一个名为“Animal”的抽象类，其中包含一个抽象方法和一个非抽象方法：

如您所见，“Animal”类有一个名为“MakeNoise”的抽象方法和一个名为“Sleep”的非抽象方法。 您还可以使用关键字“abstract”在非抽象类中创建抽象方法。 在以下示例中，我们在“Animal”类中创建了一个名为“MakeNoise”的抽象方法：

如您所见，“Animal”类有一个名为“MakeNoise”的抽象方法和一个名为“Sleep”的非抽象方法。 创建抽象方法时，必须为派生类中的方法提供主体。 您可以使用关键字“override”来做到这一点。 在下面的示例中，我们创建了一个名为“Dog”的派生类，它覆盖了“MakeNoise”方法：

如您所见，“Dog”类通过为该方法提供一个主体来覆盖“MakeNoise”方法。 当您在“Dog”类型的对象上调用“MakeNoise”方法时，“Woof！” 将显示消息。

抽象的优点是什么？

抽象在软件开发中有几个好处：

  • 抽象允许您创建可重用的代码。
  • 抽象使您的代码更高效且更易于维护。
  • 抽象可以使您的代码更加灵活和可扩展。
  • 抽象可以帮助您防止代码重复。
  • 抽象可以使您的代码更易于理解和使用。


原文:

In object-oriented programming, abstraction is a critical concept that allows you to create classes and methods that represent the general concept of an object rather than specific instances of that object. The purpose of this is to hide implementation details from users. This blog will explain what abstraction is, how it is implemented in C# and the advantages of abstraction.

*What is abstraction in object-orientated programming? *

Abstraction in object-orientated programming hides unimportant and complex details from the user and only displays the vital information. This means a user doesn't need to understand the complexities of a program in order to use it; abstraction makes it easy to use complex objects without understanding the underlying implementation details. When you drive a car, you don't need to know how the engine works to get from point A to point B. You can get in the car and turn the key, and the engine will take care of the rest. This is an example of abstraction in the real world. Abstraction is often confused with encapsulation. However, encapsulation and abstraction are two different concepts. Encapsulation is another key concept in object-oriented programming that refers to bundling data with the methods that operate on that data and involves restricting access to the data and methods, which is sometimes referred to as data hiding. Abstraction is a way of hiding the implementation details of an object from the user, whereas encapsulation is a way of hiding the data itself from the user.

How do abstract classes provide abstraction?


An abstract class is a class that cannot be instantiated, which means that you cannot create an object of the abstract class type. An abstract class can, however, be inherited by another class. When an abstract class is inherited, the inheriting class must provide implementations for all of the abstract members of the base class. The result is that the base class hides the implementation details of those members from the inheriting class by effectively adding a layer ( the abstract class) of abstraction.

How to implement abstraction in C#

As mentioned previously, abstraction is implemented in classes and methods. In C#, you can create an abstract class using the keyword "abstract" and it can contain abstract and non-abstract members. An abstract class is a class that cannot be instantiated, meaning you cannot create an object of that type. Abstract methods do not have a body, and they must be implemented in a derived class. The non-abstract methods are methods that have a body, and they can be called without having to implement them in a derived class. In the following example, we have created an abstract class called "Animal" with one abstract method and one non-abstract method:

As you can see, the "Animal" class has an abstract method called "MakeNoise" and a non-abstract method called "Sleep". The "MakeNoise" method does not have a body, so it must be implemented in a derived class. The "Sleep" method has a body, so it can be called without having to implement it in a derived class. You can also create an abstract method in a non-abstract class by using the keyword "abstract ". In the following example, we have created an abstract method called "MakeNoise" in the "Animal" class:

As you can see, the "Animal" class has an abstract method called "MakeNoise" and a non-abstract method called "Sleep". The "MakeNoise" method does not have a body, so it must be implemented in a derived class. The "Sleep" method has a body, so it can be called without having to implement it in a derived class.
When you create an abstract method, you must provide a body for the method in a derived class. You can do this by using the keyword "override ". In the following example, we have created a derived class called "Dog" that overrides the "MakeNoise" method:

As you can see, the "Dog" class overrides the "MakeNoise" method by providing a body for the method. When you call the "MakeNoise" method on an object of type "Dog", the "Woof!" message will be displayed.

What are the advantages of abstraction?

There are several benefits of abstraction in software development:

  • Abstraction allows you to create reusable code.
  • Abstraction makes your code more efficient and easier to maintain.
  • Abstraction can make your code more flexible and extensible.
  • Abstraction can help you prevent code duplication.
  • Abstraction can make your code more understandable and easier to use.
item.author: ajwag

item.title: Jest + Typescript 减去 TS-Jest | Jest + Typescript minus TS-Jest
item.description:
酸性编码器

发表于当您尝试将typescript与jest一起使用时， ts-jest是一个流行的包。

但是最近困扰我的一个问题是，未覆盖的行统计不正确，我的测试覆盖率下降了 10+%。

谷歌搜索后，没有任何有用的东西出现，经过一些故障排除后，我发现是ts-jest导致了问题

所以我决定改用 babel 进行编译

安装依赖项

npm i -D @babel/preset-env @babel/preset-typescript typescript jest

设置 npm 脚本

"scripts" : { "test" : "jest" ,
}

创建一个jest.config.js

 /** @type {import('ts-jest/dist/types').InitialOptionsTsJest} */ module . exports = { testEnvironment : ' node ' , roots : [ ' <rootDir>/src ' ], testMatch : [ ' **/__tests__/**/*.+(ts|js) ' , ' **/?(*.)+(spec|test).+(ts|js) ' ], transform : { ' ^.+ \\ .(js|ts)$ ' : ' babel-jest ' , }, moduleDirectories : [ ' node_modules ' , ' src ' ], collectCoverage : true , collectCoverageFrom : [ ' **/*.{js,ts} ' , ' !**/*.d.ts ' ], }

创建一个babel.config.js

 module . exports = { presets : [ ' @babel/preset-typescript ' , [ ' @babel/preset-env ' , { targets : { node : ' current ' , }, }, ], ], }

你不需要安装babel-jest ，它自带jest

最后npm test和繁荣，你的 100% 测试覆盖率回来了。

Jan Schenk (he/him) - Jan Schenk (他/他) - May 9 5月9日



原文:

Acid Coder

Posted on May 12

ts-jest is a popular package when you try to use typescript with jest.

But one issue that is bugging me recently is, the uncovered lines stat is not correct, my test coverage dropped by 10+%.

After googling, nothing useful come up, and after some troubleshooting, I found out that it was ts-jest that causing the issue

so I decided to use babel for compilation instead

install dependencies

npm i -D @babel/preset-env @babel/preset-typescript typescript jest

setup npm script

"scripts": { "test": "jest",
}

create a jest.config.js

/** @type {import('ts-jest/dist/types').InitialOptionsTsJest} */ module.exports = { testEnvironment: 'node', roots: ['<rootDir>/src'], testMatch: ['**/__tests__/**/*.+(ts|js)', '**/?(*.)+(spec|test).+(ts|js)'], transform: { '^.+\\.(js|ts)$': 'babel-jest', }, moduleDirectories: ['node_modules', 'src'], collectCoverage: true, collectCoverageFrom: ['**/*.{js,ts}', '!**/*.d.ts'],
}

create a babel.config.js

module.exports = { presets: [ '@babel/preset-typescript', [ '@babel/preset-env', { targets: { node: 'current', }, }, ], ],
}

you don't need to install babel-jest, it comes with jest

finally npm test and boom, your 100% test coverage is back.

Jan Schenk (he/him) - May 9
item.author: tylim88

item.title: Padrões criacionais 工厂 | Padrões criacionais Factory
item.description:
工厂
命题
问题
解决方案
埃斯特鲁图拉
实施
参考文献

命题

问题

解决方案

埃斯特鲁图拉

  1. 实施

    abstract class Creator { public abstract factoryMethod (): Product ; public someOperation (): string { const product = this . factoryMethod (); console . log ( product . opertation ()); } }
     class ConcreteCreator1 extends Creator { public factoryMethod (): Product { return new ConcreteProduct1 (); } } class ConcreteCreator2 extends Creator { public factoryMethod (): Product { return new ConcreteProduct2 (); } }

    参考文献

    • https://refactoring.guru/pt-br/design-patterns/factory-method https://refactoring.guru/pt-br/design-patterns/factory-method


原文:

Factory
Propósito
Problema
Solução
Estrutura
Implementação
Referencias

Propósito

Fornecer uma interface para criar objetos em uma superclasse, mas permitir que as subclasses alterem o tipo de objetos que serão criados

Problema

Se você tem um código muito acoplado a uma á uma classe em especifico, caso um dia precise adicionar uma outra classe vai ter problemas, por exemplo, uma empresa de logistica que usa somente caminhões, e o sistema está fortemente acoplado a classe de caminhão, caso algum dia queira ser implementada a classe navio isso vai gerar uma refatoração gigantesca no código

Solução

O padrão factory sugere substituir as chamadas diretas de construção de objetos, nesse caso caminhão para um método factory e ai esse factory vai ficar responsavel por criar e retornar os objetos que nesse caso chamamos de produtos

Nesse exemplo do site refactoring guru, podemos ver que temos uma classe Logistics que contem um método createTransport que é uma factory, as classes RoadLogistics e SeaLogistics implementam essa classe Logistics e modificam o createTransport para retornar um caminhão e um návio respectivamente

Porem existe uma limitação, as subclasses só podem retornar tipos diferentes de produtos caso eles implemente uma mesma interface em comum. Isso ajuda para que quando o cliente faça uma chamada ele apenas precisa saber qual a interface desses produtos e pode tratar todos da mesma forma pois ele sabe que todos vão ter os mesmos métodos

Estrutura

  1. O Produto declara a interface que que os objetos vão implementar

  2. O produto concreto A e B são diferentes implementações da interface Produto

  3. A classe Creator tem um método factory que cria um produto do tipo Produto
    (Apesar do nome a principal responsabilidade da classe creator não é criar produtos)

  4. Criadores concretos sobescrevem a classe Creator para retornar diferente produtos no seus metodos factory

Implementação 

abstract class Creator { public abstract factoryMethod(): Product; public someOperation(): string { const product = this.factoryMethod(); console.log(product.opertation()); }
}

class ConcreteCreator1 extends Creator { public factoryMethod(): Product { return new ConcreteProduct1(); }
} class ConcreteCreator2 extends Creator { public factoryMethod(): Product { return new ConcreteProduct2(); }
}

os concrete creators vão extender o Creator e mudar apenas o método factory

interface Product { operation(): string;
} class ConcreteProduct1 implements Product { public operation(): string { return '{Result of the ConcreteProduct1}'; }
} class ConcreteProduct2 implements Product { public operation(): string { return '{Result of the ConcreteProduct2}'; }
}

function clientCode(creator: Creator) { console.log(creator.someOperation());
}

a partir dai o clientCode precisa apenas receber qualquer classe que extende o creator e ele tem certeza que essa classe vai ter o método someOperation que pode fazer coisas diferentes como por exemplo o no exemplo do barco e caminhão ambos podem ter o método delivery que funcionam de formas diferentes mas isso não import para o clientCode

Referencias

  • https://refactoring.guru/pt-br/design-patterns/factory-method
item.author: yanpiing

item.title: 广告监听器 | ADDEVENTLISTENER
item.description:
阿夫塔布·西杜

发表于可以将任意数量的事件处理程序添加到单个元素，而不会覆盖现有的事件处理程序。

以下是一些最常见的事件类型和事件名称：

  • 鼠标事件：click、dblclick、mousedown、mouseup、contextmenu、mouseout、mousewheel、mouseover。

  • 触摸事件：touchstart、touchend、touchmove、touchcancel

  • 键盘事件：keydown、keyup、keypress。

  • 表单事件：焦点、模糊、更改、提交

  • 窗口事件：调整大小、滚动、加载、卸载、hashchange。

触摸事件在智能手机、平板电脑和触摸屏笔记本电脑等支持触摸的设备上触发。 大多数浏览器和设备都会触发鼠标事件。 键盘事件（keydown，keyup）：

  • 表单事件（更改，提交）：

    • 不可用mako - 迈克尔·安德鲁扎—— May 11 5月11日



原文:

Aftab Sidhu

Posted on May 12

The addEventListener() is an inbuilt function in JavaScript which takes the event to listen for, and a second argument to be called whenever the described event gets fired. Any number of event handlers can be added to a single element without overwriting existing event handlers.

Here are some of the most common event types and event names:

  • Mouse Events: click, dblclick, mousedown, mouseup, contextmenu, mouseout, mousewheel, mouseover.

  • Touch Events: touchstart, touchend, touchmove, touchcancel

  • Keyboard Events: keydown, keyup, keypress.

  • Form Events: focus, blur, change, submit

  • Window Events: resize, scroll, load, unload, hashchange.

Touch events are triggered on touch-enabled devices such as smartphones, tablets, and touch-screen laptops. Mouse events are triggered on the majority of all browsers and devices. The MouseEvent interface represents events that occur due to the user interacting with a pointing device.

The click event: The onclick event occurs when the user clicks on an element.

The dblclick event: ondblclick event occurs when the user double-clicks on an element.

Mousedown & Mouseup events:A pointing device button is pressed/released on an element.

Mouseout event: A pointing device is moved off the element that has the listener attached.

Keyboard events(keydown, keyup):

  • Keydown: Any key is pressed.

  • keyup: Any key is released.

Form Events(change, submit):

  • change: Event that is fired for input, select, and textarea elements when an alteration to the element’s value is done by the user.

  • submit: The submit button is pressed.

notsoavailablemako - May 11

Michael Andreuzza - May 11

item.title: 在开发中使用 Foreman 启动服务 | Using Foreman to start services in development
item.description:
在任何开发环境中，通常都需要启动许多后台服务才能正确编码。

我不喜欢让所有必要的服务一直在我的机器上运行以节省资源，所以我只在需要时才启动它们； 并一个接一个地启动它们，这是太多的工作。

工头来救援！ 例如，每次我开始在项目中编码时，我都需要运行所有这些服务：

  • 雷迪斯
  • 打包程序
  • 导轨服务器
  • 铁路工人

这个过程的自动化很简单。 让我们首先创建一个Procfile并将其保存在项目根目录中。

./Procfile

 postgres: postgres -D /usr/local/var/postgres redis: redis-server /usr/local/etc/redis.conf webpacker: bundle exec bin/webpack-dev-server rails: bundle exec rails server worker: bundle exec rails jobs:work

如您所见，这很容易。 只需遵循这个简单的规则：

 service_name: command to run it

然后输入：

 $ foreman start 20:30:42 postgres.1 | started with pid 42458 20:30:42 rails.1 | started with pid 42459 20:30:42 worker.1 | started with pid 42460 20:30:42 redis.1 | started with pid 42461 20:30:42 webpack.1 | started with pid 42462

你准备好了！

感谢David Dollar提供的这个很棒的宝石和



原文:

In any development environment it's usually needed to start many background services to be able to code properly.

I don't like to keep all necessary services running in my machine all the time to save resources, so I start them only when needed; and starting them one-by-one it's too much work.

Comes Foreman to the rescue! Foreman is a gem (for Ruby, but it already ported to many others languages) that will load a Procfile and start/stop the services configured by demand.

For example, every time I start coding in a project, I need to run all those services:

  • PostgreSQL
  • Redis
  • Webpacker
  • Rails server
  • Rails worker

Automation of this procedure is simple. Lets first create a Procfile and save it on the project root directory.

./Procfile

postgres: postgres -D /usr/local/var/postgres
redis: redis-server /usr/local/etc/redis.conf
webpacker: bundle exec bin/webpack-dev-server
rails: bundle exec rails server
worker: bundle exec rails jobs:work

As you can see, it's easy. Just follow this simple rule:

service_name: command to run it

Then type:

$ foreman start

20:30:42 postgres.1 | started with pid 42458
20:30:42 rails.1    | started with pid 42459
20:30:42 worker.1   | started with pid 42460
20:30:42 redis.1    | started with pid 42461
20:30:42 webpack.1  | started with pid 42462

and you are ready to go!

Thanks David Dollar for this awesome gem and
item.author: shayani

item.title: 如何在 JavaScript 中给句子命名 | How to Titleize a Sentence in JavaScript
item.description:
我们最近在工作中遇到了一个场景，我们经历了重做应用程序中使用的所有电子邮件模板的过程。 我们重新设计了 300 多个模板。 在我们完成后，有人要求我们将主题行更改为标题大小写，就像您在打印标题中所做的那样。 不想手动更改所有 300 多个模板，我编写了这个自动处理该过程的快速函数。

什么是标题案例

乍一看，我们可能希望主题行中每个单词的首字母大写，而单词的其余部分小写。

嗯，这几乎是正确的。 我们不希望某些单词被大写。 像a 、 an or 这样的词and我们希望保持小写。

这是我创建的为我们处理此转换的快速函数。

首先，我创建了一组我们希望在电子邮件主题中保持小写的单词。

 const small_words = ['a', 'an', 'and', 'as', 'at', 'but', 'by', 'for', 'if', 'in', 'into', 'nor', 'of', 'on', 'or', 'out', 'so', 'the', 'to', 'up', 'yet', 'and', 'as', 'at', 'but', 'by', 'for', 'if', 'in', 'into', 'nor', 'of', 'on', 'or', 'out', 'so', 'the', 'to', 'up', 'yet'];

接下来我创建了一个函数，它将主题行的文本传递给它。 我会将这个字符串分解成一个数组，主题中的每个单词都是数组中的一个条目。 现在我有了一个数组，我可以遍历每个单词。 如果单词与small_words数组中的条目匹配，那么我将其转换为小写。 否则，我将单词的第一个字母大写，并将单词的其余部分小写。

我必须考虑一个例外，那就是主题文本中的第一个字母。 不管第一个单词是什么，我都希望它的第一个字母大写。

有了这些要求，这是我的功能：

 const titleize = (title) => { return title.split(' ').map((word, index) => { if (index !== 0 && small_words.includes(word)) { return word.toLowerCase(); } else { return word[0].toUpperCase() + word.slice(1).toLowerCase(); } }).join(' '); }

为了测试这个功能，我添加了 3 个标题和 console.log 新标题。

 console.log(titleize('notes on a scandal')); // Notes on a Scandal console.log(titleize('a good man is hard to find')); // A Good Man Is Hard to Find console.log(titleize('midnight in the garden of good and evil')); // Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil

您将看到主题行的每个测试的输出。

如果您需要根据打印标题格式对字符串进行标题化，则可以使用此功能。 您需要做的就是编辑small_words数组以包含您的单词列表以保持小写。

马克·加西亚激流 - May 11 5月11日



原文:

We recently ran into a scenario at work where we went through the process to redo all the email templates used in our application. There were over 300+ templates that we redesigned. After we finished somebody requested that we change the subject line to be title cased like you would in a print headline. Not wanting to manually change all 300+ templates, I wrote this quick function that handled the process automatically.

What is Title Case

At first glance it might sound like we wanted the first letter of every word in a the subject line to be uppercase and the remainder of the word to be lowercase.

Well that is almost correct. We don't want certain words to be capitalized. Words like a, an or and we wanted to remain lowercase.

Here is the quick function I created that handled this transition for us.

First I created an array of words that we wanted to remain lowercase in the subject of the email.

const small_words = ['a', 'an', 'and', 'as', 'at', 'but', 'by', 'for', 'if', 'in', 'into', 'nor', 'of', 'on', 'or', 'out', 'so', 'the', 'to', 'up', 'yet', 'and', 'as', 'at', 'but', 'by', 'for', 'if', 'in', 'into', 'nor', 'of', 'on', 'or', 'out', 'so', 'the', 'to', 'up', 'yet'];

Next I created a function that would have the text of the subject line passed into it. I would break this string into an array with each word in the subject an entry in the array. Now that I have an array I can loop over every word. If the word matches an entry in the small_words array then I convert it to lowercase. Otherwise I capitalize the first letter of the word and lowercase the remainder of the word.

There is one exception that I have to account for and that is the first letter in the subject text. Regardless of what is the first word I want it to have the first letter capitalized.

With those requirements, here is my function:

const titleize = (title) => {
  return title.split(' ').map((word, index) => {
    if (index !== 0 && small_words.includes(word)) {
      return word.toLowerCase();
    } else {
      return word[0].toUpperCase() + word.slice(1).toLowerCase();
    }
  }).join(' ');
}

To test out the function I added 3 titles and console.log the new title.

console.log(titleize('notes on a scandal'));  // Notes on a Scandal
console.log(titleize('a good man is hard to find')); // A Good Man Is Hard to Find
console.log(titleize('midnight in the garden of good and evil')); // Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil

You will see the output for each test of a subject line.

If you ever need to titleize a string based on print title formatting, you can use this function. All you would need to do is edit the small_words array to include your list of words to remain lowercase.

Dumb Down Demistifying Dev - May 11

Marc Garcia Torrent - May 11
item.author: ratracegrad

item.title: Elixir: Qual a diferençade usar so preload e preload com join? | Elixir: Qual a diferença de usar só preload e preload com join?
item.description:
示例 só com 预加载

from ( p in Post , preload: [ :comments ]) |> Repo . all ()

瓯

Post |> preload ([ :comments ]) |> Repo . all

结果：

蓬托斯观察者：

  1. 瓯

    Post |> join ( :inner , [ p ], c in assoc ( p , :comments )) |> preload ([ :comments ]) |> Repo . all () # ou Post |> join ( :inner , [ p ], c in Comment , on: p . id == c . post_id ) |> preload ([ :comments ]) |> Repo . all ()

    结果：

    蓬托斯观察者：

    1. 结果：

      结论

      Apenas o preload buscará todas as postagens do banco de dados e, em seguida, fará uma consulta separada retornando todos os comentários associados às postagens fornecidas. Apenas o preload buscará todas as postagens do banco de dados e, em seguida, fará uma Consulta separada retornando todos os comentários associados às postagens fornecidas。 Já usando preload com join em vez de emitir uma consulta separada para buscar comentários, o Ecto buscará postagens e comentários em uma única consulta e, em seguida, fará uma passagem separada associando cada comentário à postagem pai. Já usando preload com join em vez de emitir uma Consulta separada para buscar comentários, o Ecto buscará postagens e comentários em uma única Consulta e, em seguida, fará uma Passagem separada associando cada comentário à postagem pai。



原文:

Vamos usar o projeto do post Sintaxes de consulta em Elixir: pipe X palavras-chave para fazermos uns testes com os comentários e observar o comportamento de usar só preload e preload com join.

Vamos buscar os posts com seus comentários:

Exemplo só com preload 

from(p in Post, preload: [:comments]) |> Repo.all()

ou

Post
|> preload([:comments])
|> Repo.all

resultado:

O exemplo acima buscou todas as postagens do banco de dados e, em seguida, fez uma consulta separada retornando todos os comentários associados às postagens fornecidas. Os comentários foram processados ​​e associados a cada post sendo retornados no campo comments.

Pontos a observar:

  1. Nenhum registro apareceu duplicado.
  2. Todos os posts apareceram, mesmo os posts sem comentários.

Exemplo com preload e join

Muitas vezes, você pode querer que postagens e comentários sejam selecionados e filtrados na mesma consulta. Para esses casos, você pode dizer explicitamente que uma junção existente seja pré-carregada no conjunto de resultados:

Repo.all( from p in Post, join: c in assoc(p, :comments), preload: [comments: c] )

ou

Post
|> join(:inner, [p], c in assoc(p, :comments))
|> preload([:comments])
|> Repo.all() # ou Post
|> join(:inner, [p], c in Comment, on: p.id == c.post_id)
|> preload([:comments])
|> Repo.all()

resultado:

resultado similar a uma query usando SQL:

Pontos a observar:

  1. Como o post de id 1 tinha dois comentários, ele apareceu duplicado, similar a consulta com SQL.
  2. Apareceram apenas os posts que possuem comentários.

Podemos corrigir a duplicação usando o distinct:

Repo.all( from p in Post, distinct: true, join: c in assoc(p, :comments), preload: [comments: c] )

Post
|> distinct(true)
|> join(:inner, [p], c in assoc(p, :comments))
|> preload([:comments])
|> Repo.all()

resultado:

Agora temos apenas os posts que possuem comentários.

Conclusão

Apenas o preload buscará todas as postagens do banco de dados e, em seguida, fará uma consulta separada retornando todos os comentários associados às postagens fornecidas. Já usando preload com join em vez de emitir uma consulta separada para buscar comentários, o Ecto buscará postagens e comentários em uma única consulta e, em seguida, fará uma passagem separada associando cada comentário à postagem pai.
item.author: maiquitome

item.title: 我们的开源项目获得了资助！ | Our open-source project got funded!
item.description:
迪玛格罗斯曼

发表于嗨社区，我们有令人兴奋的消息要分享！

我已经写过这个想法的起源，你可以在这里阅读：构建第一个开源通知基础设施。

多亏了这个梦幻般的社区令人难以置信的兴趣，我们的 Github 项目变得流行起来，从 200 颗星增长到 2.2K 星，今天已经超过 3,500 颗星。
我们不能对社区表示足够的感谢。

什么是诺武？

它解决了开发人员从头开始构建和维护通知系统的痛苦。 这可以是一个应用内通知中心（就像你可能从 Facebook 等平台知道的那样），用于通过多个交付渠道发送的交易消息。

我们的目标是解决从摘要引擎到高级工作流管理（包括用户偏好、时区感知等）的大量用例。

最好的部分是——我们在开源社区的难以置信的帮助和参与下构建它。 一起。

最近，我们筹集了 660 万美元的资金。 这意味着我们可以全职构建这个项目。 梦想成真。 它还将使我们能够发展它，并在考虑通知和基于产品的通信时成为开发人员事实上的解决方案。

凭借我们的新资金，Novu 招募了我们的许多贡献者在 Novu 上全职工作，现在正致力于创建最好的 OSS 通知基础设施。

那么自资助以来发生了什么？

  • 我们发布了用于管理通知的管理控制面板 (npx novu init) 耶！。
  • 我们创建了一个新网站，更新了所有新功能。
  • 我们开始与贡献者和资助项目密切合作。
  • 我们赞助了活动。
  • 发布了我们的多环境支持
  • 以 Iframe 或 React 组件的形式发布了我们的应用内通知中心
  • 发布更多供应商和供应商仪表板
  • 添加了新的团队协作和邀请
  • 发布了新的监控和可观察性页面
  • 使用 CLI 创建了一个很棒的注册流程 :)
  • 全部开源！！！

未来？

  • 支持所有可能的通知渠道：Direct、Push 等。
  • 创建用于自动化通知渠道的智能工作流程。
  • 添加摘要功能，使最终用户的生活更加轻松
  • 在我们的应用内和系统中拥有新的用户偏好控制
  • 还有更多
  • 仍然是开源的！ 如果您还没有检查过 Novu，请随时访问我们的 Github 页面
    如果你能写信告诉我你在 Novu 中缺乏什么，我会很高兴，这样我就可以将它添加到我们的想法列表中。
    谢谢你。 没有你我们做不到！



原文:

Dima Grossman

Posted on May 11

Hi Community, We have exciting news to share!

@combarnea and I open-sourced the Novu project because we both felt the pain of building a notification infrastructure from scratch. I have written about the inception of the idea and you can read it here: Building the first open-source notification infrastructure.

Thanks to the incredible interest of this fantastic community, our Github project became trending and grew from 200 stars to 2.2K stars, and today has more than 3,500 stars.
We can’t thank the community enough.

What is Novu?

Novu is the first open-source notifications infrastructure for developers. It solves developers the pain of building from scratch and maintaining their notification systems. That can be an in-app notification center (like you might know from platforms like Facebook) to transactional messages sent over multiple delivery channels.

Our goal is to solve a large number of use-cases from digest engine, to advanced workflows management including user preferences, timezone awareness, and more.

And the best part is - We are building this with the incredible help and involvement of the open-source community. Together.

Lately, we have raised a $6.6m round. This means we can go and build this project full-time. A dream comes true. It will also enable us to grow it and become the de-facto solution for developers when thinking about notifications and product-based communication.

With our new funding, Novu recruited many of our contributors to work full-time on Novu and is now working on creating the best OSS notification infrastructure.

So what happened since the funding?

  • We have released our admin control panel for managing notifications (npx novu init) yay!.
  • We have created a new website, updated with all the new features.
  • We started to work closely with contributors and fund projects.
  • We have sponsored events.
  • Released our multi-environment support
  • Released our In-app notification center as an Iframe or as a React component
  • Released more providers and providers dashboard
  • Added New team collaboration and invites
  • Released a new monitor and observability page
  • Created an awesome registration flow using a CLI :)
  • All in open source!!!

The future?

  • Support every possible notification channel: Direct, Push and others.
  • Create an intelligent workflow for automating your notifications channels.
  • Add digest capabilities to make end-user life so much easier
  • Have a new user preference control within our in-app and as a system
  • Many more to come
  • Still all in open source!! :)

If you haven’t checked Novu yet, feel free to go to our Github page
https://github.com/novuhq/novu

Happy if you can write to me about what you lack in Novu so I can add it to our ideas list.
Thank you. We couldn’t do it without you!
item.author: scopsy

item.title: 介绍迅雷⚡ | Introducing Thunder ⚡
item.description:
试试迅雷⚡

我用

  • 反应
  • 应用程序
  • 反应路由器dom
  • 反应烘烤

我以前使用过 Firebase，但 Appwrite 是新的，而且非常棒。 我的八个朋友迁移到了 Appwrite，他激励我尝试一下。 当我尝试 Appwrite 时，我的想法被震撼了🤯...

您可以只用一行代码创建一个帐户，只需编写几行代码即可创建实时体验。 我的经历太棒了！ 我对这次黑客马拉松感到非常兴奋。 虽然，由于结束 sem 考试，我开始晚了，但我仍然喜欢这段旅程并学到了很多东西。 看来我要在我的进一步项目中使用 Appwrite。

我已经使用了 Appwrite 在此应用程序中提供的大部分功能，例如帐户、用户、数据库、存储、实时等。

数据库

我的数据库包含两个集合和一个存储桶。

收藏品

此集合包含以下属性：

  1. 用户名
  2. 电子邮件
  3. 此集合包含以下属性：

    特征

    • 实时向任何用户发送消息
    • 更新您的个人资料（包括个人资料图片）
    • 删除您不想要的消息
    • 搜索您要聊天的用户

    介绍迅雷 - 实时聊天应用程序试试迅雷演示

    试用应用程序：迅雷

    特征

    让我分享一下迅雷的强大功能。

    • 实时向任何用户发送消息
    • 更新您的个人资料（包括个人资料图片）
    • 删除您不想要的消息
    • 搜索您要聊天的用户

    使用雷霆将您的体验提升到一个新的水平！ 火星任务安装步骤

     更改工作目录

内置





其他资源/信息（截图）

最后，爱你♥ Appwrite 和 Dev。 这个黑客马拉松太棒了！

Thunder is a fully secure real-time chat application built using Appwrite. Try out Thunder ⚡

I built this with

I had used Firebase before but Appwrite was new and it was amazing. Eight of my friends migrated to Appwrite and he motivated me to give it a try. When I tried Appwrite, my mind was blown 🤯...

You can create an account with only a single line of code, you can create real-time experiences by simply writing a few lines. My experience was amazing! And I was really excited for this hackathon. Though, I started it late due to end sem exams but still I enjoyed the journey and learnt a lot of things. It seems like I am gonna use Appwrite for my further projects.

item.title: 使用 Go 实现健壮的内存缓存 | Implementing robust in-memory cache with Go
item.description:
维亚切斯拉夫·波图拉耶夫

发表于您需要处理并发更新、错误缓存、故障转移处理、后台更新、过期抖动和缓存预热传输。

故事

缓存是提高性能的最有效技术之一，因为摆脱任务的最快方法是跳过它。 不幸的是，缓存不是灵丹妙药，在某些情况下，由于事务性/一致性约束，您无法重用任务的结果。 众所周知，缓存失效是计算机科学中的两个难题之一。

最好是域对不可变数据进行操作，因此不需要缓存失效。 在这种情况下，缓存通常是一种净收益。 但是，如果需要保持可变数据同步，则缓存失效是必要的。
最简单的策略是根据生存时间 (TTL) 使缓存无效。 即使与基于事件的失效相比看起来不太合适，也要考虑简单性和可移植性。 事件并不能保证及时传递，在最坏的情况下（例如，如果事件代理暂时停机或过载）事件可能比 TTL 更不精确。

短 TTL 通常是性能和一致性之间的良好折衷。 它将减少作为数据源障碍的繁忙流量下的负载。 对于低流量的影响可以忽略不计。

演示应用

让我们从一个简单的演示应用程序开始。 它将接收带有查询参数的 URL，并以由这些参数确定的 JSON 对象进行响应。 独特的结果将存储在数据库中，以使事情变得非常缓慢。

我们将使用自定义plt给应用程序增加一些负载。

自定义plt有额外的参数：

  • 这样的命令将启动一个客户端，该客户端将在循环中发送 10000 个不同的 URL，尝试通过使用多达 200 个并发请求来保持每秒 5000 个请求的速率。 每个 URL 将分批发送 100 个请求，以模拟单个资源的并发性。

    它将显示实时性能统计数据和总体结果。

    演示应用程序具有三种由CACHE环境变量控制的操作模式：

    • 应用程序可在github.com/vearutop/cache-story 获得。
      如果您想自己尝试一下，可以使用make start-deps run启动它。
      它依赖于docker-compose来启动数据库、prometheus、grafana ( http://localhost:3001 ) 和 jaeger ( http://localhost:16686/ )。 您可以稍后使用make stop-deps停止依赖关系。

      在我的机器上，我能够在没有缓存的情况下达到约 500 RPS。 在 ~130 个并发请求之后，数据库开始因Too many connections阻塞。 这样的结果并不好，也不可怕，但看起来像是一个改进的机会。
      让我们看看借助缓存可以实现什么。

      使用advanced缓存，同一台笔记本电脑能够显示这些结果（大约 60 倍的吞吐量和更低的延迟）。


       go run ./cmd/cplt --cardinality 10000 --group 100 --live-ui --duration 10h curl --concurrency 100 -X 'GET' 'http://127.0.0.1:8008/hello?name=World&locale=ru-RU' -H 'accept: application/json'
       Requests per second: 25064.03 Successful requests: 15692019 Time spent: 10m26.078s Request latency percentiles: 99%: 28.22ms 95%: 13.87ms 90%: 9.77ms 50%: 2.29ms

      字节 VS 结构

      哪一个更好？

      这取决于用例，字节缓存（或将数据存储为[]byte ）具有一些优点：

      • 它授予不变性，因为您每次需要时都需要解码一个新值，
      • 由于碎片较少，它通常占用较少的内存，
      • 它对垃圾收集器更友好，因为没有什么可以遍历的，
      • 它可以很容易地通过电线发送，因为它正是电线所期望的，
      • 允许精确的内存限制，字节数很容易计算。

      主要缺点是编码和解码的成本。 在热循环中，它可能变得非常昂贵。

      结构优势：

      • 无需每次需要时都对值进行编码/解码，
      • 更好的表现力，因为您可以潜在地缓存无法序列化的内容，

      结构缓存的缺点：

      • 可变性，因为您多次重复使用相同的值，所以很容易在无意中更改它，
      • 内存使用，结构占用相对稀疏的内存区域，
      • 垃圾收集器压力，如果你有大量长寿命结构，GC 可能会花费大量时间遍历它们并证明它们仍在使用中，
      • 几乎不可能对缓存实例施加总内存限制，动态大小的项目与其他所有内容一起存储在堆上。

      在本文中，我们将使用结构缓存。

      朴素缓存

      最简单的内存缓存是由 mutex 保护的map 。 当你需要一个键的值时，你首先检查它是否在缓存中并且没有过期。 如果不是，则从数据源构建它并将其放入缓存中。

      然后将值返回给调用者。

这个逻辑很简单，但它有一些可能导致关键问题的缺点。

并发更新

当多个调用者同时错过同一个键时，他们都会尝试构建该值。 这可能导致死锁或缓存踩踏故障导致资源耗尽。

此外，所有试图建立价值的调用者都会有额外的延迟。
如果其中一些构建失败，即使缓存中可能存在有效值，父调用者也会失败。

可以通过使用低基数和高分组来模拟该问题，以便一次发送许多类似的请求。

 go run ./cmd/cplt --cardinality 100 --group 1000 --live-ui --duration 10h --rate-limit 5000 curl --concurrency 150 -X 'GET' 'http://127.0.0.1:8008/hello?name=World&locale=ru-RU' -H 'accept: application/json'

此图表显示应用程序以naive缓存启动，然后在蓝色标记上以advanced缓存重新启动。 如您所见，密钥锁定会对性能（请注意Incoming Request Latency ）和资源使用（请注意DB Operation Rate ）产生重大影响。

解决方案可能是阻止并行构建，以便一次只进行一个构建。 但是，如果有许多并发调用者要求各种密钥，这将受到争用。

更好的解决方案是锁定每个键的构建，以便调用者之一获得锁并拥有构建，而所有其他调用者等待该值。

背景更新

当缓存条目过期时，它需要一个新值，构建新值可能会很慢。 如果我们同步执行，我们将减慢尾部延迟（99+ 百分位）。 对于需求量很大的缓存条目，提前开始构建是可行的，甚至在值过期之前也是如此。 如果我们可以为过期值提供一定程度的陈旧性，它也可以工作。

在这种情况下，我们可以立即提供陈旧/即将过期的值并在后台开始更新。 这里需要注意的一点是，如果构建依赖于父上下文，那么在我们提供过时的值之后（例如，当父 HTTP 请求被满足时），上下文可能会立即被取消。 如果我们使用这样的上下文来访问数据库，我们会得到一个context canceled错误。
此问题的解决方案是从父上下文中“分离”上下文并忽略父取消。

另一种策略可能是在没有父请求的情况下主动重建即将过期的缓存条目，但这可能会由于保留任何人都不感兴趣的过时缓存条目而导致资源耗尽。

到期同步

想象一下，我们启动一个启用了 TTL 缓存的新实例，缓存是空的，几乎每个请求都会导致缓存未命中和值创建。 这将增加数据源上的负载并存储过期时间非常接近的缓存条目。 一旦 TTL 过去，大部分缓存条目将几乎同时过期，这将导致新的负载峰值。 更新的值将再次接近到期时间，并且情况将重复。

这是热缓存条目的常见问题。

最终缓存条目将不同步，但这可能需要一段时间。

解决此问题的方法是通过在过期时间上添加抖动来中断同步。
如果过期抖动为 10% (0.1)，则意味着 TTL 将在0.95 * TTL到1.05 * TTL之间变化。 即使是这么小的抖动也已经有助于减少过期同步。

这是一个示例，我们正在向服务推送高基数和高并发的负载。 它将需要在短时间内提供许多条目，足以形成过期峰值。

 go run ./cmd/cplt --cardinality 10000 --group 1 --live-ui --duration 10h --rate-limit 5000 curl --concurrency 200 -X 'GET' 'http://127.0.0.1:8008/hello?name=World&locale=ru-RU' -H 'accept: application/json'

图表从不做任何事情来避免同步的naive缓存开始，第二个标记表示使用advanced缓存重新启动服务，在过期时间中添加了 10% 的抖动。 尖峰更宽更短，下降更快，整体服务稳定性更好。

错误缓存

当价值构建失败时，最简单的事情就是将该错误返回给调用者并忘记它。
这可能会导致严重的问题。

例如，您的服务运行良好并借助缓存处理 10K RPS，但突然缓存构建开始失败（可能是由于临时数据库过载、网络问题或什至可能是逻辑错误，如验证失败）。
此时所有 10K RPS（而不是通常的 100 RPS）都将直接命中数据源，因为没有缓存可供服务。

轻微的临时中断将呈指数级升级。

对于高负载系统，使用短 TTL 缓存故障以避免级联故障非常重要。

故障转移模式

有时提供过时的值比返回错误更好。 特别是如果过时的值最近过期并且它仍然很有可能等于最新的值。

故障转移模式有助于以准确性为代价提高弹性，这在分布式系统中通常是一个公平的权衡。

缓存传输

当缓存有相关数据时，它的效果最好。 当应用程序的新实例启动时，缓存为空。

填充有用的数据需要时间，在此期间缓存效率可能会显着降低。

有几种方法可以解决“冷”缓存问题。

您可以通过迭代假定有用的数据来预热缓存。 例如，您可以获取数据库表的最新内容并将它们存储在缓存中。 这种方法很复杂，而且并不总是有效的。 您需要决定使用哪些数据并使用一段定制代码重建缓存条目。

这可能会给数据库（或其他数据源）带来过多的负载。

您还可以通过使用共享的缓存实例（如 redis 或 memcached）来避免此问题。 它还有另一个问题，通过网络读取数据比从本地内存慢得多。 此外，网络带宽可能成为可扩展性瓶颈。

有线数据需要反序列化，这会增加延迟和资源使用。

解决此问题的简单方法是将缓存从活动实例转移到新启动的实例。 活动实例的缓存数据自然具有很高的相关性，因为它是响应实际用户请求而填充的。

传输缓存不需要重建数据，不会滥用数据源。

通常生产系统有多个并行运行的应用程序实例。
在部署过程中，这些实例会按顺序重新启动，因此始终有一个处于活动状态且具有高质量缓存的实例。

它有助于以最小的努力通过网络传输数据。 限制是它基于反射并且需要数据具有导出字段。

缓存传输的另一个警告是，不同版本的应用程序可能具有不一定兼容的不同数据结构。 为了缓解此问题，您可以对缓存结构进行指纹识别（使用反射）并在出现差异时中止传输。

这是一个示例实现。
 // RecursiveTypeHash hashes type of value recursively to ensure structural match. func recursiveTypeHash ( t reflect . Type , h hash . Hash64 , met map [ reflect . Type ] bool ) { for { if t . Kind () != reflect . Ptr { break } t = t . Elem () } if met [ t ] { return } met [ t ] = true switch t . Kind () { case reflect . Struct : for i := 0 ; i < t . NumField (); i ++ { f := t . Field ( i ) // Skip unexported field. if f . Name != "" && ( f . Name [ 0 : 1 ] == strings . ToLower ( f . Name [ 0 : 1 ])) { continue } if ! f . Anonymous { _ , _ = h . Write ([] byte ( f . Name )) } recursiveTypeHash ( f . Type , h , met ) } case reflect . Slice , reflect . Array : recursiveTypeHash ( t . Elem (), h , met ) case reflect . Map : recursiveTypeHash ( t . Key (), h , met ) recursiveTypeHash ( t . Elem (), h , met ) default : _ , _ = h . Write ([] byte ( t . String ())) } }

可以使用 HTTP 或任何其他合适的协议进行传输。 在这个例子中，我们将使用 HTTP，服务于/debug/transfer-cache 。 请注意，缓存可能包含敏感信息，不应公开。

对于这个示例，我们可以借助在不同端口上服务的应用程序的单独实例来执行传输。

 CACHE_TRANSFER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8008/debug/transfer-cache HTTP_LISTEN_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8009 go run main.go
 2022-05-09T02:33:42.871+0200 INFO cache/http.go:282 cache restored {"processed": 10000, "elapsed": "12.963942ms", "speed": "39.564084 MB/s", "bytes": 537846} 2022-05-09T02:33:42.874+0200 INFO brick/http.go:66 starting server, Swagger UI at http://127.0.0.1:8009/docs 2022-05-09T02:34:01.162+0200 INFO cache/http.go:175 cache dump finished {"processed": 10000, "elapsed": "12.654621ms", "bytes": 537846, "speed": "40.530944 MB/s", "name": "greetings", "trace.id": "31aeeb8e9e622b3cd3e1aa29fa3334af", "transaction.id": "a0e8d90542325ab4"}

此图表显示应用程序在蓝色标记处重新启动，最后两个是使用缓存传输完成的。 您可以看到性能没有受到影响，而当没有缓存传输时，会产生显着的预热损失。

一个不太明显的好处是缓存也可以转移到开发人员机器上的本地实例，这有助于更轻松地重现和调试生产问题。

锁竞争和低级性能

基本上每个缓存实现都充当具有并发（主要是读取）访问的键的值映射。

在许多情况下，低级别的性能问题可以被认为是无关紧要的。 例如，如果您使用内存缓存来支持您的 HTTP API，那么即使是使用 map 和 mutex 的最简单的解决方案也可能就足够了。 这是因为为 HTTP 服务而执行的 IO 操作比内存操作慢得多（即使有同步惩罚）。 记住这一点很重要，以避免过早的优化和不合理的复杂性。

如果依赖内存缓存的应用程序主要受 CPU 限制，则锁争用可能会在整体性能中变得很重要。

锁争用的问题是并发读取和写入必须同步以避免数据冲突。 在单个互斥体的情况下，这种同步将限制一次只能进行一个操作。 这意味着具有多核的现代 CPU 将无法使用所有容量。

对于主要读取的工作负载，标准sync.Map提供了出色的性能，但是，它会随着写入次数的增加而降低。 由于Cache-Line Hash Table (CLHT) 数据结构， github.com/puzpuzpuz/xsync.Map sync.Map

另一种减少锁争用的流行方法是映射分片（ fastcache 、 bigcache 、 bool64/cache ），当值基于键确定性地分布在单独的存储桶中时。 它在简单性和性能之间取得了很好的平衡。

内存管理

内存是一种有限的资源，因此缓存不应无限增长。

过期的项目最终应该从缓存中删除。 这可以同步或在后台完成。 后台管理员是个好主意，因为它不会阻塞应用程序。

此外，后台管理员可以配置为延迟删除项目，以便过期值仍可用作故障转移备份。

删除过期项目可能不足以控制内存使用。
有不同的策略来决定驱逐哪些项目。
在决定策略时，您应该在 CPU/内存使用率和命中/未命中率之间找到平衡点。 最终，驱逐的目标是优化命中/未命中率，同时保持可接受的性能预算，因此这是您在评估驱逐策略时需要考虑的指标。

以下是一些挑选物品的流行标准：

  • 最不常用（LFU），相对昂贵，因为它需要维护应该在每次访问时更新的计数器，
  • 最近最少使用（LRU），相对昂贵，因为它需要在每次访问时更新项目时间戳或重新排序键列表，
  • 先进先出 (FIFO)，相对便宜，因为它可以使用创建项目缓存后填充的值，
  • 随机项目，性能最高，不需要任何排序，最不准确。

我建议先评估 FIFO 和随机驱逐，如果证明对命中/未命中率有显着影响，则仅投资 LRU/LFU。

既然您对如何选择驱逐策略有了一个很好的想法，下一个问题是“应该什么时候驱逐多少项目？”。

这是[]byte缓存的一个微不足道的问题，大多数实现都为您提供了对要使用多少内存的精确控制。

这个问题对于结构缓存来说很棘手。 没有可行和可靠的方法来确定特定结构在应用程序执行期间对堆内存使用的影响，这些信息可能对 GC 可用，但对应用程序本身不可用。

两个不太精确但仍然有用的指标是：

  • 缓存中的项目总数，
  • 整个应用程序的总内存使用量。

这两个指标都可以在应用程序执行期间轻松获得，然后导致驱逐。 由于这些指标与缓存内存使用量不是线性成比例的，因此它们不能用于计算要驱逐的项目的确切列表。

一旦触发驱逐，一个适当的响应是驱逐一部分可配置的项目（例如 10% 的项目）。

缓存数据的堆影响很大程度上取决于映射实现。
从下面的基准测试中可以看出，与高效的二进制序列化（未压缩）相比， map[string]struct{...}的内存消耗可能是 4 倍。

基线基准

这是存储 1M 小结构（ struct { int, bool, string } ）并以 10% 和 0.1% 写入访问它们的基准基准。 字节缓存使用结构的二进制解码器/编码器进行检测。

 goos: darwin goarch: amd64 cpu: Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9750H CPU @ 2.60GHz
 name MB/inuse time/op (10%) time/op (0.1%) sync.Map 192 ± 0% 142ns ± 4% 29.8ns ±10% // Great for read-heavy workloads. shardedMap 196 ± 0% 53.3ns ± 3% 28.4ns ±11% mutexMap 182 ± 0% 226ns ± 3% 207ns ± 1% rwMutexMap 182 ± 0% 233ns ± 2% 67.8ns ± 2% // RWMutex perf degrades with more writes. shardedMapOf 181 ± 0% 50.3ns ± 3% 27.3ns ±13% ristretto 346 ± 0% 167ns ± 8% 54.1ns ± 4% // Failed to keep full working set, ~7-15% of the items are evicted. xsync.Map 380 ± 0% 31.4ns ± 9% 22.0ns ±14% // Fastest, but a bit hungry for memory. patrickmn 184 ± 0% 373ns ± 1% 72.6ns ± 5% bigcache 340 ± 0% 75.8ns ± 8% 72.9ns ± 3% // Byte cache. freecache 333 ± 0% 98.1ns ± 0% 77.8ns ± 2% // Byte cache. fastcache 44.9 ± 0% 60.6ns ± 8% 64.1ns ± 5% // A true champion for memory usage, while having decent performance.

如果序列化不是问题， fastcache因其出色的内存使用率而成为最佳选择。

对于 CPU 密集型应用程序， xsync.Map提供了最佳性能。

此外，字节缓存并不一定意味着有效的内存使用，如bigcache (ha!) 和freecache 。

开发者友好度

我们编写的程序并不总是像我们希望的那样运行。 复杂性会导致难以调试的意外问题。
不幸的是，缓存使这个问题变得更糟。 这就是为什么使缓存对开发人员友好特别重要的原因。

缓存可能由于多种原因而中毒，应该可以快速安全地清理它。 为此，请考虑使用特殊控件来使所有缓存项无效。 重负载下失效并不一定意味着“删除”，如果一次性删除所有缓存，数据源会承受过大的负载，可能会失败。

更温和的失效是所有具有后台更新的项目“过期”，同时提供陈旧的数据。

如果有人正在调查特定的数据源问题，缓存条目可能会过时并且会误导。 为特定请求禁用缓存很方便，这样可以排除缓存不准确的情况。 这可以通过一个特殊的标头来实现，然后在中间件中进行上下文检测。 请注意，公共用户不应使用此类控件，因为它们可用于 DOS 攻击。

对缓存操作/状态的日志和指标的精细控制也很重要。

最后，既然 Go 引入了类型参数（泛型），就可以将类型安全的 API 用于缓存接口并减轻编译器的更多负担。

毛里西奥·林哈雷斯 - Feb 16 2月16日



原文:

Viacheslav Poturaev

Posted on May 12

TL;DR In-memory caching is a great way to improve performance and resiliency of an application at cost of memory and delayed data consistency. You need to take care of concurrent updates, error caching, failover handling, background updates, expiration jitter and cache warmup with transfer.

The Story

Caching is one of the most efficient techniques to improve performance, because the fastest way to get rid of a task is skipping it. Unfortunately caching is not a silver bullet, in some cases you can not afford reusing result of a task due to transactionality/consistency constraints. Cache invalidation is notoriously one of two hard things in Computer Science.

It is best when domain operates on immutable data and so cache invalidation is not necessary. In such case cache is usually a net benefit. However, if there are requirements to keep mutable data in sync, cache invalidation is necessary.
The simplest strategy is to invalidate cache based on time to live (TTL). Even if seems like a bad fit compared to event-based invalidation, consider simplicity and portability. Events do not guarantee timely delivery, in worst case scenarios (for example if event broker is temporary down or overloaded) events could be even less precise than TTL.

Short TTL is often a good compromise between performance and consistency. It would reduce the load under heavy traffic acting as a barrier to the data source. For the low traffic impact would be negligible.

Demo Application

Let's start with a simple demo application. It will receive URL with query parameters and respond with a JSON object determined by those parameters. Unique results will be stored in database to make things realistically slow.

We're going to put some load on the application with a custom plt.

Custom plt has additional parameters:

  • cardinality - number of unique URLs to be generated, this affects cache hit rate,
  • group - number of requests with similar URL being sent at once, this imitates concurrent access to the same key. 
go run ./cmd/cplt --cardinality 10000 --group 100 --live-ui --duration 10h --rate-limit 5000 curl --concurrency 200 -X 'GET'   'http://127.0.0.1:8008/hello?name=World&locale=ru-RU'   -H 'accept: application/json'

Such a command will start a client that will send 10000 different URLs in the loop, trying keep rate of 5000 requests per second by using up to 200 concurrent requests. Every URLs would be sent in a batch of 100 requests to imitate concurrency on a single resource.

It will show live performance statistics and overall results.

Demo app has three modes of operation controlled by CACHE environment variable:

  • none - no caching, all requests are served with involvement of the database,
  • naive - naive caching with a simple map and TTL of 3 minutes,
  • advanced - caching using github.com/bool64/cache library that implements a number of features to improve performance and resiliency, TTL is also 3 minutes.

Application is available at github.com/vearutop/cache-story.
If you would like to experiment yourself with it, you can start it with make start-deps run.
It depends on docker-compose to spin up database, prometheus, grafana (http://localhost:3001) and jaeger (http://localhost:16686/). You can stop dependencies with make stop-deps later.

On my machine I was able to achieve ~500 RPS with no cache. After ~130 concurrent requests DB starts choking with Too many connections. Such result is not great, not terrible, but looks like an improvement opportunity.
Let's see what we can achieve with help of caching.

With advanced cache same laptop was able to show these results (roughly 60x throughput with lower latency).


go run ./cmd/cplt --cardinality 10000 --group 100 --live-ui --duration 10h curl --concurrency 100 -X 'GET'   'http://127.0.0.1:8008/hello?name=World&locale=ru-RU'   -H 'accept: application/json'

Requests per second: 25064.03
Successful requests: 15692019
Time spent: 10m26.078s

Request latency percentiles:
99%: 28.22ms
95%: 13.87ms
90%: 9.77ms
50%: 2.29ms

Bytes VS Structures

Which one is better?

That depends on the use case, byte cache (or storing data as []byte) have some advantages:

  • it grants immutability, because you'll need to decode a new value every time you need it,
  • it generally takes less memory, because of less fragmentation,
  • it is more friendly to garbage collector, because there is nothing to traverse through,
  • it can be easily sent over the wire, because it is exactly what wire expects,
  • allows precise memory limit, bytes are so easy to count.

Main disadvantage is the cost of encoding and decoding. In hot loops it can become prohibitively expensive.

Advantages of structures:

  • no need to encode/decode a value every time you need it,
  • better expressiveness as you can potentially cache things that can not be serialized,

Disadvantages of structure cache:

  • mutability, because you reuse same value multiple times it is quite easy to change it without intention,
  • memory usage, structures take relatively sparse areas of memory,
  • garbage collector pressure, if you have a large set of long-living structures, GC may spend significant time traversing them and proving they are still in use,
  • nearly impossible to impose a total memory limit on a cache instance, dynamically-sized items are stored on the heap together with everything else.

In this article we will use structure cache.

Naive Cache

The simplest in-memory cache is a map guarded by a mutex. When you need a value for a key, you first check if it's in the cache and not expired. If it's not, you build it from the data source and put it in the cache.

Then you return the value to the caller.

This logic is simple, but it has some drawbacks that may lead to critical issues.

Concurrent Updates

When multiple callers simultaneously miss the same key, they will all try to build the value. This can lead to a deadlock or to resource exhaustion with cache stampede failure.

Additionally, there will be extra latency for all the callers that would try to build the value.
If some of those builds fail, parent callers will fail even though there might be a valid value in the cache.

The issue can be simulated by using low cardinality with high grouping, so that many similar requests are sent at once.

go run ./cmd/cplt --cardinality 100 --group 1000 --live-ui --duration 10h --rate-limit 5000 curl --concurrency 150 -X 'GET'   'http://127.0.0.1:8008/hello?name=World&locale=ru-RU'   -H 'accept: application/json'

This chart shows application started with naive cache and then, on the blue marker it was restarted with advanced cache. As you can see key locking can have a significant impact on performance (mind Incoming Request Latency) and resource usage (mind DB Operation Rate).

The solution could be to block parallel builds, so that only one build is in progress at a time. But this would suffer from contention if there are many concurrent callers asking for a variety of keys.

A better solution is to lock the builds per key, so that one of the callers acquires the lock and owns the build, while all the others wait for the value.

Background Updates

When cached entry expires it needs a new value, building new value can be slow. If we do it synchronously, we'll slow down tail latency (99+ percentile). For cache entries that are in high demand it is feasible to start the build in advance, even before the value is expired. It can also work if we can afford some level of staleness for the expired value.

In such case we can immediately serve stale/soon-to-be-expired value and start update in background. One caveat here is that if build depends on parent context, the context may be cancelled right after we served stale value (for example when parent HTTP request was fulfilled). If we use such context to access database, we'll get a context canceled error.
Solution for this problem is to "detach" the context from the parent context and ignore parent cancellation.

Another strategy might be to proactively rebuild cached entries that are soon to be expired, without a parent request, but this may lead to resource exhaustion due to keeping obsolete cache entries that are of no interest to anybody.

Expiration Sync

Imagine situation that we start a new instance with TTL cache enabled, cache is empty and almost every request leads to cache miss and value creation. This will spike the load on the data source and store cached entries with very close expiration time. Once TTL have passed, the majority of cached entries will expire almost simultaneously, this will lead to a new load spike. Updated values will have close expiration time again and situation will repeat.

This is a common problem for hot cache entries.

Eventually cache entries will come out of sync, but this may take a while.

Solution to this problem is to break the sync by adding jitter to the expiration time.
If expiration jitter is 10% (0.1) it means TTL will vary from 0.95 * TTL to 1.05 * TTL. Even such a small jitter will already help to reduce expiration synchronization.

Here is an example, we're pushing load with high cardinality and high concurrency on the service. It will require many entries to be available in short period of time, enough to form an expiration spike.

go run ./cmd/cplt --cardinality 10000 --group 1 --live-ui --duration 10h --rate-limit 5000 curl --concurrency 200 -X 'GET' 'http://127.0.0.1:8008/hello?name=World&locale=ru-RU' -H 'accept: application/json'

The chart starts with naive cache that does not do anything to avoid the sync, second marker indicates service restart with advanced cache that has 10% jitter added to the expiration time. Spikes are wider and shorter and fall faster, overall service stability is better.

Errors Caching

When value build fails the easiest thing to do is just return that error to the caller and forget about it.
This can lead to severe issues.

For example, your service works well and handles 10K RPS with help of cache, but suddenly cache builds start to fail (be it because of temporary database overload, network issue or maybe even logical error like failing validation).
At this point all 10K RPS (instead of usual 100 RPS) will hit data source directly because there will be no cache to serve.

Minor temporary outage would escalate exponentially.

For high load systems it is very important to cache failures with short TTL to avoid cascading failures.

Failover Mode

Sometimes serving obsolete value is better than returning error. Especially if obsolete value expired recently and there is still high chance that it is equal to an up-to-date value.

Failover mode helps to improve resiliency at cost of accuracy, which is often a fair tradeoff in distributed systems.

Cache Transfer

Cache works best when it has relevant data. When a new instance of application is started, cache is empty.

Populating helpful data takes time, during this time cache efficiency may degrade significantly.

There are a few ways to work around the issue of "cold" cache.

You can warm up the cache by iterating over the data that is assumed to be useful. For example, you can fetch recent contents of a database table and store them in cache. This approach is complex and not always effective. You need to decide what data to use and rebuild cache entries with a piece of bespoke code.

This may put excessive load on the database (or other sources of data).

You can also avoid this issue by using a shared instance of cache, like redis or memcached. It has another issue, reading data over the network is much slower, than from local memory. Also, network bandwidth may become a scalability bottleneck.

Wired data needs to be deserialized which adds on latency and resource usage.

The simple solution to this problem is to transfer cache from active instance to the newly started. Cached data of active instance naturally has high relevance, because it was populated in response to actual user requests.

Transferring cache does not need to rebuild data and so it won't abuse data sources.

Usually production systems have multiple instances of application running in parallel.
During deployment, these instances are restarted sequentially, so there is always an instance that is active and has high quality cache.

Go has a built-in binary serialization format encoding/gob. It helps to transfer data over the wire with minimal effort. The limitation is that it is based on reflection and needs data to have exported fields.

Another caveat with cache transfer is that different versions of application may have different data structures that are not necessarily compatible. To mitigate this issue, you can fingerprint cached structures (using reflection) and abort transfer in case of discrepancy.

Here is a sample implementation. 
// RecursiveTypeHash hashes type of value recursively to ensure structural match.
func recursiveTypeHash(t reflect.Type, h hash.Hash64, met map[reflect.Type]bool) { for { if t.Kind() != reflect.Ptr { break } t = t.Elem() } if met[t] { return } met[t] = true switch t.Kind() { case reflect.Struct: for i := 0; i < t.NumField(); i++ { f := t.Field(i) // Skip unexported field. if f.Name != "" && (f.Name[0:1] == strings.ToLower(f.Name[0:1])) { continue } if !f.Anonymous { _, _ = h.Write([]byte(f.Name)) } recursiveTypeHash(f.Type, h, met) } case reflect.Slice, reflect.Array: recursiveTypeHash(t.Elem(), h, met) case reflect.Map: recursiveTypeHash(t.Key(), h, met) recursiveTypeHash(t.Elem(), h, met) default: _, _ = h.Write([]byte(t.String())) }
}

Transfer can be done with HTTP or any other suitable protocol. In this example, we will use HTTP, served at /debug/transfer-cache. Please be aware that cache may contain sensitive information and should not have exposure to public.

For sake of this example, we can perform transfer with help of a separate instance of an application serving on a different port.

CACHE_TRANSFER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8008/debug/transfer-cache HTTP_LISTEN_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8009 go run main.go

2022-05-09T02:33:42.871+0200    INFO    cache/http.go:282       cache restored  {"processed": 10000, "elapsed": "12.963942ms", "speed": "39.564084 MB/s", "bytes": 537846}
2022-05-09T02:33:42.874+0200    INFO    brick/http.go:66        starting server, Swagger UI at http://127.0.0.1:8009/docs
2022-05-09T02:34:01.162+0200    INFO    cache/http.go:175       cache dump finished     {"processed": 10000, "elapsed": "12.654621ms", "bytes": 537846, "speed": "40.530944 MB/s", "name": "greetings", "trace.id": "31aeeb8e9e622b3cd3e1aa29fa3334af", "transaction.id": "a0e8d90542325ab4"}

This chart shows application restarts at blue markers, last two are made with cache transfer. You can see that performance remains unaffected, while when there is no cache transfer there is a significant warmup penalty.

A less obvious benefit is that cache can also be transferred to a local instance on developer machine, this can help to reproduce and debug production issues much easier.

Lock Contention And Low-level Performance

Essentially every cache implementation acts as a map of values by keys with concurrent (mostly read) access.

Low-level performance concerns can be considered irrelevant in many cases. For example, if you use in-memory cache to power your HTTP API, even the simplest solution with map and mutex will likely be sufficient. That is because IO operations that are performed to serve HTTP are way slower than memory operations (even with synchronization penalties). This is important to keep in mind to avoid premature optimizations and unjustified complexity.

If the application that relies on in-memory cache is mostly CPU-bound, lock contention may become significant in overall performance.

Problem with lock contention is that concurrent reads and writes have to be synchronized in order to avoid data conflicts. In case of single mutex such synchronization would impose a limitation of only one operation at a time. This means modern CPUs with multiple cores would not be able to use all the capacity.

For mostly-read workloads standard sync.Map offers great performance, however, it degrades with more writes. There is a another custom implementation that outperforms sync.Map thanks to Cache-Line Hash Table (CLHT) data structure: github.com/puzpuzpuz/xsync.Map.

Another popular approach to reduce lock contention is map sharding (fastcache, bigcache, bool64/cache), when values are deterministically distributed in separate buckets based on keys. It has a good balance of simplicity and performance.

Memory Management

Memory is a limited resource, so cache should not grow indefinitely.

Expired items should eventually be removed from the cache. This can be done synchronously or in background. Background janitor is a good idea, because it will not block the application.

Also, background janitor can be configured to remove items with a delay, so that expired value is still available as a failover backup.

Removing expired items might be not enough to keep memory usage under control.
There are different strategies to decide which items to evict.
When deciding on a strategy you should find a balance between CPU/memory usage and hit/miss ratio. Ultimately, goal of eviction is to optimize hit/miss ratio while remaining in acceptable performance budget, so this is the metric you need to look at when evaluating eviction strategies.

Here are some popular criteria to pick an item:

  • least-frequently used (LFU), relatively expensive as it needs to maintain counters that should be updated on every access,
  • least-recently used (LRU), relatively expensive as it needs to update item timestamps or reorder the list of keys on every access,
  • first in first out (FIFO), relatively cheap as it can use values that are populated once item cache is created,
  • random item, most performant, does not need any kinds of sorting, the least accurate.

I would suggest to evaluate FIFO and random eviction first, and only invest in LRU/LFU if it has proven significant impact on hit/miss ratio.

Now that you have a good idea about how to pick an eviction strategy, next question is "when and how many items should be evicted?".

This is a trivial question for []byte cache, most of the implementations provide you precise control over how much memory to use.

The question is tricky for structure cache. There is no feasible and reliable way to determine impact of a particular structure on the heap memory usage during application execution, this information may be available to GC, but not to the application itself.

Two less precise, but still useful, metrics are:

  • total count of items in the cache,
  • total memory usage of the whole application.

Both metrics can be easily obtained during application execution, and then lead to eviction. Because these metrics are not linearly proportional to cache memory usage, they can not be used to calculate the exact list of items to evict.

An appropriate response would be to evict a configurable fraction of the items (for example 10% of the items), once eviction is triggered.

Heap impact of cached data largely depends on mapping implementation.
As you can see from the benchmark below, map[string]struct{...} may have 4x memory consumption compared to efficient binary serialization (uncompressed).

Baseline Benchmark

Here is a baseline benchmark for storing 1M small structures (struct { int, bool, string }) and accessing them with 10% and 0.1% writes. Byte caches are instrumented with binary de/encoder of structure.

goos: darwin
goarch: amd64
cpu: Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9750H CPU @ 2.60GHz

name             MB/inuse   time/op (10%) time/op (0.1%)      
sync.Map         192 ± 0%   142ns ± 4%    29.8ns ±10%   // Great for read-heavy workloads.
shardedMap       196 ± 0%   53.3ns ± 3%   28.4ns ±11%   
mutexMap         182 ± 0%   226ns ± 3%    207ns ± 1%    
rwMutexMap       182 ± 0%   233ns ± 2%    67.8ns ± 2%   // RWMutex perf degrades with more writes.
shardedMapOf     181 ± 0%   50.3ns ± 3%   27.3ns ±13%   
ristretto        346 ± 0%   167ns ± 8%    54.1ns ± 4%   // Failed to keep full working set, ~7-15% of the items are evicted.
xsync.Map        380 ± 0%   31.4ns ± 9%   22.0ns ±14%   // Fastest, but a bit hungry for memory.
patrickmn        184 ± 0%   373ns ± 1%    72.6ns ± 5%   
bigcache         340 ± 0%   75.8ns ± 8%   72.9ns ± 3%   // Byte cache.
freecache        333 ± 0%   98.1ns ± 0%   77.8ns ± 2%   // Byte cache.
fastcache       44.9 ± 0%   60.6ns ± 8%   64.1ns ± 5%   // A true champion for memory usage, while having decent performance.

If serialization is not a problem fastcache is the best for its outstanding memory usage.

For CPU-bound applications xsync.Map offers the best performance.

Also, byte cache does not necessarily mean efficient memory usage, as shown by bigcache (ha!) and freecache.

Developer Friendliness

Programs that we write do not always behave like we want them to. Complexity leads to unexpected issues, that are hard to debug.
Unfortunately, caching makes this problem even worse. That's why it is especially important to make caching developer-friendly.

Cache may become poisoned for a variety of reasons, it should be possible to clean it up quickly and safely. For that, consider having a special control to invalidate all cached items. Under heavy load invalidation does not necessarily mean "deletion", if all cache is deleted at once data sources will receive excessive load and may fail.

A more gentle invalidation is "expiration" of all items with background update, while serving stale data.

Cache entry can become outdated and would mislead if somebody is investigating particular data source issues. It is convenient to disable cache for a particular request, so that cache inaccuracy can be ruled out. This may be implemented with a special header and then context instrumentation in middleware. Please be aware that such controls should not be available for public users as they can be used as for DOS attack.

Fine control over logs and metrics of cache operations/state is also important.

And finally, now that Go introduced type parameters (generics), it is possible to leverage type-safe APIs for cache interfaces and offset more burden on compiler.

Maurício Linhares - Feb 16
item.author: vearutop

item.title: 你最喜欢的郎是什么？ | What's Your Fav Lang?
item.description:
穆斯塔菲

发表于你最喜欢的编程语言是什么？ 任何特定原因，如难度/学习曲线、工作前景、工具等。

就我个人而言，它是 Rust，一个原因是我喜欢 Cargo 中集成的所有工具、语言的语法和我想的一样，以及安全 Rust 中的内存/线程安全。

我总是看到自己以某种方式使用它，即使是在我自己的用 C 编写的编程语言中，猜猜所有文档（html/手册页）的组成部分，正是 Rust！

请随时评论您最喜欢的语言以及一些原因。

扎克·克里彭斯坦—— May 2 5月2日



原文:

Mustafif

Posted on May 12

What's your favourite programming language? Any particular reason like difficulty/learning curve, job prospect, tools, etc.

For me personally, it's Rust, one reason is that I love all the tools integrated into Cargo, the language's syntax that just works like how I think, and the memory/thread safety in safe Rust.

I always see myself using it in some way, even in my own programming language that's written in C, guess what going to make all of the documentation (html/man pages), exactly it's Rust!

Please feel free to comment your favourite language and some reasons of why.

Zach Klippenstein - May 2
item.author: mustafif

item.title: JavaScript 数组方法 | JavaScript Array Methods
item.description:
卡洛斯·丹尼尔·奥尔特加·埃尔南德斯

发表于更改原始数组

  • 数组存取方法

    不修改原始数组。 创建新数组。

    • 理解简单 - 博主和程序员 - May 9 5月9日

Mahmoud EL-kariouny - Mahmoud EL-kariouny - May 1 5月1日



原文:

Carlos Daniel Ortega Hernandez

Posted on May 12

Changes original array

  • pop(): Removes the las element of an array and returns it. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.pop()); // 'Element 3'
console.log(array); // ['Element 1', 'Element 2'];
  • shift(): Removes the firts element of an array and returns it. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.shift()); // 'Element 1'
console.log(array); // ['Element 2', 'Element 3'];
  • push(): Adds a new element to the array, at the end of the array, and returns the new length of the array. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.push('Element 4')); // 4
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3', 'Element 4' ]
  • reverse(): Reverses the order of the elements in an array. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.reverse()); // [ 'Element 3', 'Element 2', 'Element 1' ]
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 3', 'Element 2', 'Element 1' ]
  • unshift(): Adds one or more elements to the beginning of an array, and returns the new length of the array. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.unshift('Element 4')); // 4
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 4', 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3' ]
  • sort(): Sorts the elements of an array. 
const array = [2, 8, 1, 4];
console.log(array.sort()); // [ 1, 2, 4, 8 ]
console.log(array); // [ 1, 2, 4, 8 ]
  • splice(): Changes the contents of an array, removing existing elements and/or adds new elements. 
const array = [2, 8, 1, 4, 'a', 'b'];
console.log(array.splice(1,3,'a')); // [ 8, 1, 4 ]
console.log(array); // [ 2, 'a', 'a', 'b' ]

Array Accessor Methods

Does not modify the original array. Creates new array.

  • join(): Joins all the elements of an array into a string and returns the string. 
const array = [2, 8, 1, 4, 'a', 'b', 'c']; console.log(array.join()); // '2,8,1,4,a,b,c'
console.log(array); // [ 2, 8, 1, 4, 'a', 'b', 'c' ]
  • slice(): Returns a part of the array in a new array. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3']; console.log(array.slice(1, 2)); // [ 'Element 2' ]
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3' ]
  • concat(): Joins two or more arrays into one. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
const array2 = [1, 2, 3];
const array3 = [true, false]; console.log(array.concat(array2, array3)); // [ 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3', 1, 2, 3, true, false ]
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3' ]
  • includes(): Returns true if it finds the string, number, etc. or returns false if it finds nothing. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3']; console.log(array.includes(2)); // false
console.log(array.includes('Element 1')); // true
  • toString(): Returns all elements of the array, in a string. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3']; console.log(array.toString()); // 'Element 1,Element 2,Element 3'
  • indexOf(): Returns the first index that matches the search. 
const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 3, 5, 2, 1]; console.log(array.indexOf(1)); // 0
  • lastIndexOf(): Returns the last index that matches the search. 
const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 3, 5, 2, 1]; console.log(array.lastIndexOf(2)); // 7

Understanding Simplicity - Apr 28

Blogger and Programmer - May 9

Mahmoud EL-kariouny - May 1
item.author: carlos0318

item.title: 如何自动重新加载 node.js 服务器 | How to auto reload node.js server
item.description:
源码： https ://github.com/taowen/vite-howto/tree/main/packages/SSR/auto-reload-node-server

代码结构和动机

它是一个 node.js 应用程序，使用 express 监听http://localhost:3000

  • 当我们更改源时，开发服务器应该自动重新加载 node.js 服务器。 在不重新启动进程的情况下进行更改会很好。

    用户体验问题

    解决方案演练

    将 node.js 应用程序构建到包中

    服务器/vite.config.ts

     import { defineConfig } from ' vite ' export default defineConfig ({ build : { ssr : ' ./server-entry.ts ' , outDir : ' ../dist ' }, })

    将server/server-entry.ts到dist/server-entry.js及其引用的所有内容（node.js 标准库除外）。 它是 commonjs 格式，可以在 node.js 环境中执行。 开发服务器

    在开发过程中， http://localhost:3000/我们希望 vite 动态转换 server.ts，这样我们可以在进行更改后跳过编译过程

    import express from ' express ' ; import { createServer as createViteServer } from ' vite ' ; async function main () { const app = express () // auto reload in dev mode const vite = await createViteServer ({ server : { middlewareMode : ' ssr ' , watch : { // During tests we edit the files too fast and sometimes chokidar // misses change events, so enforce polling for consistency usePolling : true , interval : 100 } } }); app . all ( ' /(.*) ' , async ( req , resp ) => { req . url = req . originalUrl ; console . log ( req . method , req . url ); const { default : handle } = await vite . ssrLoadModule ( ' ./server/server.ts ' ); handle ( req , resp , ( e ) => { if ( e ) { vite . ssrFixStacktrace ( e ) console . error ( e . stack ) resp . status ( 500 ). end ( e . stack ); } else { resp . status ( 404 ). end (); } }); }) app . listen ( 3000 , () => { console . log ( ' http://localhost:3000 ' ) }); } main ();

    我们使用await vite.ssrLoadModule('./server/server.ts')来转换代码并运行它。 因为每个请求都会调用 ssrLoadModule，并且配置了server.watch

     const vite = await createViteServer ({ server : { middlewareMode : ' ssr ' , watch : { // During tests we edit the files too fast and sometimes chokidar // misses change events, so enforce polling for consistency usePolling : true , interval : 100 } } });

    如果我们更改服务器代码，我们只需刷新浏览器向开发服务器发送另一个请求即可看到效果。 vite.ssrFixStacktrace(e) will fix the exception stack trace, to report the correct original line number, instead of the line number in transformed file. vite.ssrFixStacktrace(e)将修复异常堆栈跟踪，以报告正确的原始行号，而不是转换文件中的行号。



原文:

Source code: https://github.com/taowen/vite-howto/tree/main/packages/SSR/auto-reload-node-server

Code Structure & Motivation

It is a node.js application, using express to listen at http://localhost:3000

  • server/server-entry.ts is the entry point, which listens the http port
  • server/server.ts is the main logic

DX Problems

dev server should auto reload the node.js server when we have changed the source. nodemon can monitor soure code change and restart node process, but it takes time to restart. It would be nice to make the change without process restart.

UX Problems

vite build server should package every server-entry.ts dependency (except node itself), so we do not need to npm install again when deploy.

Solution Walkthrough

build node.js application to a bundle

server/vite.config.ts

import { defineConfig } from 'vite' export default defineConfig({ build: { ssr: './server-entry.ts', outDir: '../dist' },
})

will bundle the server/server-entry.ts to dist/server-entry.js with everything it referenced (except node.js standard library). It is in commonjs format, ready to be executed in node.js environment. build.ssr is provided by vite to build node.js server.

development server

During development, http://localhost:3000/ we want vite to transform server.ts on the fly, so we can skip compilation process after making changes

import express from 'express';
import { createServer as createViteServer } from 'vite'; async function main() { const app = express() // auto reload in dev mode const vite = await createViteServer({ server: { middlewareMode: 'ssr', watch: { // During tests we edit the files too fast and sometimes chokidar // misses change events, so enforce polling for consistency usePolling: true, interval: 100 } } }); app.all('/(.*)', async (req, resp) => { req.url = req.originalUrl; console.log(req.method, req.url); const { default: handle } = await vite.ssrLoadModule('./server/server.ts'); handle(req, resp, (e) => { if (e) { vite.ssrFixStacktrace(e) console.error(e.stack) resp.status(500).end(e.stack); } else { resp.status(404).end(); } }); }) app.listen(3000, () => { console.log('http://localhost:3000') });
} main();

We use await vite.ssrLoadModule('./server/server.ts') to transform the code and run it. Because the ssrLoadModule invoked per request, and server.watch is configured

 const vite = await createViteServer({ server: { middlewareMode: 'ssr', watch: { // During tests we edit the files too fast and sometimes chokidar // misses change events, so enforce polling for consistency usePolling: true, interval: 100 } }
});

If we changed the server code, we can see the effect just by refreshing browser to send another request to dev server. vite.ssrFixStacktrace(e) will fix the exception stack trace, to report the correct original line number, instead of the line number in transformed file.

item.title: InfoAbout.me | InfoAbout.me
item.description:
这个应用程序的主要目的是让用户使用这个应用程序在各种社交媒体平台上查看自己上传的内容，这就是为什么应用程序被命名为InfoAbout.me的原因。

大多数时候，全球各地的人们必须在一天内访问各种应用程序或社交媒体平台，以检查他们在社交媒体平台上上传的内容，因此牢记这个问题，我制作了这个 Web 应用程序，人们可以在其中查看上传的内容他/她使用这个单一应用程序在 twitter 和 Dev 上发布的内容。

由于时间限制，我只集成了两个应用程序（Twitter + Dev）简单的功能，所以使用InfoAbout.me的用户只能看到他们上传的内容，不能对他们上传的内容进行任何操作。

将来这个应用程序将由我增强，并且像 auth2 等 appwrite 服务也将在其中实现。

目前，此应用程序允许用户使用 InfoAbout.me 登录，如果用户没有帐户，则用户需要创建一个帐户。

成功登录后，用户需要通过 Twitter 和 Dev 授权 InfoAbout.me 应用程序，以便 infoAbout.me 可以成功地从这些应用程序中获取用户数据。

提交类别：

链接到代码

其他资源/信息

[Note:] # (Be sure to link to any open source projects that are using your workflow!) [注意：] # （请务必链接到使用您的工作流程的任何开源项目！）

原文:

InfoAbout.me is an web application which provides convenience to users to check uploded contents by themselves on various social media platforms like Twitter and Dev.

The main motive of this application is to let the users to check their own uploaded contents on various social media platforms using this single application, that's why the application is named as InfoAbout.me.

Most of the time people all around the globe have to visit to various applications or social media platforms in a day to check what they have uploaded on social media platforms,so by keeping this problem in mind I made this web application where one can check uploaded contents by him/her on twitter and Dev using this single applcation.

Because of time limitation I integrated only two applications (Twitter + Dev) simple features in it, so using InfoAbout.me user can only see their uploaded contents, can't perform any operation with their uploaded contents.

In future this application will be enhanced by me and appwrite services like auth2 etc will also be implemented in it.

For now this application lets a user to login with InfoAbout.me, if user don't have an account then user will need to create an account.

After successfully Login user will need to authorize InfoAbout.me application with Twitter and Dev so that infoAbout.me can fetch User Data from these applications successfully.

Web2 Wizards

item.title: 如何在你的 nodeJS 应用程序中修复 openai 错误 | How to Fix error with openai in your nodeJS app
item.description:
凯欣德·阿德莱克

发表于当我遇到这个错误时，我正在尝试 openAI 的 api。

这很烦人。 为了解决这个问题，你将"type": "module"添加到你的 package.json 文件中。

如果您没有 package.json 文件，请使用 `npm init -y" 创建一个。

有了这个，你应该有这个：

这应该解决它。

但是......可能会出现其他问题。 由于您需要 .env 文件中的 API 密钥才能访问 api，因此您需要导入 dotenv 包。

在使用 npm 或 yarn 安装后，我使用了 require 关键字来导入它：

出于某种原因，这似乎引发了另一个错误：

这非常令人沮丧，堆栈溢出似乎没有直接的答案。

为了解决这个问题，将 require 更改为正常的 import 语句，

在这种情况下， import dotenv from 'dotenv/config'

最终代码如下所示：



原文:

Kehinde Adeleke

Posted on May 12

I was trying out openAI's api when I came across this error.

It was quite annoying. To solve it, you add the "type": "module" to your package.json file.

If you don't have a package.json file, use `npm init -y" to create one.

With that, you should have this:

which should solve it.

but...something else might come up. Since you need your API key in a .env file to access the api, you would need to import the dotenv package.

after installing with npm or yarn, I used the require keyword to import it:

`require('dotenv').config();

For some reason, that seems to throw another error:

It was very frustrating and stack overflow didn't seem to have a direct answer.

To solve it, change require to a normal import statement,

in this case it would be, import dotenv from 'dotenv/config'

the final code looks like this:

item.title: 只有垂直边距折叠 | Only vertical margins collapse
item.description:
当向 CSS 规范添加 margin-collapse 时，语言设计者做出了一个奇怪的选择：水平边距不应该折叠。

在早期，CSS 不打算用于布局。 编写规范的人想象的是标题和段落，而不是列和侧边栏。

所以这是我们的第一条规则：只有垂直边距折叠。

这是一个例子。 如果您使用的是桌面浏览器，请打开开发人员工具并自己检查边距：



原文:

1 Introduction of Margin Collapse in CSS 2 Only vertical margins collapse

When margin-collapse was added to the CSS specification, the language designers made a curious choice: horizontal margins shouldn't collapse.

In the early days, CSS wasn't intended to be used for layouts. The people writing the spec were imagining headings and paragraphs, not columns and sidebars.

So that's our first rule: only vertical margins collapse.

Here's an example. If you're using a desktop browser, pop open the developer tools and inspect the margins for yourself:

item.title: 在 MacOS 上加密文件夹 | Encrypting Folders on MacOS
item.description:
迈克尔·沃尔

发表于创建一个新文件夹，在我的示例中，该文件夹名为“secretstuff”。

  • 将您想要保护的任何文件、文件夹或内容复制到 secretstuff 文件夹中。

  • 启动磁盘工具，从文件菜单中选择新图像，从文件夹中选择图像。

  • 我选择了从上面的步骤 1 创建的文件夹。 将加密设置为 256 位 AES，并将图像格式设置为压缩。 选择保存。 出现提示时，提供一个长而强的密码，将密码存储在与文件夹和创建后的最终 dmg 文件分开的安全位置。

    1. 将创建一个新的secretstuff.dmg 文件，不要忘记删除源secretstuff 文件夹，但保留secretstuff.dmg 文件。

    2. 如果您想访问 secretstuff.dmg 文件的内容，您可以使用相同的磁盘工具工具，从文件菜单中选择打开磁盘映像，当提示时输入上面在步骤 4 中创建的密码。

    切尔西德弗罗 - 塞缪尔·欣奇利夫🚀 - May 5 5月5日

    Pachi 🪐 (she/her/ela) - Pachi 🪐 (她/她/ela) - May 3 5月3日



    原文:

    Michael Wahl

    Posted on May 11

    1. Create a new folder, for my example the folder is named "secretstuff".

    2. Copy whatever files, folders or content you want to protect into the secretstuff folder.

    3. Launch the Disk Utility, from the file menu, select new image, image from folder.

    4. I selected the folder I created from step 1 above. Set the encryption to 256-bit AES, and the image format to compressed. Select Save. When prompted, provide a long and strong password, store the password in a safe place separate from the folder and the final dmg file once created.

    1. A new secretstuff.dmg file will be created, dont forgot to delete the source secretstuff folder, but leave the secretstuff.dmg file.

    2. If you want to access the contents of the secretstuff.dmg file, you may do so by using the same Disk Utility tool, from the file menu, select open disk image, when prompted enter the password created above during step 4.

    Chelsea Devereaux - May 5

    Samuel Hinchliffe 🚀 - May 5

    Pachi 🪐 (she/her/ela) - May 3

    Pet and Vet is a micro social network with the purpose of connecting veterinarians with pet owners, as well as making your pet known to the world and learn about events in your country.

    The main functions of this micro social network are:

    • Register/Login with email
    • Registration as a Veterinarian or Pet Owner
    • Veterinarians can enter 1 or more contact addresses that can be located on a map
    • Pet owners can have a catalog of their pets and decide whether to display them publicly or privately
    • Registry of tips so that everyone knows information of interest
    • Share your pet via QR
    • Connect with a veterinarian via email or chat in real time

    Pet and Vet is built on:

    • AppWrite: as the backend that provides all the services
    • Gitpod: for remote development platform
    • Svelte: as a javascript framework
    • Agnostic UI: for the design of the components
    • Express JS: server-side sdk specific functionality

    The inspiration for the idea was to have a project that will use many of the features provided by AppWrite and at the same time be an example of how to connect people who are interested in pets.
